title: Verify station resolves pytest venv from wrong sample (non-hermetic gate provenance)
description: verify.ex:45 calls Workspace.venv_opts() with no arg, defaulting to samples/tasks_service instead of the slice's own workspace requirements.lock. Fix: thread the actual workspace/sample path into venv_opts (or always build_or_cache_venv from the workspace's own requirements.lock). Surfaced by the M2-exit adversarial review; relates to M4 hermetic gate verification.

title: SerialDriver skip/ordering ignores integration_order work edges
description:
SerialDriver.work_edges/2 filters work_dependencies to execution_hard ONLY (serial_driver.ex). So integration_order edges (a first-class work-edge kind per SliceDependency.@work_kinds) participate in NEITHER topo ordering (do_topo) NOR the M3 skip cascade (blocking_predecessors).

Implications:
- Ordering gap (PRE-EXISTING, not from M3): two slices whose only relation is A--(integration_order)-->B are unordered by do_topo, so declaration order wins — B can run/commit before A.
- Skip gap (NEW with M3 skip-and-continue): if A parks, a B that only integration_order-depends on A is NOT skipped — it still runs.

Deliberate-for-now rationale: skip cascades along execution_hard because that is the 'B's code depends on A' edge; integration_order is a softer 'integrate B after A' constraint that does not imply B needs A's code, so building B independently after A parks is arguably correct. The ordering gap is pre-existing.

Decision needed: either (a) feed integration_order into do_topo (fix ordering) while keeping it OUT of the skip closure, with a test pinning 'integration_order predecessor parking does NOT skip its dependent'; or (b) include it in both. No test currently pins either behavior; M3 plans are small/hand-authored (low practical risk). Surfaced by the M3 Commit-1 adversarial review.
close_reason: Fixed via decision (a): integration_order now participates in ordering but NOT the skip cascade. SerialDriver.work_edges/2 (execution_hard-only, used for both do_topo AND blocking_predecessors) is split into ordering_edges/2 (execution_hard + integration_order) feeding do_topo, and blocking_edges/2 (execution_hard-only) threaded into the skip cascade. Rationale: both kinds mean 'A runs before B' (topo constraint), but only execution_hard ('B's code depends on A') justifies skipping B when A parks; integration_order is a softer 'integrate B after A' that lets B build independently after A parks. blocking_edges ⊆ ordering_edges, so the one-hop==transitive-closure skip proof still holds (every execution_hard predecessor is still ordered before its dependent). Closes BOTH gaps: the pre-existing ordering gap (integration_order-only slices were left to declaration order) and confirms the skip gap stays correct-by-design. Regression tests (test/conveyor/planning_serial_driver_test.exs, pure map-fakes): 'integration_order edges order the run...' (declared Y-before-X, X->Y integration_order -> runs X first) and 'an integration_order predecessor parking does NOT skip its dependent' (X parks -> Y still runs+passes, run :partial). Full suite 1330 passed; credo/dialyzer/format clean.

title: Factory Legibility & Operator-Trust Program — make the unattended serial loop legible, actionable & spend-safe
description:
# Factory Legibility & Operator-Trust Program

## Why this exists
Conveyor's promise (STRATEGY, README) is: hand off a substantial plan, walk away, and wake up to it built — with the human reviewing "a morning digest and a small needs-judgment queue." That human interface is named in the strategy but largely unbuilt. ROADMAP v2 places the morning digest, parked-queue triage, and BudgetReservations/EmergencyStop enforcement in **M6 (long-horizon autonomy)**, and the recent cockpit work (React/Inertia/React Flow living-graph on /runs, PRs #32–#35) is the host surface for it.

## Thesis
The current era's biggest *non-loop* gap is making the unattended serial loop **legible, actionable, and spend-safe for the human**. This program operationalizes that. Every item is **accretive** — it rides infrastructure that already exists (the event-sourced run ledger from PR #21, the gate's existing GateResult/TrustScore/TrustEvidence output, the agent_usage@1 / budget_envelope@1 / budget_reservation@1 schemas, and the cockpit stack) — and **respects the ratified strategy**: serial-first, **no new gate _concepts_** (we visualize and wire existing gate output, never invent stages), no cross-slice parallelism.

## How it serves the north star & key metrics
- Makes overnight throughput & autonomous-completion-rate legible (digest, time-travel, run-health).
- Makes the gate's trustworthiness auditable (why-panel, calibration dashboard) — the precondition for trusting unattended merge.
- Makes the gate-escape rate measurable for the first time (escape tracking).
- Protects unattended spend (budget cockpit + enforcement) — "cost is a scheduling input," ROADMAP §6.
- De-risks the §4 exit bar (multi-target eval corpus; preview/dry-run against the R4 decomposition risk).

## Scope (5 clusters / 15 ideas)
1. Human Window — morning digest, trust-evidence why-panel, needs-a-human inbox, time-travel replay, live run-health.
2. Spend Safety & Pre-flight — cost/budget cockpit + enforcement, plan preview/dry-run.
3. Gate Measurement & Honesty — calibration/exit-bar dashboard, gate-escape tracking + feedback.
4. Eval & De-risk — multi-target greenfield corpus (>=3), eval-dataset flywheel.
5. Persona & Ops Ergonomics — contract-authoring assistant, agent-ergonomic CLI audit, interrogator-on-ambiguity, structured incident timeline.

## Non-goals
- No cross-slice / fleet parallelism (Track B; gated behind the §4 exit bar).
- No new gate *concepts* or stages (ROADMAP Principle 1 freeze) — only projection and wiring of existing gate output.
- Not on the M4/M5/M6 *critical loop* path; this is additive observability/ergonomics that *helps* build that path. Ranked accordingly (mostly P2–P3).

## Provenance
Generated via the idea-wizard flow (30 ideas → winnowed to 15) against ROADMAP v2, STRATEGY, CONCEPTS, M4-PROGRESS, the 27 ADRs, and the active bead tree (2026-06-28). Overlaps are merged onto existing beads where they exist (dr1m.6/.1/.2, 90v0, dr1m.1, eval-093).

title: Human Window — cockpit observability surfaces for the unattended run
description: Cluster feature. The human's window into a run: what happened (digest), why the gate decided as it did (why-panel), what needs me (inbox), how it unfolded (time-travel), and is it healthy right now (run-health). All host on the existing React/Inertia/React Flow cockpit (PRs #32–#35) and the event-sourced run ledger (PR #21). No new gate concepts — projection only.

title: Morning Digest — the legible artifact the persona wakes up to
description:
## What
The single legible artifact the persona wakes up to: a per-night + per-run summary of slices merged, slices parked (each with its park reason + a deep link to the needs-a-human inbox), what needs my judgment, tokens/$ burned vs budget, drift/health signals, and the run timeline.

## Why it serves the goal
STRATEGY and README name "a morning digest" as THE human interface; ROADMAP M6 lists "wire the morning digest" but it is unbuilt. This is the direct payoff of the overnight promise and makes overnight-throughput / autonomous-completion-rate legible at a glance.

## Design (rides existing infra)
A read-model folding the event-sourced run ledger (PR #21) + GateResult + cost aggregation into a DigestSummary projection. A cockpit route on the existing React/Inertia stack; a `mix conveyor.digest [--since] [--format md|html|json]` CLI sibling for headless/cron; Markdown/HTML export so it lands in email/Slack/a file. Every line deep-links into the living-graph cockpit and (for parked items) the inbox. No new metrics the gate does not already emit — projection only.

## Subtasks
Split into: digest read-model/projection; cockpit route; CLI + export; tests (see child beads).

## Acceptance criteria
- Given a completed multi-slice run, the digest lists every slice with its terminal disposition (merged/parked/failed), aggregate cost vs budget, and a count of needs-judgment items.
- `mix conveyor.digest --format md` emits deterministic Markdown for a recorded run (golden-file stable).
- Each parked entry links to its inbox item and its trust-evidence why-panel.

title: Digest read-model: DigestSummary projection over the run ledger
description: Fold the event-sourced run ledger (PR #21) + GateResult + cost aggregation into a DigestSummary read-model: per-run + per-night, listing each slice's terminal disposition (merged/parked/failed), aggregate cost vs budget, and needs-judgment counts. Pure read path; no writes. Acceptance: seeded ledger fixture -> DigestSummary with correct disposition counts and cost totals.
close_reason: Added Conveyor.Digest.Summary.build/3: pure DigestSummary read-model folding RunReadModel stories (ledger + GateResult) + Cost.ReadModel aggregation into per-run and per-night terminal-disposition tallies (merged/parked/skipped/failed/in_flight), needs-judgment counts (parked slices), and aggregate cost vs budget_envelope. Slice outcome strings normalized to dispositions. 5 seeded-fixture unit tests (stories built via the pure RunReadModel.project fold). No writes.

title: Cockpit /digest route (React/Inertia)
description: A cockpit route rendering the DigestSummary on the existing React/Inertia/React Flow stack. Each line deep-links into the living-graph cockpit; parked entries link to the inbox (I3) and the why-panel (I2). Acceptance: the route renders a recorded run's digest with working deep links.

title: mix conveyor.digest CLI + Markdown/HTML/JSON export
description: A headless `mix conveyor.digest [--since <ref>] [--format md|html|json]` sibling of the cockpit route, so the digest can be emailed/Slacked/filed by cron. Acceptance: `--format md` emits deterministic, golden-file-stable Markdown for a recorded run; `--format json` matches the DigestSummary schema.
close_reason: Added mix conveyor.digest [--since ISO8601] [--format md|html|json]: folds the run ledger (run.started enumeration + RunReadModel.summarize per run) into a Conveyor.Digest.Summary and renders it via the new pure Conveyor.Digest.Render (to_markdown/to_json/to_html). Markdown is deterministic (runs sorted by id) → golden-file-stable; JSON is the DigestSummary structure. 6 tests: renderer (md determinism/sort, json round-trip, html) + DB task test (recorded run → correct dispositions in md+json).

title: Tests & e2e: digest projection + golden-Markdown render
description: Unit: DigestSummary projection over a seeded ledger fixture (assert disposition counts, cost totals, needs-judgment count). E2E: run the hermetic `mix conveyor.demo`, generate the digest, assert the golden Markdown and that every deep link resolves. Detailed logging: log each digest section rendered and each link target so a failing run is diagnosable from the log alone.

title: Trust-Evidence "Why" panel — make every gate verdict auditable
description:
## What
For any slice verdict, a cockpit panel showing the gate's full reasoning: which stages ran (currently 7, target 14) and their pass/fail, each trust signal's value + weight + contribution (calibration, integrity, baseline, replay, corpus), the calibrated TrustScore, the band (accept/abstain/reject), and the abstain/park reason.

## Why it serves the goal
The core bet is "the verification gate is the human's stand-in"; you cannot delegate to a judge whose reasoning you cannot audit. It is also the primary debugging surface for calibrating the gate (M4) and for the eventual factory-builds-factory.

## Design
A read-model over GateResult / TrustScore / TrustEvidence + the per-stage outputs already present in default_gate_context. Render in the cockpit dossier (the U8 dossier from PR #35 is the natural host). **No new gate concept** — pure projection (ROADMAP Principle 1 freeze respected).

## Depends on
dr1m.1 (ADR-23 ternary verdict + TrustScore) — the band/score this panel visualizes.

## Acceptance criteria
- For an accepted reference slice, the panel shows all run stages green, each signal's weight, and the final score (e.g. the reference auto-accepts at the OD19 cold-start 0.9118).
- For SLICE-007 (parks on weak acceptance), the panel shows calibration not-valid -> abstain, with the park reason.

## Tests
Unit: assemble the panel view-model from a recorded GateResult fixture for both an accept and a park; assert every signal + weight + band is present. E2E: render in the cockpit, assert the abstain reason is shown; log the assembled view-model.

title: Gate-evidence read-model: stages + signals + weights + score + band
description: A read-model over GateResult / TrustScore / TrustEvidence + per-stage outputs in default_gate_context: every stage's pass/fail, each trust signal's value + weight + contribution, the calibrated score, the band, and the abstain/park reason. Projection only — no new gate concept. Acceptance: a recorded GateResult fixture yields a complete evidence view-model for both an accept and a park.

title: Cockpit why-panel UI in the dossier (U8 host)
description: Render the gate-evidence view-model in the cockpit dossier (the U8 dossier from PR #35 is the host). Show stages, per-signal weights/contributions, the final score, the band, and the abstain reason. Acceptance: the panel shows all-green stages + final score for the reference, and calibration-not-valid -> abstain with reason for SLICE-007.

title: Tests & e2e: why-panel view-model for accept + park
description: Unit: assemble the view-model from recorded GateResult fixtures for both an accept (reference, ~0.9118 cold-start) and a park (SLICE-007); assert every signal + weight + band is present. E2E: render in the cockpit and assert the abstain reason is shown. Logging: log the full assembled view-model on assertion failure.

title: Needs-a-human inbox — actionable parked-queue triage
description:
## What
The bounded daily-attention surface. Each parked slice as an actionable inbox item: a typed park reason, a trust-evidence summary (from the why-panel), the human_review_rubric, the diff, and one-click dispositions (approve-merge / reject / rework / amend-plan), ordered least-trusted-first.

## Why it serves the goal
The ROADMAP exit bar caps the human at "<= N judgment-items/day"; this is where that budget is spent. It turns calibrated abstention from a halt into operator value — ADR-23's payoff realized, and the other half of the named human interface.

## Design / MERGE (do not duplicate)
Builds ON existing beads: dr1m.6 (parked-queue read) + dr1m.6.2 (least-trusted-first ordering + nondeterministic dedup) + ParkedQueueLive; upgrades read -> act, wiring dispositions to the existing closers (rework/amend). The park-reason taxonomy is its own subtask (the M4-PROGRESS "optional future polish": weak_acceptance_tests vs no_behavior_change vs missing_signal). Reuses human_review_rubric@1.

## Depends on
I2 (why-panel evidence). Related: dr1m.6, dr1m.6.2, 90v0 (skip-and-continue feeds the parked set).

## Subtasks
park-reason taxonomy; inbox cockpit surface; dispositions wired to closers; tests (see child beads).

title: Park-reason taxonomy: typed enum + producer mapping
description: Promote the M4-PROGRESS "optional future polish" to a typed park-reason enum: weak_acceptance_tests | no_behavior_change | missing_signal (extensible). Map the gate's existing abstain/park outputs onto it at the producer. Acceptance: each park path yields a typed reason; an unknown path maps to a safe default and is logged.

title: Inbox cockpit surface: rubric + evidence + diff, least-trusted-first
description: The actionable inbox built ON dr1m.6 (parked-queue read) + dr1m.6.2 (least-trusted-first ordering + dedup) — do not duplicate them. Each item shows its typed park reason (I3a), a trust-evidence summary (links to I2), the human_review_rubric@1, and the diff. Acceptance: parked slices render least-trusted-first with reason + rubric + diff + evidence link.

title: Inbox dispositions wired to closers (approve/reject/rework/amend)
description: One-click dispositions wired to the existing closers: approve-merge, reject, rework (AttemptLoop), amend-plan (ADR-26 AmendmentRouter). Each disposition is recorded to the ledger for audit. Acceptance: each disposition drives the correct closer and is durably recorded; the inbox item leaves the queue.

title: Tests & e2e: park-reason mapping + disposition round-trip
description: Unit: park-reason mapping for each park path (incl. unknown -> default). E2E: a parked slice -> approve/rework/amend round-trip drives the right closer and updates the ledger; ordering is least-trusted-first. Logging: log each disposition, the closer invoked, and the resulting slice state.

title: Time-travel run replay — a timeline scrubber in the cockpit
description:
## What
Scrub any run's timeline — station transitions, gate-stage firing, rework attempts, park/accept events — replayed from the event-sourced ledger, with the living-graph re-rendering at each point in time.

## Why it serves the goal
README names "time-travel debugging" as a core bet; the ledger records everything but there is no UI. It is the temporal dimension of the digest (what happened) and the why-panel (why): this shows *how it unfolded*.

## Design
Project the ordered ledger events into timeline frames; a cockpit timeline scrubber drives the existing React Flow graph state. Read-only.

## Acceptance criteria
- Scrubbing to event N renders the graph state as of N (nodes/edges/status match an independent fold at N).
- Rework attempts and park/accept transitions appear as distinct timeline markers.

## Tests
Unit: fold a ledger fixture to frame N, assert graph state equals an independent reconstruction. E2E: scrub a recorded run end-to-end, assert the final frame == the digest's disposition; log the frame count.

title: Live run-health / drift signal in the cockpit
description:
## What
A live "is this run healthy?" signal: rework-count climbing, no-progress, repeat-offender, time-on-slice vs budget — so the human sees a run heading for death before it dies.

## Why it serves the goal
"The real dragon is autonomous *duration*" (ROADMAP Principle 2). M6 plans the *detectors* (drift/no-progress, Convergence Sentinel, Repeat-Offender); this surfaces their output legibly in the same era. Pairs with the watchdog (p639).

## Design / dependency note
Rides the M6 drift/convergence/repeat-offender detectors. Those detectors are roadmap-narrative, not yet a detailed bead — this bead is the *surfacing* layer and should be co-developed with (or sequenced after) them. Until they exist it surfaces the cheap signals available now (rework count, time-on-slice vs budget).

## Acceptance criteria
- The cockpit shows a per-run health state derived from rework-count + time-on-slice; it transitions to a warning state when a configurable threshold trips.

## Tests
Unit: a health classifier over a seeded run-state fixture (healthy -> warning). E2E: drive a thrashing run, assert the warning fires; log the triggering signal.

## Non-goals
Does not *build* the detectors (M6); it surfaces them.

title: Spend Safety & Pre-flight — protect unattended token spend & catch bad plans before the night
description: Cluster feature. Two pre-conditions for trusting an overnight run: (1) a visible AND enforced budget so it cannot blow the wallet (cost/budget cockpit + BudgetReservations/EmergencyStop), and (2) a plan preview / dry-run so a bad decomposition is caught at $0 instead of after a wasted night. Both ride existing schemas (agent_usage@1, budget_*@1) and the compiler's existing graph + audits.

title: Cost/Budget cockpit + enforcement — the economic governor, made real
description:
## What
Live token/$ accounting per run/slice/agent/attempt against a not-to-exceed budget, shown in the cockpit AND enforced — activate the dormant BudgetReservations + EmergencyStop so an overnight run cannot blow the budget.

## Why it serves the goal
The persona hands the keys to an unattended token-spender overnight; a visible, enforced budget is the difference between "I trust it while I sleep" and "I daren't." STRATEGY/ROADMAP §6: cost is a scheduling input, not an afterthought. ROADMAP M6: "activate BudgetReservations + enforce EmergencyStop."

## Design (rides existing schemas)
agent_usage@1 (per-call usage), budget_envelope@1 (the NTE budget), budget_reservation@1, and emergency_stop_state@1 already exist as schemas / dormant resources. Aggregate usage into a cost read-model; reserve-before-spend in the driver; trip EmergencyStop on breach. The cockpit panel shows burn vs envelope. No new cost *concepts* — activate what exists.

## Subtasks
cost read-model; cockpit budget panel; reserve-before-spend enforcement; EmergencyStop trip-on-breach; tests (see child beads).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: STEER (2026-07-02, idea-wizard pass): sequence CLI-first per DOGFOOD-READINESS §6 step 5 — the enforcement subset (a3hf.2.1.3 reserve-before-spend, a3hf.2.1.4 EmergencyStop trip-on-breach, plus a plain --budget-usd cap on mix conveyor.run) is what unattended overnight runs need; the cockpit burn panel (a3hf.2.1.2) is a later rendering. Enforcement beads should not depend on any cockpit work. Rationale: spend safety is a prerequisite for the M6 medium-plan overnight run; dashboards are not.

title: Cost read-model: aggregate agent_usage@1 per run/slice/agent/attempt
description: Aggregate agent_usage@1 records into a cost read-model keyed by run/slice/agent/attempt, with running totals against budget_envelope@1. Acceptance: a seeded usage fixture yields correct per-dimension totals and a remaining-budget figure.
close_reason: Added Conveyor.Cost.ReadModel.aggregate/2: pure, deterministic projection of conveyor.agent_usage@1 records into per-dimension totals (by_run/by_slice/by_agent/by_attempt) + overall totals (tokens/cost_usd/latency_ms/count) with remaining budget vs conveyor.budget_envelope@1 (token+cost, over_budget? flag). 6 fixture-seeded unit tests. Cockpit (a3hf.2.1) consumes it.

title: Cockpit budget panel: burn vs envelope
description: A cockpit panel showing live burn vs the budget_envelope@1 not-to-exceed figure, per run and per slice. Acceptance: the panel reflects the cost read-model and visibly flags approaching/over budget.

title: Reserve-before-spend enforcement via budget_reservation@1
description: Enforce a reservation in the driver before an agent call spends: check the envelope, reserve, proceed or refuse. Activates the dormant budget_reservation@1. Acceptance: a call that would exceed the envelope is refused before spending; a within-budget call reserves then spends.

title: EmergencyStop trip-on-breach (emergency_stop_state@1)
description: On envelope breach (or operator command), trip EmergencyStop (emergency_stop_state@1) to halt the run cleanly and durably, surfaced in the cockpit + digest. Activates the dormant EmergencyStop. Acceptance: a breach trips EmergencyStop, the run halts at a safe point, and the state is recorded + shown.

title: Tests & e2e: reservation enforcement + budget-breach halt
description: Unit: reservation refuses over-budget, allows within-budget; cost aggregation totals. E2E: drive a run against a tight envelope, assert it reserves, then trips EmergencyStop on breach and halts safely. Logging: log every reservation decision, the running total, and the trip event.

title: Plan preview / dry-run — sanity-check before the night burns
description:
## What
Before committing tokens to an overnight run: a preview of the computed work-graph (DAG), each slice's contract + acceptance criteria, plan-lint warnings (cycles, orphan slices, untestable/empty acceptance, unlocked interfaces), and an estimated token/$ cost — behind an explicit human approve-to-run gate.

## Why it serves the goal
R4: decomposition quality is the load-bearing artifact for the whole dream. This catches a bad plan at $0 instead of after a wasted night — a pragmatic, accretive wedge into the M5 decomposition-quality problem.

## Design (rides existing compiler output)
The compiler already computes the graph (WorkGraphBuilder) and has audits (Planning.*) — surface them. `mix conveyor.preview <plan>` + a cockpit planned-DAG view reusing the React Flow renderer; a cost estimate from historical agent_usage; an approve-to-run gate before the run commits tokens.

## Subtasks
plan-lint engine (surface compiler audits); cost estimator; preview CLI + cockpit DAG + approve gate; tests (see child beads).

title: Plan-lint engine: surface compiler audits as warnings
description: Surface the compiler's existing audits (Planning.*) as plan-lint warnings: dependency cycles, orphan slices, untestable/empty acceptance criteria, unlocked public interfaces. Acceptance: a plan with each defect class produces the corresponding warning; a clean plan produces none.
close_reason: Added Conveyor.Planning.PlanWarnings.warn/2: surfaces the compiler's existing audits as non-blocking (severity :warning) plan-preview advisories — dependency_cycle + orphan_slice (from SliceDependency.analyze work_graph_cycle/unreachable_active_slice), untestable_acceptance (from StructuralAudit unmeasurable_acceptance/missing_oracle_path/missing_requirement_acceptance), and unlocked_interface (interface missing version/schema_ref). 6 tests: one per defect class + clean-plan-produces-none + advisory-severity. Distinct from the blocking PlanLint gate.

title: Cost estimator from historical agent_usage
description: Estimate a plan's token/$ cost from historical agent_usage@1 (per-slice-archetype averages), surfaced in the preview. Honest about uncertainty (range, not point). Acceptance: a plan yields an estimate with a confidence range derived from historical usage; empty history yields an explicit "no basis" rather than a fake number.
close_reason: Added Conveyor.Cost.Estimator.estimate/2: estimates a plan's token/cost from historical conveyor.agent_usage@1 grouped by slice archetype, as a low/expected/high range (observed min/mean/max per archetype summed over plan slices) — never a false-precision point. Empty history or no matching archetype -> {:no_basis, reason} (no fake number); partial coverage estimates covered slices and reports the uncovered count. 5 fixture unit tests.

title: mix conveyor.preview + cockpit planned-DAG + approve-to-run gate
description: `mix conveyor.preview <plan>` and a cockpit view that render the computed work-graph (reuse the React Flow renderer on the *planned* DAG), each slice's contract + acceptance, the lint warnings (I5a), and the cost estimate (I5b) — behind an explicit approve-to-run gate so no tokens are spent until the human confirms. Acceptance: preview renders graph + contracts + lints + estimate and blocks the run until approved.
close_reason: Added mix conveyor.preview PLAN_ID [--format text|json] + Conveyor.Planning.Preview.assemble/2: renders the computed work graph (WorkGraphBuilder), each slice's contract summary, the non-blocking plan-lint warnings (PlanWarnings — adapted work_dependencies->dependencies), and a cost estimate (Cost.Estimator; honest no-basis with no history, range when usage injected), behind the approve-to-run gate (output states 'blocked until approved: mix conveyor.plan.approve PLAN_ID'). Read-only, never runs. 4 tests: assemble (graph/contracts/lints/no-basis estimate/unapproved), approval status, injected-usage range, CLI JSON. NOTE: cockpit React-Flow planned-DAG view is UI, tracked with the cockpit-route beads; the CLI meets the stated acceptance.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: OVERLAP COORDINATION (plan-space review 2026-07-02): prose-to-plan-aaun.1 (mix conveyor.plan.approve, lint-gated bulk approve) now owns the CLI approve-to-run gate. This bead narrows to: conveyor.preview read-only dry-run + cockpit planned-DAG + wiring the cost estimate (a3hf.2.2.2) into both surfaces. Coordinate so there is exactly one approval path.

title: Tests & e2e: lint detection + preview render + approve gate
description: Unit: lint detects each defect class; estimator returns a range and an honest "no basis" on empty history. E2E: `mix conveyor.preview` on a seeded plan renders DAG + estimate and the approve gate blocks until confirmed. Logging: log each lint finding, the estimate basis, and the approve/deny decision.

title: Gate Measurement & Honesty — make the gate's own trustworthiness measurable
description: Cluster feature. The §4 exit bar requires first-pass-gate-success, dispute-rate, parked-rate, and gate-escape-rate to be *measured on real runs* — today they are provisional and unmeasured. This cluster builds the aggregate calibration/exit-bar dashboard and the gate-escape feedback loop (STRATEGY key metric "keeps autonomy honest"). Projection + a feedback producer; no new gate concepts.

title: Gate calibration & exit-bar dashboard
description:
## What
A trend view of the metrics the §4 exit bar requires measured-but-isn't: first-pass-gate-success, abstain rate, parked rate, material-dispute rate — over time, per plan-size band.

## Why it serves the goal
The bar's thresholds (>=70% first-pass, <20% dispute, <15% parked) are explicitly provisional and "must be calibrated on real runs." You cannot calibrate what you cannot see. Distinct from the per-slice why-panel by being the aggregate trend — it tells you if calibration is drifting.

## Design
Project GateResult history into a time-series read-model; cockpit dashboard. Reuses the I2 evidence read-model shape (related).

## Acceptance criteria
- Given a set of recorded gate results, the dashboard reports first-pass-success, abstain%, and parked% and bins them by plan size.

## Tests
Unit: aggregate over a fixture of mixed verdicts, assert the rates. E2E: render the dashboard for the eval corpus; log the computed rates.

## Related
eval-093 (honesty/calibration).

title: Gate-escape tracking + calibration feedback
description:
## What
When merged work is later reverted or found defective, record it as a gate-escape and feed it back to calibration. Closes the loop on STRATEGY's gate-escape-rate key metric (currently unmeasured).

## Why it serves the goal
The metric that "keeps autonomy honest" and guards against "autonomous but wrong." Without it, every other gate number is one-sided.

## Design / honesty note
Needs a defect signal (revert / later-failing-test / human flag) + a feedback path to calibration. Near-term data is thin (merged work accrues from M6+), so this bead builds the *scaffold and producer*; the calibration feedback may remain advisory until enough escapes accrue. Depends on I8 (which surfaces the escape rate).

## Acceptance criteria
- A revert/flag on a previously-merged slice produces a GateEscape record linked to the original GateResult, and the I8 dashboard's escape-rate reflects it.

## Tests
Unit: synthesize a merged-then-reverted slice, assert a GateEscape record + a dashboard delta; log the escape.

title: Eval & De-risk — broaden the corpus and harvest runs into learning data
description: Cluster feature. De-risk the exit bar's reliance on a single greenfield target (ROADMAP §4: ">=3 distinct greenfield targets ... materially de-risk over-fitting to Beads-Insight") and close STRATEGY's Observability & Learning loop by auto-harvesting recorded runs into the eval corpus the factory learns from.

title: Multi-target greenfield eval corpus (>=3 distinct targets)
description:
## What
Add >=2 more greenfield pure-logic targets beside Beads-Insight so the exit-bar metrics are not measured on a single task.

## Why it serves the goal
ROADMAP §4, verbatim: "a small corpus of >=3 distinct greenfield targets before the medium-plan run would materially de-risk over-fitting to Beads-Insight." Today every bar metric rides one of the easiest possible tasks.

## Design
Two new sample targets under samples/ (deterministic, golden-file oracle, committed reference patches), mirroring the beads_insight harness so they plug into the existing eval program. Distinct from eval-011 (mutant generator) and l290 (lift sweep) — this is corpus *breadth*.

## Acceptance criteria
- >=2 new greenfield targets run end-to-end through the eval harness with a golden oracle; the exit-bar metrics can be computed per-target and aggregated.

## Tests
E2E: each new target runs through mix conveyor.run + the gate to a known outcome; CI-gated; log per-target pass-rate.

title: Eval-dataset flywheel — auto-harvest runs into the learning corpus
description:
## What
Recorded runs automatically become the eval corpus — every accepted/parked/escaped slice is a labeled example calibration and future agents learn from.

## Why it serves the goal
STRATEGY's Observability & Learning track closes with "the eval dataset the factory learns from"; the recordings exist but are not harvested into a growing corpus. Longer-horizon — it turns operation into improvement data.

## Design
A harvester projecting ledger + gate outcomes into eval_* records (eval_metric@1 / eval_scorecard@1 exist); dedup against the committed corpus. Depends on I10 (a multi-target corpus to grow).

## Acceptance criteria
- A completed run yields labeled eval records (outcome + evidence refs) appended to the corpus without duplicating existing entries.

## Tests
Unit: harvest a recorded-run fixture -> assert N labeled records, idempotent on re-harvest; log the harvested count.

title: Persona & Ops Ergonomics — lower the cost of driving and operating the factory
description: Cluster feature. Make the factory pleasant and safe to drive for both the human persona and (eventually, at self-hosting M8) an AI operator: help author the acceptance contracts the persona must write, harden the mix conveyor.* CLI for agents, ask clarifying questions before a vague plan burns the night, and capture failures as first-class incidents.

title: Contract-authoring assistant — lower the persona's up-front cost
description:
## What
Help the persona write the machine-checkable acceptance contracts — interface lock, required tests, definition of done — with templated scaffolds, lint, and "is this acceptance criterion actually falsifiable?" checks.

## Why it serves the goal
"Contracts cap quality" is a core bet, and authoring them is the persona's main up-front cost. Lowering that cost widens who can drive the factory and improves contract quality (which the gate depends on). Pairs with I5 (preview lints contracts; this helps write them).

## Design / hard constraint
Surface scaffolds + lints at the mix conveyor.author front door against the conveyor.plan@1 / interface_contract@1 / test_specification@1 schemas. **Project anti-pattern (must hold):** the assistant aids the *human* author only; it must NEVER let the implementer agent author its own acceptance contract or red-team tests.

## Acceptance criteria
- Authoring a slice with an empty or non-falsifiable acceptance criterion emits a lint warning before the contract locks.
- A templated scaffold produces a schema-valid conveyor.plan@1 contract.

## Tests
Unit: lint catches empty/tautological acceptance; scaffold output validates against the schema; log each lint finding.

title: Agent-ergonomic CLI audit of mix conveyor.*
description:
## What
Make the mix conveyor.* operator surface excellent for humans and AI agents: consistent --json/robot modes, machine-parseable errors, stable exit/error keys (ADR-21), clear help.

## Why it serves the goal
The factory is operated through these mix tasks, and self-hosting (M8) means an *agent* operates them. Ergonomics serve the rubric (intuitive/ergonomic) and de-risk dogfooding.

## Design / MERGE
A coherent ergonomics pass that **folds the existing dr1m.6.1 operator-UX bug** (conveyor.show unordered 'latest' verdict; conveyor.run conflates parked with hard-fail) — relate, do not duplicate. Honor the ADR-21 process-exit / error-key conventions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Each mix conveyor.* task offers --json with a stable schema; non-zero exits carry a stable error key; conveyor.show orders verdicts deterministically; parked is distinct from hard-fail in conveyor.run output.

## Tests
CLI: snapshot --json output + exit codes for success/park/fail per task; log each task's contract.

## Related
dr1m.6.1 (folded).

title: Interrogator-on-ambiguity at authoring time (ADR-27)
description:
## What
When an authored intent is genuinely ambiguous, the factory asks clarifying questions before committing tokens — ADR-27's Interrogator, fired at the mix conveyor.author front door.

## Why it serves the goal
R4: decomposition quality is load-bearing. Catching ambiguity at authoring (a cheap question) beats discovering it after a parked overnight run. The M5 quality wedge that pairs with I5 (preview).

## Design
Wire the ADR-27 Interrogator (plan_interrogation@1 schema exists) to fire on an ambiguity heuristic at authoring; route questions to the human; block the run until resolved or explicitly waived. The real work is the ambiguity trigger heuristic.

## Acceptance criteria
- An intentionally ambiguous intent produces >=1 clarifying question (plan_interrogation@1) and does not auto-proceed; a clear intent proceeds untouched.

## Tests
Unit: the ambiguity heuristic flags an ambiguous fixture and passes a clear one; log the questions emitted.

## Related
M5 / ADR-27.

title: Structured incident timeline (incident_record@1)
description:
## What
When a run halts or a slice goes sideways, emit a structured incident (the incident_record@1 schema exists) with a causal timeline, surfaced in the cockpit for post-mortem.

## Why it serves the goal
Complements run-health (warns) and time-travel (replays) by capturing the failure as a first-class, triageable artifact you can learn from.

## Design
An incident producer keyed on halt / park-storm / runaway conditions, writing incident_record@1; a cockpit incident view reusing the time-travel timeline frames.

## Acceptance criteria
- A halted run produces an incident_record with a causal event timeline; the cockpit renders it.

## Tests
Unit: synthesize a halt -> assert an incident_record with ordered causal events. E2E: render the incident view; log the event chain.

## Related
I6 (time-travel frames), I7 (run-health).

title: Phase 1.5 + Phase 2 — Trust Qualification, Plan Compiler & Contract Foundry
description:
# Phase 1.5 + Phase 2 — program root

**What.** The complete next body of work after Phase 0/1, delivered as ONE strategic
program through **four independently useful increments** behind **two public release
gates** (`qualification_gate`, `phase2_gate`) plus one internal, non-authorizing
checkpoint (`compiler_structure_gate`). The four increments are P15-A (Evidence
Kernel), P15-B (Trust Qualification), P2-A (Compiler Core), P2-B (Contract Foundry +
serial pilot).

**One-line outcome.** Conveyor proves and scopes trust in its real-agent loop, then
compiles an immutable human plan through pure, inspectable passes into critic-reviewed,
verification-bearing, hierarchically approved contracts whose pre-registered serial
execution can be replayed, diagnosed, selectively invalidated, and stopped safely.

**Why / background.** Phase 0/1 proved Conveyor can drive ONE already-good Slice
through a fenced station loop and reject known-bad gate mutants. It leaves three
unknowns the program must close before Phase-3 parallelism amplifies them: the **loop
unknown** (do real stochastic agents produce correct, policy-compliant, replayable,
stoppable outcomes?), the **authority unknown** (can Conveyor state and expire the
exact scope its evidence qualifies?), and the **compiler unknown** (can it manufacture
honest work packets from a human plan without hiding assumptions, weakening intent, or
generating confetti graphs / vacuous tests?). The first two must be reduced before the
third is amplified.

**Central strategic choice.** Do not scale the number of agents until Conveyor has
proven the loop it will multiply, the exact authority that proof grants, and the
contracts it will feed into that loop.

**Sequencing (recommended default).** finish/freeze Phase 0/1 → retrospective + branch
decision → throwaway generated-contract tracer → **P15-A** dogfooded on Phase 1 →
**P15-B** → issue scoped QualificationGrant → targeted hardening if scope insufficient →
**P2-A** → `compiler_structure_gate` → **P2-B** + hierarchical approval → pre-registered
serial pilot → `phase2_gate` → evidence-based Phase-3 decision.

**Ship-and-stop boundary.** A completed P15-B (qualified, replayable, diagnosable
single-Slice factory with scoped grants) is independently valuable even if Phase 2 is
delayed by evidence uncovered during qualification.

**Design laws.** The 51 program design laws (§3) and Corrections A–Q (§0.2) are
invariants enforced across every increment, not slogans. Key spine: agents propose /
deterministic systems materialize; the loop is proven by eval not assertion; every
trust tool proves its own honesty; stochastic-from-tape / authority-from-current-checks;
no approval without scoped digest roots; fence every station + receipt every effect;
one PolicyDecision layer; ToolContracts + RoleViews are the security boundary;
qualification is a scoped grant not a badge; compilation fidelity ≠ product correctness.

**Refs.** Whole plan; §0 (executive recommendation), §1 (product contract), §18
(milestones), §19 (cutline), §28 (workstreams/ADRs/quality rule).
**Supersedes.** roadmap placeholder `software-factory-ai-sgp.1` (Phase 2).
close_reason: Phase 1.5 and Phase 2 locally completable work is complete and verified; remaining descendants are deferred roadmap follow-ups, not current local implementation work.

title: ADRs — irreversible design decisions required before implementation
description:
# ADR program (§28.2)

**What.** The set of Architecture Decision Records that MUST be approved before the
implementation they govern begins. Each ADR fixes an irreversible design decision; the
milestone that depends on a decision is wired to block on the corresponding ADR so the
decision is made first.

**Why.** These choices (canonicalization, digest domains, the single PolicyDecision
interface, fencing/effect semantics, replay freshness, compiler purity, root layering,
contract-evolution semantics, pilot pre-registration, …) are expensive to reverse once
schemas, evidence, and approvals depend on them. Recording them as first-class beads
keeps the rationale discoverable and prevents silent drift / a second migration wave.

**Scope.** 22 ADRs enumerated in §28.2, created as `docs` beads under this epic.

**Refs.** §28.2; cross-referenced by the milestone that each ADR gates.
close_reason: All 22 required ADR child beads are closed with accepted ADR documents under docs/adrs/.

title: ADR-01 — Phase 1.5 insertion, four increments, and gate semantics
description:
# ADR-01 — Phase 1.5 insertion, four increments, and gate semantics

**Decision.** Fixes the program shape: two public gates (qualification_gate, phase2_gate) + one internal compiler_structure_gate, delivered through four increments and one throwaway vertical tracer. Reframes the deferred roadmap Phase 2 (sgp.1) into P15-A/B + P2-A/B. Irreversible because all milestone IDs, evidence roots, and gate contracts hang off it.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A0` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 1; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-10 — Retention/redaction/GC and active-authority preservation
description:
# ADR-10 — Retention/redaction/GC and active-authority preservation

**Decision.** Decides retention classes, legal/audit holds, reference/hold-aware deterministic GC, erasure tombstones, and secure deletion (law 47): no retention rule erases active grant/approval/lock/incident/anchor evidence; erased evidence becomes explicit incomparable, not silently inspectable.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A4` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 10; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-11 — Emergency stop and global budget reservation
description:
# ADR-11 — Emergency stop and global budget reservation

**Decision.** Establishes durable emergency-stop (block new starts / revoke active authority / pause queues / human-decision resume, law 40) and transactional global+project budget reservation/circuit breaking ahead of any per-run budget (law 41). The break-glass control and runaway-spend guard.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A4` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 11; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-12 — CassetteSeries causal replay and mode-specific freshness
description:
# ADR-12 — CassetteSeries causal replay and mode-specific freshness

**Decision.** Decides the generation-vs-evaluation surface separation, the four replay modes (full/hybrid/proposal/compatible) + strict, causal partial-order transcripts, the NondeterminismLedger, and that recorded gate claims are never replay authority (Correction O, law 4).

**Gates.** milestone `P15-B3` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 12; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-13 — VerificationObligations, quarantine, and waiver semantics
description:
# ADR-13 — VerificationObligations, quarantine, and waiver semantics

**Decision.** Fixes per-obligation authority with multi-dimensional EvidenceRequirement (not a TestPack aggregate color), no flaky-required-evidence laundering by quarantine (Correction F, law 21), and scoped expiring waivers with owner/expiry/compensating-controls/reduced-autonomy.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-B4` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 13; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-14 — Pure compiler-pass architecture and memoization
description:
# ADR-14 — Pure compiler-pass architecture and memoization

**Decision.** Establishes that compiler semantics live in pure passes over a restricted PassContext with content-addressed memoization and hermeticity status (Correction K, law 44): Oban persists/schedules but owns no semantics; undeclared reads fail the pass. Defines the staged IR (Source/Intent/Candidate/Work/Contract/Authority).

**Gates.** milestone `P2-A2` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 14; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-15 — ClaimSet/SourceAnchor and deterministic provenance
description:
# ADR-15 — ClaimSet/SourceAnchor and deterministic provenance

**Decision.** Decides that the compiler assigns provenance wherever deterministically decidable and the model annotates only the residual (law 5); subtree claim inheritance + ClaimCoverageReport; stable byte/commit/symbol SourceAnchors. A forged human_explicit tag must be impossible.

**Gates.** milestone `P2-A0` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 15; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-16 — Separate work/interface/decision/verification/derivation graphs
description:
# ADR-16 — Separate work/interface/decision/verification/derivation graphs

**Decision.** Fixes that work dependencies model only execution-hard/integration-order, while interface readiness, human decisions, verification, and derivation/invalidation live in their own graphs (laws 17, 39, §4.5) — preventing false serialization, O(N²) interface edges, and unsafe selective reuse.

**Gates.** milestone `P2-A3` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 16; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-17 — Hierarchical authority/review/archive roots
description:
# ADR-17 — Hierarchical authority/review/archive roots

**Decision.** Decides the layered shared_authority_root / epic_authority_root / review_root / archive_bundle_root domains computed from a domain-separated RootManifest, with the approval record excluded from the root it signs (laws 8, 38). Enables honest partial reapproval and review-only errata.

**Gates.** milestone `P2-B4` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 17; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-18 — Interface lock/compatibility authority
description:
# ADR-18 — Interface lock/compatibility authority

**Decision.** Establishes InterfaceContract lock levels (strict/compatible_superset/review_required/informational) and compatibility policy, reserving strict for genuinely public/cross-Slice surfaces (law 15). No interface over-freezing of internal implementation detail.

**Gates.** milestone `P2-B1` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 18; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-19 — Mutation/reference-solution and compiler-falsifier policy
description:
# ADR-19 — Mutation/reference-solution and compiler-falsifier policy

**Decision.** Decides that universal code mutation at lock is circular without a legitimate independent reference (Correction D, law 16); Phase 2 hard-gates calibration/hermeticity/repeatability/base-behavior/obligation-mapping/falsifiers/adversarial review, and compiler-derived falsifiers establish a non-model floor (law 45).

**Gates.** milestone `P2-B2` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 19; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-02 — Live statistical quality vs deterministic hard invariants
description:
# ADR-02 — Live statistical quality vs deterministic hard invariants

**Decision.** Separates two evidence classes that must never be conflated: binary deterministic safety/authority invariants vs predeclared statistical sampling for stochastic live quality (laws 29–30). Decides the sampling-unit (repository case cluster) and anytime-valid stopping discipline. Reversal would let a flaky rerun-until-green gate or an averaged-away safety failure ship.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-B1` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 2; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-20 — Contract evolution always creates new lock/spec/attempt
description:
# ADR-20 — Contract evolution always creates new lock/spec/attempt

**Decision.** Fixes that any changed ContractLock/RunSpec terminates the prior immutable attempt and creates a new lock/spec/RunAttempt; contract faults are separate from implementation retries; no in-place attempt renegotiation (Correction E, laws 20, 7).

**Gates.** milestone `P2-B6` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 20; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-21 — Static/UI parity and process exit/error-key conventions
description:
# ADR-21 — Static/UI parity and process exit/error-key conventions

**Decision.** Establishes that CLI/static/LiveView are projections with identical authority (law 26), portable shell exit classes 0..125, and stable machine error_key/reason_codes for CI branching (§14.3). Prevents UI-as-source-of-truth and unportable exit codes.

**Gates.** milestone `P2-A4` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 21; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-21-static-ui-parity-and-process-exit-error-key-conventions.md and docs/adrs/adr-22-pre-registered-pilot-selection.md; full ADR verifier, ASCII scan, and git diff --check passed.

title: ADR-22 — Pre-registered pilot selection
description:
# ADR-22 — Pre-registered pilot selection

**Decision.** Decides that PilotSelection is immutable and frozen BEFORE the first selected implementation attempt; the selected set cannot change after outcomes and failed selections cannot be replaced (§17.4). Prevents easy-case cherry-picking and hidden compiler weakness.

**Gates.** milestone `P2-B7` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 22; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.

title: ADR-03 — Scoped QualificationGrant and impact/expiry semantics
description:
# ADR-03 — Scoped QualificationGrant and impact/expiry semantics

**Decision.** Decides that qualification is immutable scoped evidence (adapter/profile/archetype/toolchain/risk/environment/policy/verification/autonomy), not a runtime lease, plus the QualificationScopeLattice, direct/inherited/supporting evidence, expiry/invalidation triggers, and the AdmissionPermit/PermitCheckpoint split. Hard to reverse once grants gate every spec admission.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-B8` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 3; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-04 — Canonical schema registry, DigestRef, and canonicalization
description:
# ADR-04 — Canonical schema registry, DigestRef, and canonicalization

**Decision.** Fixes the algorithm-agile DigestRef, the canonicalization profile (rfc8785-jcs unless superseded), JCS-safe encoding of money/large-ints/timestamps/sets, domain-separated authority-root hashing, and the SchemaRegistryEntry/migration model. Everything downstream content-addresses against these rules; changing them forces a second migration wave.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A1` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 4; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.

title: ADR-05 — Attestation envelope and signature status
description:
# ADR-05 — Attestation envelope and signature status

**Decision.** Decides the in-toto Statement envelope, DSSE wrapping for team/cross-host/release/portable profiles, and the unsigned/locally_signed/externally_verified signature ladder. Emitting an in-toto shape must not imply a supply-chain assurance level not otherwise met.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A1` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 5; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.

title: ADR-06 — One PolicyDecision interface and reason-code stability
description:
# ADR-06 — One PolicyDecision interface and reason-code stability

**Decision.** Mandates a single typed DecisionContract evaluate() path with stable reason codes and a fail-closed `indeterminate` distinct from an authored deny (law 33). Domain modules may not call an untyped evaluate/4. Reversal reintroduces drifting, bypassable, per-call-site policy.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A2` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 6; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-07 — ToolContracts, RoleViews, and instruction authority
description:
# ADR-07 — ToolContracts, RoleViews, and instruction authority

**Decision.** Establishes that labels are not a prompt-injection boundary (Correction N, laws 34–37, 50): the real boundary is policy-compiled RoleViews + typed ToolContracts + host authorization + EnforcementProfiles + output validation. Untrusted content never gains instruction authority.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A2` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 7; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-08 — Station leases/fencing and EffectReceipts
description:
# ADR-08 — Station leases/fencing and EffectReceipts

**Decision.** Decides DB fencing tokens on every state write/effect (queue uniqueness ≠ ownership, Correction M) and the EffectAttempt→EffectReceipt→reconciliation model with declared delivery semantics (laws 31–32). Foundational to every external effect; near-impossible to retrofit.

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A3` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 8; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: ADR-09 — Causal events, trace propagation, PubSub, and ArtifactStore boundary
description:
# ADR-09 — Causal events, trace propagation, PubSub, and ArtifactStore boundary

**Decision.** Fixes the AuthorityEvent/ObservationSegment split, the staged-commit (no Postgres↔CAS distributed transaction), one trace_id correlation, transient PubSub vs durable segment catch-up, and Postgres-canonical-state / heavy-exhaust-in-CAS (Correction P, laws 42–43).

**Gates.** milestone `P15-A3` blocks on this ADR; the decision must be approved before that implementation begins.

**Status.** proposed — to be written under `docs/.../adrs/` and approved.

**Refs.** §28.2 item 9; see the gated milestone for the consuming context.
close_reason: Accepted ADRs documented in docs/adrs/adr-01 through adr-20; verified required sections, ASCII, and git diff --check.

title: P15-A — Evidence Kernel
description:
# P15-A — Evidence Kernel

**What.** Establish the reusable trust substrate BEFORE multiplying workflow-specific
resources: canonical identity/digests/attestations, typed PolicyDecisions, ToolContracts
and policy-compiled RoleViews, station leases + fencing tokens + effect receipts,
causal authoritative events + trace propagation, a pluggable content-addressed
ArtifactStore (LocalCAS default), retention/redaction/GC, emergency stop, global budget
reservation, and adapter-health primitives.

**Why.** Every later subsystem (Battery, compiler, approval, recovery, fleet) depends on
trustworthy evidence, policy, effect, trace, and storage semantics. Building these once,
correctly, avoids amplifying a loop whose authority/replay/policy/invalidation semantics
are still informal.

**Two checkpoints (§0, §18.1).** *P15-A-core* (A0–A3-ish: identity, typed policy,
RoleViews/ToolContracts, fencing, receipts, authoritative events, LocalCAS, trace, stop,
budget) is the minimum needed to trust an early Battery run — P15-B may begin once it is
dogfooded. *P15-A-hardening* (full retention/erasure, backend conformance, migration
breadth, restore testing, operator projections, remaining control-plane canaries)
completes before the release `qualification_gate`.

**Dogfood.** Run the existing Phase-1 loop through the kernel immediately (P15-A5).

**Cutline.** `P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED` (§19).
**Capability.** EVIDENCE-KERNEL, SCHEMA-REGISTRY, ATTESTATION-ENVELOPES, POLICY-DECISIONS,
TOOL-CONTRACTS, ROLE-VIEWS, FENCED-STATIONS, TRACE-EVENTS, ARTIFACT-STORE,
RETENTION-CONTROLS, EMERGENCY-CONTROL, DERIVATION-GRAPH.
**Refs.** §2.18, §4, §5, §13, §18.1, §28 (Workstreams A & B).
close_reason: Closed after P15-A0 through P15-A5 milestones, evidence-kernel runtime slices, schemas, canaries, and dogfood checkpoint were implemented and verified.

title: P15-A0 — Phase-1 retrospective, baseline freeze, and vertical tracer
description:
# P15-A0 — Retrospective, baseline freeze, vertical tracer

**What.** Freeze Phase-1 schema/gate/canary/toolchain/environment/adapter-capability/
artifact versions by digest; answer the retrospective with evidence; create the first
`PhaseNextDecision`; run ONE throwaway one-prompt generated-contract tracer through the
**real** Phase-1 loop (not the fake runner); publish a one-page findings note and update
the branch decision.

**Why.** The first durable artifact is `PhaseNextDecision`. The throwaway tracer tests the
program's most expensive assumption — that a machine-authored contract can drive the real
loop to an honest verdict — BEFORE horizontal infrastructure and schemas accumulate. Its
value is disproportionate to its tiny code because it tests the cross-phase integration
bet early. The branch table (§2.1) selects stop-the-line branches (gate_first,
adapter_first, policy_sandbox_first, evidence_integrity_first, …) that can block later
authority activation.

**Scope (children).** baseline freeze; retrospective evidence; PhaseNextDecision;
throwaway tracer; golden-journey suite seed; branch decision + findings note.

**Acceptance.** every branch cites a measured signal/incident/tracer finding; stop-the-line
branches block later authority; tracer code/contract is NOT promoted; human repair is
enumerated field-by-field; P2 schema work cannot freeze before findings are reviewed.

**Cutline.** never-cut (gates schema freeze). **Capability.** EVIDENCE-KERNEL (entry).
**Refs.** §0 (sequence), §2.1, §18.1 P15-A0.
close_reason: All P15-A0 deliverable leaves and acceptance gate are closed; artifacts live under docs/phase-1.5/p15-a0 and PhaseNextDecision schema/examples under docs/schemas.

title: P15-A1 — Capability/schema registries, digests, and attestations
description:
# P15-A1 — Registries, digests, attestations

**What.** `CAPABILITY-REGISTRY.md` (with legacy `C11–C20` aliases); a machine-readable
Schema Registry with shared enum vocabularies; the canonical JSON profile (`rfc8785-jcs`
unless superseded) and algorithm-agile `DigestRef`; an artifact schema-migration
framework; the in-toto attestation envelope + local verification; migrate/projection
adapters for Phase-1 evidence.

**Why.** Correction A — `C11–C20` are reused for different features; a canonical registry
removes the ambiguity. Exact bytes, execution authority, and review presentation are
different digest domains (Correction L); `DigestRef` is algorithm-agile and authority
roots use explicit domain separation so a digest can't be confused across root kinds.

**Acceptance.** no new ticket/ADR/schema uses ambiguous `Cxx` alone; every new artifact
carries schema version+digest+canonicalization profile; frozen old artifacts validate or
fail explicitly; breaking changes require migration; attestation subject-digest mismatch
fails; migration preserves original bytes + emits new lineage.

**Cutline.** `P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED`. **Capability.** SCHEMA-REGISTRY,
ATTESTATION-ENVELOPES. **Refs.** §0.2 A & L, §5.1, §21, §18.1 P15-A1.
close_reason: All P15-A1 deliverable leaves and acceptance gate are closed; registry artifacts, schemas, examples, and support docs are committed.

title: P15-A2 — PolicyDecision, ToolContracts, RoleViews, output boundaries
description:
# P15-A2 — Policy, tools, role views, output boundaries

**What.** PolicyBundle validation + `PolicyDecision` resource; the required `DecisionContract`
keys (§4.1.1); the ToolContract registry + host authorization + `EnforcementProfile`;
the RoleView compiler with scorer/implementer separation; generated-output
schema/size/depth/sensitivity/rendering validation; policy-bypass, hidden-oracle,
benign-content, and renderer fixtures.

**Why.** One auditable PolicyDecision layer (Correction N, laws 33–37): every consequential
question uses one typed `DecisionContract`; `indeterminate` fails closed and is distinct
from an authored deny. Labels are NOT a prompt-injection boundary — the real boundary is a
policy-compiled RoleView, typed ToolContracts, host-side authorization, least-privilege
capabilities, and output validation. Declared tool effects are enforced below the model.

**Acceptance.** every consequential action cites a PolicyDecision; alternate code paths
can't bypass policy; model-generated shell text never executes without an authorized
ToolContract; RoleViews exclude hidden/scorer-only subjects; benign repo docs remain usable
context; malicious active content is escaped/stripped; unsupported policy input defaults
deny/require-human.

**Cutline.** `P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED`. **Capability.** POLICY-DECISIONS,
TOOL-CONTRACTS, ROLE-VIEWS, PERMISSION-MODES. **Refs.** §4.1, §4.1.1, §5.2, §15.1, §18.1 P15-A2.
close_reason: Closed after P15-A2 policy boundary artifacts, registry resources, acceptance gate, and follow-on seam schemas were implemented and verified.

**Why.** Deliverable of **P15-A2 — PolicyDecision, ToolContracts, RoleViews, output boundaries** (§18).

**Refs.** §4.1, §4.1.1, §5.2, §15.1, §18.1 P15-A2.  **Cutline.** P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED.  **Capability.** POLICY-DECISIONS, TOOL-CONTRACTS, ROLE-VIEWS, PERMISSION-MODES.

title: P15-A2 — Definition of Done / acceptance gate
description:
# Acceptance gate — P15-A2 — PolicyDecision, ToolContracts, RoleViews, output boundaries

Close only when **all** milestone exit criteria (§18) hold:

- every consequential domain action cites a PolicyDecision
- alternate code paths cannot bypass policy
- model-generated shell text never executes without an authorized ToolContract
- RoleViews exclude hidden/scorer-only subjects
- a benign repository document remains usable context
- malicious active content is escaped/stripped
- default is deny/require-human when policy input is unsupported

**Refs.** §4.1, §4.1.1, §5.2, §15.1, §18.1 P15-A2.  Blocks on every deliverable leaf of this milestone.
close_reason: P15-A2 acceptance gate passed in docs/phase-1.5/p15-a2/acceptance-gate.md; verifier confirmed PolicyDecision citation, bypass fixture, ToolContract authorization, RoleView hiding, benign content, active-content stripping, and fail-closed defaults.

title: P15-A3 — Station leases, fencing, effect receipts, trace events, ArtifactStore
description:
# P15-A3 — Fencing, effect receipts, trace events, ArtifactStore

**What.** StationRun lease epoch/heartbeat/expiry; `EffectAttempt`→`EffectReceipt`
+ reconciliation; the canonical causal event envelope (`AuthorityEvent`) + trace
propagation; generic EventRouter/EventSegmentWriter; `ArtifactStore.LocalCAS` + a backend
conformance contract; optional S3-compatible backend; PubSub progress + durable catch-up;
a generic station worker skeleton.

**Why.** Correction M — queue uniqueness is not execution ownership: every durable station
uses a DB lease + monotonically increasing fencing token; every external effect declares
delivery semantics and has an idempotency key + durable receipt (laws 31–32). An
`outcome_unknown` call must not be silently treated as success/failure. Postgres stores
canonical state, not exhaust (Correction P, law 42): heavy event/blob payloads go to the
ArtifactStore; an authority transition + its outbox notification share one Postgres
transaction, but a blob upload and a DB update do not (no distributed transaction).

**Acceptance.** stale-epoch writes/effects rejected; duplicate effect reconciled or fails
ambiguous (never silently repeats); every effect/artifact correlates to trace/station/spec;
LiveView reconnect reconstructs ordered events after dropped PubSub; Postgres/Oban payloads
hold pointers/digests not heavy data; LocalCAS + optional S3 pass the same tests; worker
crash leaves recoverable segment/effect state.

**Cutline.** `P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED`. **Capability.** FENCED-STATIONS, TRACE-EVENTS,
ARTIFACT-STORE, DERIVATION-GRAPH. **Refs.** §4.6, §4.7, §5.8, §13.1–13.7, §16.1.1–16.1.2, §18.1 P15-A3.
close_reason: Closed after P15-A3 runtime slices, schema/model/crash artifacts, and acceptance gate were implemented and verified.

title: P15-A3.1 — StationRun lease epoch/heartbeat/expiry + fencing
description:
**What.** Extend `StationRun` with lease_epoch/owner/acquired/expires/heartbeat; claiming atomically validates an AdmissionPermit + control generation, checks stop/grant/budget/prereqs, increments the epoch, and stamps trace; every later write/effect carries the epoch and older-epoch writes are rejected.

**Why.** Deliverable of **P15-A3 — Station leases, fencing, effect receipts, trace events, ArtifactStore** (§18).

**Refs.** §4.6, §4.7, §5.8, §13.1–13.7, §16.1.1–16.1.2, §18.1 P15-A3.  **Cutline.** P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED.  **Capability.** FENCED-STATIONS, TRACE-EVENTS, ARTIFACT-STORE, DERIVATION-GRAPH.
close_reason: Implemented StationRun lease_epoch/owner-instance/acquired/trace fields, fencing helpers, claim-control validation, and station write fencing; verified by station_fencing_test and compile.

title: P15-A3.3 — Canonical causal AuthorityEvent envelope + trace propagation
description:
**What.** Implement the CloudEvents-compatible `AuthorityEvent` (stream_id/version, causation/correlation, trace_context, payload_ref, fencing epoch, policy decision) and one-`trace_id`-per-run propagation across jobs/effects/logs/artifacts, never leaking sensitive internal IDs to providers.

title: P15-A4 — Retention, redaction, emergency stop, global budget, adapter health
description:
# P15-A4 — Retention, emergency stop, budget, adapter health

**What.** Retention classes + legal/audit holds + GC dry-run/apply + erasure tombstones;
redaction/sensitivity scan before event/Cassette seal; `EmergencyStop` durable state +
CLI/UI + queue pause + cancellation/revocation; `BudgetEnvelope`/`BudgetReservation` +
rolling system/project circuits; `AdapterHealthState`/probe framework; control-plane
canaries.

**Why.** Immutable evidence still needs lifecycle, redaction, budget circuits, and a big
red button (laws 40–41, 47). Emergency stop blocks new starts, revokes/cancels active
authority, pauses queued work, requires a human decision to resume. Every provider/tool
call reserves budget first; global circuits stop a runaway graph even if per-run budgets
are wrong. GC never erases active grant/approval/lock/incident/anchor evidence.

**Acceptance.** active authority evidence cannot be GC'd; erased/unavailable evidence
becomes explicit `incomparable`; stop prevents new claims/effects/publication + requires
HumanDecision resume; active sessions cancel/revoke within policy deadline or qualification
fails; provider calls can't start without a reservation; runaway fixture opens the budget
circuit; adapter-health failure expires/narrows affected authority but a coding-quality
miss alone does NOT open the circuit.

**Cutline.** `P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED` (hardening). **Capability.** RETENTION-CONTROLS,
EMERGENCY-CONTROL. **Refs.** §4.8, §5.9, §13.8, §18.1 P15-A4.
close_reason: Closed after P15-A4 schemas, control-plane hardening modules, canary manifest, and acceptance gate were implemented and verified.

description:

**Why.** Deliverable of **P15-A4 — Retention, redaction, emergency stop, global budget, adapter health** (§18).

**Refs.** §4.8, §5.9, §13.8, §18.1 P15-A4.  **Cutline.** P15_A_EVIDENCE_KERNEL_REQUIRED (hardening).  **Capability.** RETENTION-CONTROLS, EMERGENCY-CONTROL.

title: P15-A5 — Evidence Kernel dogfood checkpoint
description:
# P15-A5 — Evidence Kernel dogfood

**What.** Run the existing Phase-1 tracer through PolicyDecision, ToolContract, RoleView,
fencing, receipts, trace, ArtifactStore, stop, budget, and retention paths; produce a
static evidence report + migration notes. No new functionality beyond kernel adoption.

**Why.** The kernel must be useful BEFORE the Battery exists (ship-and-stop discipline) and
must not change Phase-1 semantics. Dogfooding proves no bespoke workflow bypasses the kernel
and that adoption is real, not aspirational.

**Acceptance.** original Phase-1 success/failure semantics unchanged; deterministic replay
of the Phase-1 fixture stable; all new kernel canaries pass; no bespoke workflow bypasses
the kernel; the kernel is useful before the Battery.

**Depends on.** P15-A2 (policy/tools/roleviews) + P15-A4 (effects/controls). Unlocks P15-B.
**Cutline.** never-cut (gates P15-B start). **Capability.** EVIDENCE-KERNEL (proven).
**Refs.** §0 (dogfood), §18.1 P15-A5, §28 Workstream B.
close_reason: Closed after tracer kernel route, static evidence report, no-bypass audit, and acceptance gate were implemented and verified.

title: P15-B — Trust Qualification
description:
# P15-B — Trust Qualification

**What.** Turn the Phase-1 tracer into a permanent full-loop **Battery** with separate
conformance, safety-invariant, outcome-quality, and operability case classes; qualify the
primary live adapter; prove capability-degradation paths with a deterministic
MockDegraded; record multi-sample causal Agent Cassettes; harden VerificationObligation
integrity; expand canaries + trust-tool meta-canaries; build the Evidence Time Machine
(typed comparison), immutable diagnosis and separately authorized recovery; and issue
scoped, expiring **QualificationGrants** rather than a global green badge.

**Why.** A deterministic fake runner proves plumbing and gate canaries, not real-agent
outcome quality, safe trajectories, or honest refusal. The public `qualification_gate`
issues machine-enforced grants whose scope is no broader than the evidence.

**Thesis.** Gate canaries prove deterministic authority rejects labeled bad patches; the
Battery proves the whole loop follows safe trajectories and reaches statistically adequate
outcomes on real work (including cases where the correct outcome is refusal, dispute,
policy block, or explicit uncertainty); a grant states exactly which scope that authorizes.

**Depends on.** P15-A (kernel). Forensics (B6) may start right after B1.
**Cutline.** `P15_B_QUALIFICATION_CORE_REQUIRED` (§19).
**Capability.** TRUST-BATTERY, ADAPTER-QUALIFICATION, AGENT-CASSETTES, TEST-INTEGRITY,
QUALIFICATION-GRANTS, EVIDENCE-FORENSICS, FAILURE-DIAGNOSIS, BEHAVIOR-LOCK (seed).
**Refs.** §2, §5.3, §12, §16, §17.1–17.2, §18.2, §28 (Workstreams C & D).
close_reason: All P15-B trust qualification milestones B1-B8 are closed, including the public qualification_gate, scoped grants, live sampling, canaries/meta-canaries, Evidence Time Machine, recovery, schemas, and acceptance gates; verified focused P15-B/P15-B8 suites and compile checks during milestone closures.

title: P15-B1 — Battery case classes, secure scorer store, and honest runner
description:
# P15-B1 — Battery classes, scorer store, honest runner

**What.** conformance / safety-invariant / outcome-quality / operability case schemas; the
trace-assertion language (`never`/`always`/`eventually`/bounded-count over canonical
events + effect receipts); the role-safe vs scorer-only fixture split; an archetype/trap
corpus including the **poison pill**; a predeclared versioned `SamplingPolicy`; the
`BatteryRun`/`BatterySampleResult`/`BatteryCaseResult` scorer (`RunBattery`).

**Why.** The original plan mixed four concerns (Correction B, §2.3). A terminal outcome is
insufficient — a run that read a hidden oracle and was later policy-blocked still violated a
safety invariant, so cases assert over the whole trace. The runner must prove its own
honesty (Correction C): the poison pill yields `battery_fixture_failure`, never an agent
failure. Statistical unit is a repository case cluster, not a repeated attempt.

**Acceptance.** fixture validation precedes provider calls; poison pill → `battery_fixture_failure`;
safety-trajectory violations detected even when terminal outcome is safe; failed samples
cannot be omitted/replaced; scorer-only metadata never reaches RoleViews/prompts/workspaces/
projections; threshold/stop-rule change creates a new policy digest; provider/infra failures
separated from quality.

**Cutline.** `P15_B_QUALIFICATION_CORE_REQUIRED`. **Capability.** TRUST-BATTERY.
**Refs.** §2.3–2.6, §5.3, §16.4, §18.2 P15-B1.
close_reason: All P15-B1 children and acceptance gate are closed; evidence in docs/phase-1.5/p15-b1/acceptance-gate.md and focused Battery tests.
