The seed creates the sample Project, Plan, Requirements, HumanDecisions, Epic, Slice, AgentBrief, TestPack, ContractLock, and initial RunSpec with the first recorded base commit, and re-seed preserves existing immutable rows and the original base commit without duplicates. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused conveyor_seed_sample_test (2 passed), format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, full mix test (164 passed), credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P1.3 — Plan audit gate (run on sample)
description:
# P1.3 — Plan audit gate (run on sample)

**What.** Exercise `mix conveyor.plan_audit` against the seeded sample plan and
the deliberately-broken fixtures.

**Why / background.** Proves the P0.4 audit machinery on the real Phase-1 plan —
the first station of the loop (§22.4 step 4). Treating manual input as
automatically executable is the bad habit this gate prevents.

**Acceptance.** Sample plan reaches handoff_ready; missing-AC / missing-test /
missing-decision fixtures fail with stable findings.

**Refs.** §22.4 (4), §25 P1.3. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P1.3 by exercising mix conveyor.plan_audit on the seeded sample plan and deliberately broken missing-AC, missing-test, and missing-decision fixtures. Sample reaches handoff_ready; broken fixtures block with stable findings. Latest verification for child iqb.3.1: focused plan audit tests (12 passed), full mix test (167 passed), credo --strict, and dialyzer under Elixir 1.20.

title: P1.3.1 Run plan_audit on sample + failing fixtures
description:
**What.** Run `mix conveyor.plan_audit` on the seeded sample + broken fixtures.
**Why.** Proves the P0.4 audit on the real Phase-1 plan (station 4) and that
broken handoffs are blocked.
**Scope.** run audit on sample → handoff_ready; missing-AC / missing-test /
missing-decision fixtures → blocked; assert findings.
**Acceptance.** Sample reaches handoff_ready; each broken fixture fails with
stable findings.
**Refs.** §22.4 (4), §25 P1.3.
close_reason: Exercised mix conveyor.plan_audit against the seeded sample plan and broken fixtures. Plan audit now reuses seeded Project/Plan records by local path and contract digest, the sample sidecar reaches handoff_ready, missing-ac/missing-test/missing-decision fixtures block with stable findings, and the missing-decision fixture is snapshotted. Tightened sample DEC-001 wording to avoid vague audit language. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused plan audit tests (12 passed), format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, full mix test (167 passed), credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P1.4 — Readiness gate
description:
# P1.4 — Readiness gate

**What.** `Readiness.check/1` confirms a locked Brief with concrete acceptance
criteria, required tests, key interfaces, out-of-scope, risk, and matching
ContractLock content hashes before a Slice may run.

**Why / background.** Readiness is the gate between "approved" and "ready" — the
second contract gate after plan audit (§22.4 step 5). It catches vague/oversized
Briefs before any container spins up.

**Scope.** Readiness.check/1; failure modes (vague/testless →
needs_clarification/too_large).

**Acceptance.** Complete Brief → ready; vague or testless Brief →
needs_clarification/too_large.

**Refs.** §22.4 (5), §25 P1.4. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P1.4 readiness gate: Readiness.check/1 locked Brief validation (099a163) and failure-mode classification/tests for needs_clarification and too_large (61c14f7). Latest full Elixir 1.20 gate passed with 184 tests, credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P1.4.1 Readiness.check/1 (locked Brief validation)
description:
**What.** `Readiness.check/1`.
**Why.** Station 5: confirm a locked Brief with concrete ACs, required tests, key
interfaces, out-of-scope, risk, and matching ContractLock content hashes before a
container spins up.
**Scope.** validate Brief lock + ContractLock digests match; concrete ACs;
required tests present; → Slice ready.
**Acceptance.** Complete Brief → ready.
**Refs.** §22.4 (5), §25 P1.4.
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.Readiness.check/1 for locked Brief validation. It verifies handoff-ready Plan, latest AgentBrief, ContractLock, TestPack, required Brief fields, acceptance criteria/tests, and ContractLock digests; passing checks transition the Slice to ready. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused readiness tests (2 passed), full gate (180 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P1.4.2 Readiness failure modes + tests
description:
**What.** Readiness failure modes + tests.
**Why.** Vague or testless Briefs must be rejected as needs_clarification/
too_large before implementation.
**Scope.** detect missing ACs / no required tests / vague wording; map to
needs_clarification/too_large; tests for each.
**Acceptance.** Vague/testless Brief → needs_clarification/too_large.
**Refs.** §25 P1.4, §20 (Brief Failure).
close_reason: Added Readiness failure classification for missing acceptance criteria, missing required tests, vague wording, and oversized briefs. Vague/testless/missing briefs return needs_clarification; oversized briefs return too_large; lock mismatches remain blocked. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused readiness tests (6 passed), full gate (184 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P1.5 — Context Scout + quality-signal baseline
description:
# P1.5 — Context Scout + quality-signal baseline

**What.** The read-only `ContextScout` station producing a cited `ContextPack`,
plus the `CodeQualityAdapter` family (Noop + LocalPython defaults; optional
CodeScent) and the before-work quality baseline artifact.

**Why / background.** The scout reduces agent confusion before the implementer
gets edit authority. Design law 7: context tools never mutate source. Quality
signals are risk/context/gate-delta signals — NEVER sole proof. For an
OSS-friendly default, the demo must work with the Noop/local adapter; CodeScent
is an optional advanced adapter.

**Scope.** ContextScout (rg + file tree + Plan/Req/Decision/Slice/Brief +
AGENTS.md) → ContextPack (relevant_files w/ reasons, key_interfaces, existing
tests, risks, suggested validation, confidence); CodeQualityAdapter behaviour +
Noop + LocalPython; optional CodeScent adapter + baseline artifact + adapter
conformance contract.

**Acceptance.** Pack names router/model/tests with reasons and confidence;
baseline quality artifact recorded; Noop/local path needs no proprietary tools.

**Refs.** §13, §22.4 (8), §25 P1.5. Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED (advanced CodeScent cut-first).
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (B1): priority raised to P1 (was P2). The minimal ContextScout station + Noop/LocalPython quality adapter (iqb.5.1 and iqb.5.2, also raised to P1) are on the P1 tracer critical path -- they block the P1 prompt builder iqb.6. Cutline clarification: the minimal scout is TRACER_REQUIRED; only the advanced CodeScent adapter (iqb.5.3, stays P3) is cut-first. Also now blocked by the station-execution behaviour q19.6.8 (finding A1).
close_reason: ContextScout, Noop/LocalPython quality adapters, and optional CodeScent adapter are implemented, verified, committed, and pushed.

title: P1.5.1 ContextScout station → ContextPack
description:
**What.** ContextScout station → cited ContextPack.
**Why.** Read-only station (design law 7: no source mutation) that reduces agent
confusion before the implementer gets edit authority.
**Scope.** inputs (Plan/Req/Decision/Slice/Brief + AGENTS.md + config + rg/file
tree + quality signals + existing tests); output ContextPack (relevant_files w/
reasons, key_interfaces, existing_tests, risks, suggested_validation, confidence,
code_quality_refs).
**Acceptance.** Pack names router/model/tests with reasons + confidence.
**Refs.** §13, §22.4 (8).
close_reason: Implemented ContextScout station in lib/conveyor/context_scout.ex with persisted ContextPack output and coverage in test/conveyor/context_scout_test.exs.

title: P1.5.2 CodeQualityAdapter behaviour + Noop + LocalPython
description:
**What.** CodeQualityAdapter behaviour + Noop + LocalPython adapters.
**Why.** For an OSS-friendly default, the demo must work WITHOUT proprietary
tools. Quality signals are risk/context/gate-delta — never sole proof.
**Scope.** CodeQualityAdapter/ScoutSignalAdapter behaviour (before/after/gate);
Noop (advisory only); LocalPython (configured local tools); result schema
conveyor.quality_result@1.
**Acceptance.** Noop + LocalPython produce valid results; default demo needs no
proprietary tools.
**Refs.** §13.
close_reason: Implemented CodeQualityAdapter behavior, schema result, Noop and LocalPython adapters, persisted CodeQualityRun output, and focused adapter coverage.

title: P1.5.3 Optional CodeScent adapter + baseline + conformance
description:
**What.** Optional CodeScent adapter + baseline artifact + conformance contract.
**Why.** CodeScent is an optional ADVANCED adapter (cut-first); it may block the
gate only if it declares a deterministic adapter_contract (version command,
result schema, fixture suite, threshold policy).
**Scope.** CodeScent adapter; before-work baseline artifact (codescent/before.json);
adapter_contract + fixture conformance; gate-blocking only when policy selects it.
**Acceptance.** Baseline artifact recorded; adapter passes conformance; absent
CodeScent never breaks the default demo.
**Refs.** §13, §22.4 (8).
close_reason: Implemented optional CodeScent adapter contract, before-work baseline helper, conformance fixture test, and missing-tool blocked result; default adapters remain independent.

title: P1.6 — Prompt builder
description:
# P1.6 — Prompt builder

**What.** `PromptBuilder` emits a versioned, immutable prompt envelope (§14) from
structured inputs (Brief, ContextPack, AGENTS.md, policy, required tests, output
schema), with explicit trust labels on untrusted context.

**Why / background.** The conductor owns the instruction hierarchy. Repo files,
comments, tool output, and scout findings are UNTRUSTED data — they may inform
implementation but must never override the Slice contract, safety policy, locked
tests, AGENTS.md, or Conveyor rules. PromptBuilder must label untrusted excerpts
explicitly so RunCheck can later reject outputs that followed them.

**Scope.** versioned prompt envelope (Role, Autonomy, Project Instructions, Slice
Contract, Context Pack w/ untrusted-data banner, Safety Policy, Work Rules,
Required Verification, Required Output Schema); InstructionSource trust labeling;
snapshot tests; prompt immutability + template version recording.

**Acceptance.** Snapshot-tested; no unlocked fields omitted; untrusted sections
carry explicit trust banners.

**Refs.** §5, §14, §22.4 (9), §25 P1.6. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: PromptBuilder envelope, trust-labeled InstructionSource rows, structured output schema, immutability, and snapshot coverage are implemented, verified, committed, and pushed.

title: P1.6.1 PromptBuilder envelope + InstructionSource trust labels
description:
**What.** PromptBuilder versioned prompt envelope + InstructionSource trust labels.
**Why.** The conductor owns the instruction hierarchy; untrusted repo/tool data
must be labeled so it cannot override the contract/policy/locked tests.
**Scope.** envelope sections (§14: Role, Autonomy, Project Instructions, Slice
Contract, Context Pack + untrusted-data banner, Safety Policy, Work Rules,
Required Verification, Required Output Schema); InstructionSource records
(trust_level + digest + included_in_prompt); template_version.
**Acceptance.** Untrusted sections carry explicit trust banners; InstructionSource
rows recorded for each input.
**Refs.** §5, §14.
close_reason: Implemented PromptBuilder envelope assembly with required sections and persisted trust-labeled InstructionSource rows for template, project, plan, brief, AGENTS.md, context pack, repo files, and tool outputs.

title: P1.6.2 Output schema + prompt immutability + snapshot tests
description:
**What.** Output-schema section + prompt immutability + snapshot tests.
**Why.** Prompts are immutable artifacts; template versions are recorded so later
learning compares outcomes across revisions.
**Scope.** required output schema (summary/files_changed/commands_attempted/
acceptance_mapping/known_risks/blocker?); persist RunPrompt immutably; snapshot
tests asserting no unlocked fields omitted.
**Acceptance.** Snapshot-tested; prompt body is content-addressed + immutable.
**Refs.** §14, §23.
close_reason: Added structured required output schema, RunPrompt body_sha256 content address, create/read-only prompt resource, DB immutability trigger, and prompt snapshot/immutability tests.

title: P1.7 — Docker workspace + policy + baseline/calibration
description:
# P1.7 — Docker workspace + policy enforcement + baseline/calibration

**What.** The `SandboxRunner` behaviour with a hardened Docker adapter, workspace
materialization + cleanup + reaper, the CredentialBroker/CredentialLease,
conductor-network segregation, end-to-end implement-profile enforcement, and the
BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration stations.

**Why / background.** Docker is necessary but not sufficient. Sandbox constraints
must remain true even if the agent ignores instructions (rootless, no socket, no
host home, network=none by default, no-new-privs, resource limits). The
conductor DB/ledger must be UNREACHABLE from the sandbox network so a
compromised/injected agent cannot probe or mutate conductor state. Baseline must
be green and the locked acceptance suite must calibrate RED on the base commit
for expected reasons before implementation — otherwise the test does not prove
anything.

**Scope.** SandboxRunner behaviour + Docker adapter (materialize/exec/destroy);
sandbox hardening (§12.3); WorkspaceMaterialization lifecycle + cleanup_policy +
SandboxReaper; CredentialBroker + CredentialLease; conductor-network
segregation; implement-profile enforcement (allowed run; forbidden→incident);
BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration + TestPackCalibration record.

**Acceptance.** Allowed commands run; forbidden fixtures create incidents;
baseline green on base; locked acceptance suite calibrates expected red;
unexpected greens/missing identities block before implementation.

**Refs.** §12.3, §12.4, §12.5, §22.4 (6,7,10), §25 P1.7. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: All P1.7 child beads 7.1-7.7 implemented, verified, committed, and pushed.

title: P1.7.1 SandboxRunner behaviour + Docker adapter
description:
**What.** SandboxRunner behaviour + Docker adapter (materialize/exec/destroy).
**Why.** Keeps Docker from becoming the conductor API; leaves room for Podman/
Firecracker/K8s/remote sandboxes later.
**Scope.** SandboxRunner behaviour (§12.5); Docker adapter: container-per-run from
pinned image; materialize repo checkout at base commit; exec NormalizedCommand;
destroy.
**Acceptance.** Materialize→exec→destroy round-trips against the sample image.
**Refs.** §12.5, §15.
close_reason: Implemented Sandbox.Runner behavior callbacks, DockerRunner materialize/exec/destroy, Materialized handle, workspace tracking, and Docker adapter coverage.

title: P1.7.2 Sandbox hardening (rootless/no-socket/network=none/limits)
description:
**What.** Sandbox run-spec hardening.
**Why.** Sandbox constraints must remain true even if the agent ignores
instructions.
**Scope.** non-root/rootless; no privileged; no Docker socket; no host home mount;
read-only mounts for contracts/policies/.conveyor; rw only for workspace;
no-new-privileges; seccomp/AppArmor where available; CPU/mem/process/output/
wall-clock limits; network=none by default. Doctor fails hard if required
constraints unavailable for the selected profile.
**Acceptance.** A container runs with all constraints; missing required constraint
fails doctor for that profile.
**Refs.** §12.3.
close_reason: Implemented hardened Docker profile flags, wired them into DockerRunner, and added Doctor sandbox constraint checks with tests.

title: P1.7.3 WorkspaceMaterialization lifecycle + SandboxReaper
description:
**What.** WorkspaceMaterialization lifecycle + cleanup + SandboxReaper.
**Why.** Each purpose (baseline/calibration/implement/gate/canary/post_integration)
gets its own materialization; failed workspaces preserved only when policy allows;
orphans reaped.
**Scope.** materialization records + head_tree_sha256; cleanup_policy (delete/
preserve_on_failure/preserve_always) + cleanup_status; Sandbox.Reaper for orphan
containers/workspaces.
**Acceptance.** Workspaces clean up per policy; reaper removes orphans after
crash.
**Refs.** §6.1, §8 (reaper), §15 (steps 1,10,11).
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (B2): this bead is the SOLE owner of the SandboxReaper (orphan container/workspace cleanup). iqb.14.1 and iqb.14.7 reference it; they do not rebuild it.
close_reason: Implemented workspace cleanup policy handling, head tree digests, and SandboxReaper orphan cleanup with coverage.

title: P1.7.4 CredentialBroker + CredentialLease
description:
**What.** CredentialBroker + CredentialLease.
**Why.** Pi provides no built-in credential controls; the broker owns provider
access. Raw secrets should not be injected unless no safer adapter mode exists.
**Scope.** short-lived leases scoped to one run/station; exposed only as named env
keys allowed by policy; never written to prompts/artifacts/child logs; revoked on
cancel/violation/completion.
**Acceptance.** A lease is scoped + expiring; revoked on completion; secrets never
appear in artifacts.
**Refs.** §12.4, §12.5.
close_reason: Implemented CredentialBroker issue/revoke/expire lifecycle with policy env-key validation and non-persistent secret handling.

title: P1.7.5 Conductor-network segregation (DB unreachable from sandbox)
description:
**What.** Conductor-network segregation.
**Why.** A compromised/injected agent must not be able to probe or mutate
conductor state. The AshPostgres DB, ledger, and internal services must be
UNREACHABLE from the sandbox network; approved egress is brokered to external
hosts only.
**Scope.** network policy per station (§12.4 table); egress proxy/allowlist;
assert conductor DB unreachable from sandbox.
**Acceptance.** From inside the sandbox, the conductor DB/ledger are unreachable;
only allowlisted egress works.
**Refs.** §12.0 (threats), §12.4.
close_reason: Implemented sandbox network policy defaults, internal egress allowlist rejection, and Docker profile host-network safeguards.

title: P1.7.6 Implement-profile enforcement end-to-end
description:
**What.** End-to-end implement-profile enforcement.
**Why.** Prove the policy engine + sandbox + ToolExecutor compose: allowed
commands run; forbidden ones create incidents.
**Scope.** wire ToolExecutor (P0.6) through the Docker SandboxRunner under the
implement profile; allowed-command happy path; forbidden-command fixtures →
Incident + block.
**Acceptance.** Allowed commands run; forbidden fixtures create incidents and stop
the run.
**Refs.** §12.5, §25 P1.7.
close_reason: Implemented policy-bound sandbox executor that routes allowed commands through DockerRunner and blocks forbidden commands before sandbox execution.

title: P1.7.7 BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration stations
description:
**What.** BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration stations + TestPackCalibration.
**Why.** Stations 6–7. Baseline must be green on the base commit; the locked
acceptance suite must calibrate RED for the expected reasons BEFORE implementation
— otherwise a test proves nothing. Unexpected greens / missing identities /
unrelated failures block the Slice before implementation.
**Scope.** BaselineHealth (clean materialization, baseline_regression suites);
AcceptanceCalibration (locked acceptance_locked TestPack on base, expected red);
TestPackCalibration record (expected_failures/unexpected_passes/unexpected_failures,
status valid/invalid).
**Acceptance.** Baseline green on base; acceptance suite calibrates expected red;
unexpected results block before implementation.
**Refs.** §22.4 (6,7), §17 (tests stage).
close_reason: Implemented BaselineHealth and AcceptanceCalibration stations with TestPackCalibration records and result blobs.

title: P1.8 — Pi AgentRunner over RPC (+ fake runner)
description:
# P1.8 — Pi AgentRunner over RPC (+ fake runner)

**What.** The `AgentRunner` behaviour + capability→autonomy mapping, the
normalized agent event envelope, the `Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi` adapter (BEAM Port
over RPC/JSONL, 11-step flow §15), heartbeat/idle/timeout enforcement, PatchSet
capture, and a deterministic Fake runner for default CI + adapter conformance
tests.

**Why / background.** Pi is first because it provides a structured RPC seam and
minimal orchestration overlap, but the contract is deliberately broader so
Cursor/Codex/Claude Code/OpenHands/Aider/Goose can be swapped without reworking
the state machine. RawRunResult is the agent's REPORTED output — NOT trusted
evidence. The conductor collects the final git diff as a PatchSet (never trusts
uncommitted workspace state) and verifies on a fresh checkout. Default CI uses
the Fake runner — no live model calls.

**Scope.** AgentRunner behaviour + Capabilities + AgentProfile capability→
autonomy mapping; normalized event envelope + required event types → ledger;
Pi adapter (Port, RPC, 11-step flow, host-controlled-tools vs in-container
observe-only profiles); heartbeat/idle/timeout/output-limit + cancel; PatchSet
capture from fresh git diff; Fake AgentRunner + adapter conformance tests.

**Acceptance.** Events stream to ledger w/ monotonic sequence numbers; final diff
captured; timeouts/idle detection work; Fake runner exercises the flow with no
credential; conformance tests pass.

**Refs.** §15, §15.1, §22.4 (10), §23, §25 P1.8. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (fake runner never-cut; live Pi tagged).
close_reason: Closed after all P1.8 child beads were implemented and verified: AgentRunner behaviour/capabilities, ledger event envelope, Pi RPC adapter, session limits/cancel, PatchSet capture, and Fake runner conformance.

title: P1.8.1 AgentRunner behaviour + Capabilities + autonomy mapping
description:
**What.** AgentRunner behaviour + Capabilities + capability→autonomy mapping.
**Why.** Keeps Pi/Cursor/Codex/Claude Code/OpenHands/Aider/Goose interchangeable.
Capabilities (pre-exec policy, cancellation, diff capture, cost reporting, mcp,
structured output, session resume, known_limitations) map to an autonomy ceiling
— explicitly, not implicitly. Negative capabilities recorded in RunSpec so old
evidence stays interpretable.
**Scope.** AgentRunner behaviour (run/cancel/capabilities); Capabilities struct
(§15); AgentProfile; capability→autonomy-ceiling mapping per the §15 matrix.
**Acceptance.** An adapter without pre-exec policy is capped at L1 (observe-only)
in a hardened sandbox; capabilities recorded in RunSpec.
**Refs.** §15.
close_reason: Implemented AgentRunner behaviour, capabilities/profile/raw-result structs, explicit capability-to-autonomy mapping, negative capability normalization, and RunSpec snapshot coverage.

title: P1.8.2 Normalized agent event envelope → ledger
description:
**What.** Normalized agent event envelope → ledger.
**Why.** Adapters may emit rich raw transcripts, but the NORMALIZED event stream
is what LiveView/RunCheck/replay/policy/conformance consume. MCP/slash-commands
are alternative transport, never a bypass of command policy or evidence capture.
**Scope.** conveyor.agent_event@1 envelope; required event types (session_started
… session_completed); monotonic sequence_no; raw_ref to blobs; write into Ledger.
**Acceptance.** All required event types stream with monotonic sequence numbers;
raw transcript content-addressed.
**Refs.** §15.1.
close_reason: Implemented normalized AgentRunner event recorder with conveyor.agent_event@1 envelope, monotonic sequence enforcement, raw blob refs, and ledger writes.

title: P1.8.3 Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi (Port/RPC, 11-step flow)
description:
**What.** `Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi` over RPC (BEAM Port, JSONL), 11-step flow.
**Why.** Pi is first: a structured RPC seam, no TUI scraping, minimal
orchestration overlap. Two profiles: pi_host_controlled_tools (preferred;
tools via ToolExecutor) and pi_in_container_observe_only (L1 only; stricter
sandbox).
**Scope.** §15 steps: materialize implement workspace; container from pinned image;
mount workspace + caches only; inject scoped creds + safe env; launch Pi RPC over
Port; prefer ToolExecutor routing else observe-only + lower ceiling; stream events
+ heartbeats; enforce limits; collect final git diff as PatchSet; apply to fresh
gate materialization; teardown per cleanup policy.
**Acceptance.** Given a RunPrompt, Pi edits the repo and returns a RawRunResult;
events stream; diff captured from fresh base.
**Refs.** §15, §12.5 (profiles).
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi with Phase 1 profiles, JSONL RPC client seam, ledger event streaming, raw transcript blob refs, and fresh-base git diff capture.

title: P1.8.4 Heartbeat/idle/timeout/output limits + cancel
description:
**What.** Heartbeat / idle / timeout / output-limit enforcement + cancel.
**Why.** Bound runaway sessions; non-progress is budget exhaustion (ties to
RunBudget P0.6.7).
**Scope.** heartbeat tracking; max wall-clock/idle; output-size limit; cancel
(best_effort/hard per capability); emit cancel_requested/acknowledged events.
**Acceptance.** Idle/timeout/output-flood stops the session with a structured
finding; cancel is acknowledged.
**Refs.** §12.2 (budget), §15.
close_reason: Implemented AgentRunner session limits with heartbeat/idle/wall-clock/output accounting, structured stop findings, and Pi cancel_requested/cancel_acknowledged event emission.

title: P1.8.5 PatchSet capture from fresh git diff
description:
**What.** PatchSet capture from a fresh git diff.
**Why.** Do NOT trust uncommitted workspace state as evidence; the conductor
captures the final git diff against the base commit.
**Scope.** compute diff base_commit→head; PatchSet (patch_ref/sha256,
changed/added/deleted/renamed, lines_added/deleted, touches_locked_paths,
applies_cleanly); store patch as content-addressed blob.
**Acceptance.** PatchSet records scope + locked-path touch flag + clean-apply
status.
**Refs.** §6.1, §15 (step 9).
close_reason: Implemented fresh-base PatchSet capture with content-addressed patch blobs, file scope metrics, locked-path touch detection, clean-apply checks, and Pi run integration.

title: P1.8.6 Fake AgentRunner + adapter conformance tests
description:
**What.** Deterministic Fake AgentRunner + adapter conformance tests.
**Why.** Default CI must use a deterministic fake (no live model calls); the
hermetic tracer + canaries depend on it (never-cut). Conformance tests validate
every adapter.
**Scope.** Fake runner returning canned RawRunResult/patch; adapter conformance
suite (capability reporting, startup, normalized event streaming, monotonic
sequence, cancellation, timeout, diff capture, malformed-output handling, policy
semantics).
**Acceptance.** Fake runner drives the full flow with no credential; conformance
suite passes for Fake (and is ready for Pi).
**Refs.** §15.1, §18 (adapter_conformance), §23.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic Fake AgentRunner with canned RawRunResult/PatchSet flow and reusable adapter conformance tests for capabilities, event streaming, monotonic sequence, cancellation, diff capture, and malformed-output findings.

title: P1.9 — Evidence recorder + independent verification
description:
# P1.9 — Evidence recorder + independent verification

**What.** `EvidenceRecorder` applies the recorded PatchSet to a CLEAN gate
workspace, independently re-runs verification suites from structured test results,
maps acceptance criteria to evidence, writes evidence/dossier/manifest/diff/logs
idempotently, and runs the Security.Redactor.

**Why / background.** Design law 3: no completion without evidence — agent
self-report is not evidence. The conductor independently records it. The
clean-container reproducibility law: a diff that passes in the agent container
must pass in the gate container or the run fails. Redaction must preserve
provenance semantics (raw vs redacted digests) and never project sensitive blobs.

**Scope.** apply PatchSet to clean gate workspace; independent rerun via test
result adapters (JUnit/TAP/JSON); AC→evidence mapping + reject missing required
tests; evidence/dossier/manifest/diff/logs write + idempotency; Security.Redactor
(scan + sensitivity classification + quarantine); clean-container reproducibility
test.

**Acceptance.** Writes the full evidence set; maps ACs; rejects missing required
tests; regeneration is idempotent; secrets are detected and blocked/redacted per
policy.

**Refs.** §5, §16, §16.1, §22.4 (11), §23, §25 P1.9. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (clean-gate verification never-cut).
close_reason: Completed P1.9 child beads 9.1 through 9.6: clean gate PatchSet application, independent verification reruns/adapters, AC evidence mapping, evidence packet recording/idempotency, secret redaction/quarantine, and clean-container reproducibility. Verified during child beads with Elixir 1.20 full test gates plus static checks.

title: P1.9.1 Apply PatchSet to clean gate workspace
description:
**What.** Apply the recorded PatchSet to a CLEAN gate workspace.
**Why.** The conductor independently verifies against a fresh checkout — agent
self-report is not evidence (design law 3). Patch must apply cleanly to the
expected base.
**Scope.** materialize gate workspace at base commit; apply PatchSet; record
head_tree_sha256; fail on non-clean apply / unexpected base.
**Acceptance.** Patch applies to a clean checkout; head tree digest recorded.
**Refs.** §5, §15 (step 10), §16.
close_reason: Implemented clean gate workspace PatchSet application with base validation, patch apply failure findings, WorkspaceMaterialization records, and RunAttempt head tree digest updates.

title: P1.9.2 Independent rerun + structured test-result adapters
description:
**What.** Independent rerun of verification suites + structured test-result
adapters.
**Why.** The conductor re-runs required suites from STRUCTURED results, not by
trusting the agent. Result adapters parse junit/tap/json into pass/fail per test
identity.
**Scope.** TestResultAdapter family (JUnit/TAP/JSON/stdout); run baseline_regression
+ acceptance_locked suites; repeat/flake_policy/infra_retry_policy support;
classify flakes.
**Acceptance.** Suites rerun cleanly; structured results map to test identities;
flakes classified per policy.
**Refs.** §6.3 (command_specs), §16, §17 (tests stage), §24 (flaky tests).
close_reason: Implemented structured TestResultAdapter family for stdout/JSON/TAP/JUnit and VerificationRerunner for baseline_regression/acceptance_locked suites with repeats, flake classification, and infra retry support.

title: P1.9.3 Acceptance mapping + reject missing required tests
description:
**What.** Acceptance mapping (AC→evidence) + reject missing required tests.
**Why.** Every AC must have passed evidence; the locked required tests must be
present and not replaced.
**Scope.** map each AC to required_test results; evidence_status per AC;
reject/flag missing or skipped required tests.
**Acceptance.** Missing required test → blocking finding; every AC maps to a
passed/failed/skipped result.
**Refs.** §6.3 (acceptance_criteria), §16.1, §17 (stage 8).
close_reason: Implemented acceptance criteria to structured test-result mapping with evidence_status/evidence_refs and blocking findings for missing or skipped required tests.

title: P1.9.4 Evidence/dossier/manifest/diff/logs + idempotency
description:
**What.** Write evidence/dossier/manifest/diff/logs + idempotency.
**Why.** Evidence packets are product artifacts (§16); regeneration must be
idempotent (checksums stable, only expected timestamps change).
**Scope.** EvidenceRecorder writes machine evidence (§16.1) + dossier (§16.2) +
diff.patch + command logs via the Projector; RunAttempt → evidence_recorded.
**Acceptance.** Full evidence set written; regeneration is idempotent.
**Refs.** §16, §16.1, §16.2, §22.4 (11).
close_reason: Implemented Evidence Recorder artifact packet generation with machine evidence, dossier, diff, verification logs, projector integration, RunAttempt evidence_recorded transition, and idempotent regeneration. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate and static checks.

title: P1.9.5 Security.Redactor (secret scan + redaction/quarantine)
description:
**What.** Security.Redactor (secret scan + sensitivity classification + quarantine).
**Why.** Before evidence is displayed/exported, scan prompts/tool outputs/command
logs/diffs/env metadata/reports for secrets. Redaction must preserve provenance
semantics (raw vs redacted digests); sensitive blobs are never projected.
**Scope.** secret scanning; findings; classify raw as sensitive/quarantined;
produce redacted projections w/ own digest; record raw_sha256 (if retained) +
redacted_sha256; a blocked secret prevents gate success unless policy allows
redacted continuation.
**Acceptance.** Fake credentials in logs/diffs/prompts are detected; exported
evidence blocks or redacts per policy; gate never compares redacted bytes vs raw
digests blindly.
**Refs.** §16, §12.0 (secret exposure), §23 (redaction tests).
close_reason: Implemented Security.Redactor secret scanning, redaction/block policy, artifact raw/redacted digest provenance, recorder integration, and projection-safe redacted evidence. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate, Credo, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.9.6 Clean-container reproducibility test
description:
**What.** Clean-container reproducibility test.
**Why.** A diff that passes in the agent container must pass in the gate
container, or the run fails — the core anti-"works on my machine" invariant.
**Scope.** test that runs verification in the agent container and the fresh gate
container and asserts equivalence.
**Acceptance.** A divergence between agent-container and gate-container results
fails the run.
**Refs.** §23 (clean-container reproducibility).
close_reason: Implemented clean-container reproducibility verification by running suites through agent and gate runners, comparing deterministic result digests, and failing on divergence. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate, Credo, and Dialyzer.

title: M4 Stream E: wire static gate stages live on the production path
description: M4 Stream E activates the dormant static gate stages on the production SerialDriver path (default_gate_stages), each gated on the green reference (samples/beads_insight). LANDED (3, all required, merged in PR #19): workspace_integrity (b04190f — non-mutating git-write-tree head-tree producer in default_gate_context; the intended PatchSetApplicator producer is dormant/never called in the live loop, so the digest is produced at gate time), policy_compliance (2e95458), acceptance_mapping (529f48b — agent_brief+verification_result already in context). Production gate now runs 7 required stages: workspace_integrity, contract_lock, diff_scope, secret_safety, policy_compliance, test_execution, acceptance_mapping. REMAINING (6, each needs a real producer or risks vacuity): observed_risk (review_policy resolver — an empty default policy = advisory-theater, needs real risk semantics), build_install (build_install_result, M), provenance_attestation (prompt_sha256 + evidence_sha256, M), reviewer_aggregation (Reviews + reviewer_health, L), canary_freshness (GateHealth seeder + project_id, L), code_quality_delta (CodeQualityRun + adapter contract, L).

Production gate now runs 7 required stages. Gated green: deterministic 911, eval 71 (incl. replay determinism), credo/format clean. title: ROADMAP Track A — serial-autonomous loop (M0-M3 filed; M4-M8 in ROADMAP.md)
description: Source of truth: ROADMAP.md (repo root), from the 2026-06-21 16-agent evidence audit. Strategic call: harden a width-1 serial but FULLY AUTONOMOUS loop to a numeric exit bar, THEN parallelism. This epic tracks M0–M3 (the serial spine); M4–M8 to be filed as they near. Exit bar (§4 of ROADMAP.md): real agent through production loop in CI; unattended >=20-slice plan to green x5; mix conveyor.author front-end; first-pass-gate>=70%; dispute<20%; parked<15%; gate_canary zero false-pass in CI; watchdog+resumability; demonstrated lift over bare-agent. Principle: FREEZE net-new verifier surface; wire what exists.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: PROGRAM MAPPING (2026-07-02 idea-wizard): the improvement program filed today carries ROADMAP M4/M5/M6 remainder work — M4: trust-reviewer-m4b2 (reviewer stage + producer; static mutants in gauntlet-static-mutants-2fgf; hermetic gate rerun in containment-complete-h3o2.2; other static-stage producers stay in jmnt), M5: prose-to-plan-aaun (Decomposer, author->import, bulk approve), M6: rework-intel-rt6k (convergence sentinel, no-progress, infra-failure classification) + terminal-triage-uevc (parked-queue triage, CLI-first) + a3hf.2.1.x (budget/emergency-stop, CLI-first steer applied). Exit evidence: bd50 (retitled) + gexs + 9z4r.2. STILL UNFILED from M6 remainder, deliberate: GC/retention sweeper (ADR-10) and morning-digest wiring (a3hf.1.1 exists) — file when overnight medium-plan runs get near.

title: Conveyor-owned agent containment — pull M6 blast-radius forward (wire DockerRunner/ToolchainRunner/Sandbox.Reaper)
description: Pull ROADMAP M6 blast-radius isolation (D1 #1/#5) forward: run the agent subprocess inside a Conveyor-enforced boundary (network egress per %Policy{}, workspace-scoped writes, env allowlist) by wiring existing DockerRunner + ToolchainRunner hermetic backend + Sandbox.Reaper (one pinned image shared by agent+gate), or 'unshare -n' + egress on non-Docker hosts. No new runtime dependency. Triggered by Claude Code default (bypassPermissions removes Codex --sandbox confinement). Plan U8: docs/plans/2026-06-29-001-feat-claude-code-agent-backend-plan.md. Distinct from r6c5 (M7 worktree). Future remote backends (crabbox/E2B/Cloudflare Sandboxes) deferred.
close_reason: Shipped: Conveyor-owned agent containment via ContainedExec (docker run reusing DockerProfile/NetworkPolicy hardening); both station adapters routed through it; real-Docker enforcement verified (egress blocked, writes confined, env scrubbed, non-root). Commit f3b9361 (U8). Branch feat/claude-code-agent-backend.

title: Claude Code agent backend (both touchpoints, default; Codex selectable)
description: Add Claude Code as a pluggable Conveyor agent backend at both touchpoints (AgentRunner station adapter + PlanFoundry Drafter), driven by the headless claude CLI; make it the default with Codex selectable. Implements plan docs/plans/2026-06-29-001-feat-claude-code-agent-backend-plan.md (U1-U7). Default flip gated on agent-containment. Related: bd50 (real agent through production loop), p639 (watchdog reused via shared adapter base).
close_reason: Shipped: Claude Code backend at both touchpoints (AgentRunner.ClaudeCode + ClaudeCodeDrafter via shared PlanPrompt/AdapterBase), default at station + PlanFoundry + conveyor.run CLI, Codex selectable. Commits 0f72204/639e466/73e9d11/9065fd5 + docs 5b48bb0. Full suite green (1205 passed). Plan docs/plans/2026-06-29-001-feat-claude-code-agent-backend-plan.md.

title: EVAL-095: Lift Duel reasoning sweep + harder tasks
description: Follow-up to R5 (EVAL-091). v1 seed showed 0 correctness lift (both arms one-shot the 3 easy mutants at reasoning=medium) but a real ~15% efficiency lift (rich brief -> 72k fewer tokens, cost_per_verified_ac_ratio 0.846). Two extensions to find the correctness lift: (1) Reasoning sweep — run LiftDuel at low/medium/high (the harness already takes :reasoning_effort / k); hypothesis: the brief's correctness edge appears at LOW reasoning where the agent is weaker. (2) Harder tasks — add multi-file / underspecified broken->fix tasks beyond the 3 single-line behavioral mutants. Also: wire the static gate stages (P2-B) so false_pass/policy_violations/test_integrity deltas (currently always 0) become measurable — that is the factory's biggest differentiated value vs vanilla. Optional: pass@k (k>1 real samples/cell) for tighter CIs; AgentStation-routed e2e for governance fidelity.

title: M6: durable crash-recovery for serial runs (SerialRun anchor + accept-SHA + reconciliation)
description:
DEFERRED FROM M3 (Robert's call 2026-06-22): the M3 exit bar tests slice-failure survival + N-run determinism, NOT crash-recovery; §4's 'crashed run resumes from durable state' is M6's bar (overnight 15-40 slice plan, where a lost run = hours). M3 ships only minimal idempotency (find-or-create graph + skip-already-gated + convergent re-run). This is the HEAVY part (Option B from the m3-resume-scope understand pass), cost L:

- New SerialRun/RunCursor Ash resource (today PlanRunner returns an in-memory Result, never persisted) anchoring: serial_order, the frozen selected_slice_ids, per-slice cursor status, events list + replay_digest.
- Capture + persist the accept-commit SHA right after commit_workspace_changes! (git rev-parse HEAD) onto each RunAttempt/cursor row — today the SHA is discarded, so the DB cannot say which slice produced which HEAD commit.
- Resume coordinator: load SerialRun, reconcile each accepted slice's stored SHA against git reachability (git merge-base --is-ancestor of HEAD: skip if reachable, re-run if absent, HALT on divergence), then continue the loop.
- Tests: crash-at-each-boundary, divergence detection, byte-exact replay continuity on a fresh process.

Brittleness to handle: SHA anchoring breaks if a post-step rewrites HEAD (squash/rebase on integration, post_integration.ex). This is the genuinely M6-shaped, correctness-heavy design; do it where the overnight plan makes it load-bearing AND actually tests it.

title: Wire ADR-24 MidflightCheck: conductor-mediated read-only AC/locked-path feedback to the agent
description: Give the live agent a scoped read of which AC it is failing / whether it touched a locked path (never the hidden mutation/red-team oracle). pass@1 lever. Built+tested, zero callers today.
close_reason: Duplicate of dr1m.2 (ADR-24 MidflightCheck, in_progress). M2 (vp73) now links dr1m.2 directly. See ROADMAP.md v2 M2.

title: M1b: production RunSpecAssembler for first-light slice
description: Build the first M1b vertical slice from HANDOFF.md: add Conveyor.Planning.RunSpecAssembler by productionizing the test-only BridgeFixtures chain enough to assemble a self-describing station_plan for a single slice, with station module metadata embedded so RunSlice can resolve stations without test opts. Follow TDD; keep eval-shared ToolchainRunner untouched unless explicitly coordinated.
close_reason: Implemented production RunSpecAssembler vertical slice with self-describing station plans; verified with planning_run_spec_assembler_test, run_slice_test, golden_thread_test --include eval, mix compile --warnings-as-errors, and mix format --check-formatted.

title: Watchdog/timeout around codex.ex System.cmd (bounded + kill)
description: codex.ex:201 System.cmd has NO timeout; a hung Codex hangs an unattended multi-hour run forever. Add bounded timeout + kill + route to retry/park.
close_reason: Implemented: AdapterBase.run_with_timeout/5 watchdog wraps the agent System.cmd for both adapters (Codex + ClaudeCode); brutal_kill on timeout (default 900s) -> exit 124 + empty output -> slice fails its gate and parks/reworks. Covered by codex + claude_code watchdog tests. Shared base extracted in #39 (U6); covers the codex.ex:201 path. --force: parent M2/vp73 still open, but this watchdog bug is resolved.

title: Ignore local Mix and tooling cache artifacts
description: Update .gitignore with sensible local defaults for this Elixir/Phoenix repo and remove accidentally staged .mix-home cache files from the Git index while preserving them on disk.
close_reason: Updated .gitignore for local Mix/Hex caches and removed .mix-home from Git tracking while preserving local files.

title: feat: DB-native Plan/Epic authoring CLI (F2) — conveyor.plan.import/create + conveyor.epic.create
description:
F2 from the 2026-06-25 dogfood (docs/brainstorms/2026-06-25-conveyor-dogfood-loop-requirements.md): there was no operator CLI to create an Epic or Plan, so the DB-native author->run loop required a hand-written Ash seed script to set up Project+Plan+Epic before the task.* CLI could be used.

Delivered (plan docs/plans/2026-06-25-001-feat-dep-direction-and-plan-cli-plan.md, units U2-U4; requirements R4-R8):
- mix conveyor.plan.import <doc> -- materialize a whole plan document (Project+Plan+Epic+Slices+deps) via PlanContract.load + PlanImporter.import_result!.
- mix conveyor.plan.create -- find-or-create Project by --workspace-path, plus a runnable :draft Plan + first Epic carrying a minimal conveyor.plan@1 contract.
- mix conveyor.epic.create --plan <uuid> -- add another epic to an existing plan.

Two latent bugs the new CLI surfaced were fixed in the same change:
- PlanImporter.create_edges! used String.to_existing_atom, which crashed any edge-bearing import in a fresh VM and half-persisted; replaced with a literal parse_kind/1 plus up-front edge-kind validation (nothing persists on a bad/unsupported graph).
- ContractBuilder.build/1 hardcoded verification_commands => [], so the lock-time recompile dropped a plan.create shell's verification commands (run path fell back to pytest -q); build/1 now preserves the plan's verification_commands.

Note: the origin plan mis-referenced this finding as bead g2iu. g2iu is actually the F1 conveyor.seed_sample / SampleTasksContract AGENTS.md test failure (still open, unrelated). This bead is the correct F2 tracker.
close_reason: Delivered in feat/dep-direction-and-plan-cli (commit 546c3b2): conveyor.plan.import/create + conveyor.epic.create, R4-R8, with the two latent import/contract-builder fixes.

title: conveyor.plan@1 schema forbids work_dependencies -> hand-authored plans cannot express a dep-graph
description: Found during M3 live validation. PlanRunner READS contract['work_dependencies'] (plan_runner.ex:253) and the work_graph fully supports a branching dep-graph, but the conveyor.plan@1 JSON schema has additionalProperties:false and does not list work_dependencies, so PlanContract.load REJECTS any plan that declares them (schema_validation_failed at #/work_dependencies). Net: mix conveyor.run is forced into the linear chunk_every fallback; a hand-authored plan cannot express independents/branches. Blocks (a) a rich LIVE skip-and-continue demo (independents proceed past a park) and (b) Phase-2 hand-authored plans with real dependencies. Fix: add an optional work_dependencies array (items {from,to,kind}) to the plan@1 schema. Low risk (the consumer already exists).
close_reason: Done on feat/plan-work-deps-schema (pending PR): optional work_dependencies [{from,to,kind}] added to conveyor.plan@1 + semantic validation (refs/self-loop/cycle) at load. Proven LIVE: gx 7-slice branching plan through the production loop — happy :passed 7/7; induced SLICE-003 park -> independents 004/005 proceeded + 006/007 skip-cascade -> :partial 4/7 (impossible under the old linear chunk_every fallback).

title: Phase 0 — Foundations & factory kernel
description:
# Phase 0 — Foundations & factory kernel

**What.** Stand up Conveyor's deterministic Elixir/BEAM core and the small
"factory kernel" surface that makes every later phase safer: Ash/Postgres
domain, append-only ledger, durable Oban stations, policy resources, config +
doctor, plan audit, AGENTS.md generation, artifact projection, and a LiveView
skeleton.

**Why / background.** Conveyor is an AI-first software factory. The guiding bets
are: isolation over coordination, the verification gate is the human's stand-in,
agents produce bounded execution (not authority), and the deterministic
conductor owns truth while stochastic agents own generation/judgment. Phase 0
deliberately lays MORE domain surface than Phase 1 exercises, because
retrofitting evidence, policy, traceability, and adapter boundaries after agents
are already running is how these systems become fragile. We build only the
kernel — NOT an issue tracker, chat system, LLM framework, static analyzer, or
deployment platform.

**Scope.** P0.0 docs · P0.1 scaffold · P0.2 config+doctor · P0.3 Ash domain ·
P0.4 plan audit · P0.5 Slice state machine + ledger · P0.6 policy engine ·
P0.7 AGENTS.md · P0.8 artifact projector · P0.9 LiveView skeleton.

**Definition of done.** Active resources defined & migrating; legal/illegal
state transitions enforced with one ledger event per transition; doctor reports
missing prerequisites clearly; good plan reaches handoff_ready; artifacts
regenerate idempotently with content digests.

**Refs.** PHASE-0-1 plan §1, §6, §7, §8, §25 (Phase 0). Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: All direct Phase 0 child beads are closed; deferred resource specs remain explicitly deferred

title: P0.0 — Product contract docs
description:
# P0.0 — Product contract docs

**What.** Author the standalone product-contract documents that define what
Conveyor promises and refuses: VISION.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, AUTONOMY_LEVELS.md,
SAFETY_POLICY.md, TASK_SCHEMA.md, EVIDENCE_SCHEMA.md, plus the canonical JSON
schema registry under docs/schemas/.

**Why / background.** The first public promise must be modest and credible:
"Conveyor converts a human-approved plan into coordinated, verified
implementation work packets, with evidence strong enough to support PRs and
eventually low-risk auto-merge." We deliberately do NOT promise "fully
autonomous software development" yet. These docs anchor every downstream
decision (autonomy dial, evidence requirements, policy defaults, non-goals) so
contributors and agents share one contract.

**Scope.** L0–L4 autonomy dial with Phase-1 target stated (L1 + L2 artifacts);
the 11-class threat model; determinism boundary; evidence schema; schema
registry (conveyor.plan@1, run_spec@1, station_plan@1, evidence@1, review@1,
gate@1, run_bundle@1) with golden valid/invalid examples.

**Acceptance.** Docs state the L1 Phase-1 target, evidence requirements, policy
defaults, and non-goals; schema files validate the sample contract.

**Refs.** §0.2, §3, §5, §6.1.1, §10, §12.0. Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED.
close_reason: All P0.0 product-contract docs and schema registry are implemented. Verified required docs, L1/L2 artifact constraints, evidence requirements, policy defaults, non-goals, and JSON Schema golden examples with local acceptance script (46 checks passed).

title: P0.0.1 Write VISION.md + ARCHITECTURE.md
description:
**What.** Write VISION.md and ARCHITECTURE.md.
**Why.** Anchor the product contract ("verified work packets, human-approved
merges") and the architecture overview (§4 station pipeline) + determinism
boundary (§5: deterministic conductor owns truth; agents own generation/judgment;
instruction hierarchy). These are the documents new contributors/agents read
first.
**Scope.** VISION (one-paragraph context §0, product contract & autonomy line
§0.2, design laws §3, non-goals §1); ARCHITECTURE (station diagram §4,
determinism boundary §5, OTP/Oban topology §8 overview).
**Acceptance.** Docs state the L1 Phase-1 target and the 10 design laws; explicit
non-goals listed (no issue tracker/chat/LLM-framework/static-analyzer/deploy).
**Refs.** §0, §0.2, §1, §3, §4, §5.
close_reason: Implemented VISION.md and ARCHITECTURE.md. Verified by local acceptance script: Phase 1 L1 target, all 10 design laws, explicit non-goals, station pipeline, determinism boundary, and OTP/Oban topology present (34 checks passed).

title: P0.0.2 Write AUTONOMY_LEVELS.md (L0–L4 dial)
description:
**What.** Write AUTONOMY_LEVELS.md documenting the L0–L4 policy dial.
**Why.** Autonomy is modeled as a dial from day one so authority increases ONLY
after evidence proves the gate is trustworthy (design law 4: no authority without
measured trust). Phase-1 target is L1 with L2-shaped artifacts; no auto-merge/
auto-deploy.
**Scope.** Table of L0 planning → L4 auto-deploy with authority allowed at each;
state Phase-1 target; describe how capability matrix + gate metrics gate
promotion.
**Acceptance.** Doc states L1 target with L2 artifacts and that merge stays a
manual human action in Phase 1.
**Refs.** §0.2, §15 (capability→autonomy matrix).
close_reason: Implemented AUTONOMY_LEVELS.md with L0-L4 dial, Phase 1 L1 with L2-shaped artifacts, manual merge constraint, capability gates, and promotion metrics; verified with acceptance script.

title: P0.0.3 Write SAFETY_POLICY.md (threat model + defaults)
description:
**What.** Write SAFETY_POLICY.md.
**Why.** Safety is not deferred. Docker limits blast radius; policy limits intent.
This doc records the 11-class threat model and the policy defaults the engine
will enforce, and states that each threat class must have at least one Phase-1
test/canary/doctor check.
**Scope.** threat model table (§12.0); policy profiles (§12.1); minimum denylist
(§12.2); sandbox run spec (§12.3); image/egress/credential policy (§12.4);
conductor-network-unreachable rule.
**Acceptance.** Doc enumerates all 11 threats with primary defenses and the
denylist; states "Docker necessary but not sufficient".
**Refs.** §12.0–§12.5.
close_reason: Implemented SAFETY_POLICY.md with 11 threat classes, policy profiles, minimum denylist, sandbox run spec, egress/credential policy, and conductor-network-unreachable rule; verified by local acceptance script (20 checks passed).

title: P0.0.4 Write TASK_SCHEMA.md + EVIDENCE_SCHEMA.md
description:
**What.** Write TASK_SCHEMA.md and EVIDENCE_SCHEMA.md.
**Why.** Define the human-facing contract for what a Slice/Brief must contain and
what counts as evidence, so plan audit and the gate have an authoritative spec to
validate against. Evidence is the trust primitive (design law 3).
**Scope.** TASK_SCHEMA (Brief fields, acceptance_criteria shape §6.3, required
tests, verification commands, contract lock fields); EVIDENCE_SCHEMA (machine
evidence §16.1, dossier §16.2, PR-body §16.3, RunBundle §6.4).
**Acceptance.** Schemas match the §6.1 resource fields and §16 artifact shapes.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.3, §16.
close_reason: Implemented TASK_SCHEMA.md and EVIDENCE_SCHEMA.md matching §6.1 resource fields, §6.3 embedded shapes, §6.4 RunBundle projection, and §16 evidence/dossier/PR-body artifacts; verified with local acceptance script.

title: P0.0.5 JSON schema registry (docs/schemas/) + golden examples
description:
**What.** Create the canonical JSON Schema registry under docs/schemas/.
**Why.** RunCheck and plan audit validate EXACT schema versions; schemas are
append-only within a major version. A local registry of canonical schemas +
golden valid/invalid examples is the contract every artifact is checked against.
**Scope.** conveyor.plan@1, run_spec@1, station_plan@1, evidence@1, review@1,
gate@1, run_bundle@1; examples/ (evidence.valid.json,
evidence.invalid.missing-ac-evidence.json); compatibility notes.
**Acceptance.** Each schema has a schema_version, a golden valid example, and a
golden invalid example; the sample conveyor.plan@1 contract validates.
**Refs.** §6.1.1, §10 (schema policy), §23 (schema compatibility tests).
close_reason: Implemented docs/schemas registry for conveyor.plan@1, run_spec@1, station_plan@1, evidence@1, review@1, gate@1, and run_bundle@1 with compatibility notes and golden valid/invalid examples; verified with python3 jsonschema meta-check and example validation (7 schemas checked, 0 failures).

title: P0.9 — LiveView skeleton
description:
# P0.9 — LiveView skeleton

**What.** A minimal Phoenix LiveView run viewer rendering the Project/Plan/Epic/
Slice hierarchy and the ledger timeline, updating live as ledger events append.

**Why / background.** Phase 1 is headless-first: the static report and machine
artifacts are the primary product surface; LiveView is a live projection of the
SAME data. Do not overbuild a beautiful dashboard yet — "LiveView polish" is
explicitly cut-first under the schedule-protection rule.

**Scope.** ConveyorWeb endpoint + run viewer; live updates via EventOutbox/PubSub.

**Acceptance.** A seeded Slice updates live when ledger events append.

**Refs.** §21, §25 P0.9. Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY (polish cut-first).
close_reason: Run viewer LiveView and EventOutbox/PubSub live updates implemented, verified, committed, and pushed.

title: P0.9.1 Run viewer LiveView (hierarchy + ledger timeline)
description:
**What.** Run viewer LiveView (Project/Plan/Epic/Slice + ledger timeline).
**Why.** A live projection of the same data the static report shows. Keep it
minimal — polish is cut-first.
**Scope.** LiveView rendering the hierarchy + ledger timeline; routes; basic
layout.
**Acceptance.** Renders a seeded Slice + its ledger timeline.
**Refs.** §21, §25 P0.9.
close_reason: Implemented Run viewer LiveView at /runs with Project/Plan/Epic/Slice hierarchy and seeded ledger timeline coverage.

title: P0.9.2 Live updates via EventOutbox/PubSub
description:
**What.** Live updates from EventOutbox/PubSub.
**Why.** The viewer must update live as ledger events append — consuming the
outbox so it never shows an uncommitted transition.
**Scope.** subscribe to PubSub topics; patch assigns on new events.
**Acceptance.** A seeded Slice updates live when ledger events append.
**Refs.** §8.1, §21, §25 P0.9.
close_reason: Run viewer subscribes to ledger_events PubSub broadcasts and refreshes after EventOutboxRelay publishes committed ledger events; covered by LiveView test.

title: P0.1 — Project scaffold
description:
# P0.1 — Project scaffold

**What.** A booting Phoenix + Ash + AshPostgres + Oban application with the
supervision tree from §8, green CI, a pinned tooling matrix, and an
OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry skeleton.

**Why / background.** The BEAM is chosen for durable supervision, concurrent
orchestration, stronger compile-time checks, and later self-healing. Getting the
supervision topology and telemetry conventions right on day one avoids
backfilling trace_ids and metric dimensions once Phase 3 scheduling and Phase 6
cost observability arrive.

**Scope.** Conveyor.Application supervision tree (Repo, Oban, Endpoint,
Conductor.Supervisor children); CI (mix test, format --check, Credo/Dialyzer);
tested matrix Elixir 1.20.x / OTP 27+ recorded in evidence; telemetry span
hierarchy + metric cardinality rules.

**Acceptance.** App boots; CI is green; runtime versions are captured per run.

**Refs.** §2, §8, §8.1, §9.1, §25 P0.1. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (never-cut foundation).
close_reason: All P0.1 scaffold child beads are implemented. Verified Docker Elixir 1.20 CI-equivalent checks (format, compile, 11 tests, Credo, Dialyzer), runtime_versions capture, and Phoenix boot against Postgres 16.

title: P0.1.1 App boots + §8 supervision tree skeleton
description:
**What.** Boot a Phoenix+Ash+AshPostgres+Oban umbrella/app and stand up the §8
supervision tree skeleton.
**Why.** Durable supervision and a correct topology on day one. The
Conductor.Supervisor children (Ledger, Telemetry, Config, Policy.Engine,
Security.Redactor, Artifacts.Projector, EventOutbox, Effects.Reconciler,
Sandbox.Reaper) and the Oban worker modules should exist as supervised
skeletons even before full behavior lands.
**Scope.** mix project; Repo; Oban; Endpoint; Conductor.Supervisor with named
child skeletons; Oban worker module stubs (RunSlice, BaselineHealth, etc.).
**Acceptance.** `mix phx.server`/app boots cleanly; supervision tree matches §8.
**Refs.** §2, §8.
close_reason: Implemented Phoenix/Ash/AshPostgres/Oban scaffold with Conveyor.Application, Repo, Endpoint, Oban, Conveyor.Conductor.Supervisor children, and station Oban worker stubs. Verified under Docker elixir:1.20 (Elixir 1.20.1/OTP 29): mix format --check-formatted, mix compile, mix test (4 passed), and mix phx.server boot against Postgres 16 with Oban migrations.

title: P0.1.2 CI pipeline (test/format/credo/dialyzer)
description:
**What.** CI pipeline running mix test, mix format --check-formatted, Credo, and
Dialyzer (where configured).
**Why.** A green baseline from commit one; TDD the deterministic core means CI
must be trustworthy before any station logic lands.
**Scope.** CI workflow; format/credo/dialyzer config; cache for deps/PLTs.
**Acceptance.** CI is green on the empty scaffold.
**Refs.** §25 P0.1, §23.
close_reason: Implemented CI workflow for Elixir 1.20.1/OTP 29 with Postgres 16, dependency/_build/PLT cache, format, compile, tests, Credo, and Dialyzer. Added Credo/Dialyxir deps and configs. Verified locally in Docker elixir:1.20: mix format --check-formatted, mix compile --warnings-as-errors, MIX_ENV=test mix test (4 passed), mix credo --strict (no issues), mix dialyzer (passed with one narrow Phoenix upstream ignore).

title: P0.1.3 Tool matrix pinning + runtime version capture
description:
**What.** Pin the tested tool matrix and capture per-run runtime versions.
**Why.** Reproducibility first. Every run records the runtime that produced its
evidence so old evidence stays interpretable.
**Scope.** pin Elixir 1.20.x / OTP 27+, Phoenix/Ash/Oban versions; capture
elixir/otp/phoenix/ash/oban/docker_engine/sandbox_runner/agent_adapter versions +
toolchain_image_digest into RunSpec/evidence.
**Acceptance.** A run records all version fields from §9.1.
**Refs.** §2, §9.1.
close_reason: Pinned the tested Elixir 1.20/Phoenix/Ash/Oban dependency matrix, added runtime version capture, and required runtime_versions in RunSpec and Evidence schemas/examples. Verified schema registry examples, Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (7 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.1.4 Telemetry/OTel span hierarchy + metric cardinality rules
description:
**What.** OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry skeleton + metric conventions.
**Why.** Traces are the most mature Elixir SDK signal; getting the span hierarchy
and metric cardinality rules right now avoids backfilling for Phase 3 scheduling
and Phase 6 cost observability.
**Scope.** span hierarchy (conveyor.run_slice → station.* → adapter.pi.session →
tool.command); trace_id propagation into LedgerEvent/StationRun/ToolInvocation/
artifacts; allowed metric dimensions only (project_id, station, adapter, profile,
status, failure_category, policy_profile, suite_kind); reject high-cardinality
labels.
**Acceptance.** Spans nest per §8.1; metrics reject disallowed dimensions.
**Refs.** §8.1.
close_reason: Implemented OpenTelemetry-compatible span hierarchy, trace context propagation helpers, allowed metric dimension validation, and guarded metric emission. Verified with Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (11 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.2 — Config + doctor + init
description:
# P0.2 — Config + doctor + init

**What.** `.conveyor/config.toml` schema and loader; `mix conveyor.init` to
scaffold `.conveyor/` (config, policies, starter AGENTS.md, artifact dirs); and
`mix conveyor.doctor` to verify all prerequisites with actionable findings.

**Why / background.** An OSS-friendly factory needs crisp operator commands.
Doctor must fail HARD and ACTIONABLY when required sandbox constraints, policy
profiles, or test commands are missing, and only WARN for missing optional
future adapters. Every blocking finding must carry at least one suggested
NextAction and the exact Mix command to rerun.

**Scope.** config schema + Conveyor.Config; init scaffolding; doctor checks
(versions, Postgres, Oban, Docker rootless, Pi image, provider credential scope,
CodeScent if gate-blocking, git, sample repo cleanliness, AGENTS.md lint, test
commands, policy profiles, artifact dir writable, no prod secrets); stable exit
codes 0–7.

**Acceptance.** Missing Docker/Pi/CodeScent/Postgres/test-commands/policies are
reported clearly with NextActions; exit codes match §9.

**Refs.** §9, §9.1, §25 P0.2. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P0.2 config/init/doctor work: child beads q19.3.1-q19.3.4 closed; focused Elixir 1.20 + Postgres verification passed (config/init/doctor task tests: 12 passed).

title: P0.2.1 .conveyor/config.toml schema + Config loader
description:
**What.** `.conveyor/config.toml` schema + `Conveyor.Config` runtime/project
loader.
**Why.** Single source for project command specs, policy profile selection,
quality adapter choice, and artifact dirs. Language-specifics live in config, not
in core (portability rule §2).
**Scope.** config.toml schema (project command_specs, policies dir, prompts dir,
quality adapter, autonomy default); Config loader + validation; sample config.
**Acceptance.** Loader parses + validates; missing required keys produce clear
errors.
**Refs.** §2, §6.4 (.conveyor layout), §9.
close_reason: Implemented .conveyor/config.toml schema loader using latest toml_elixir 3.1.0, typed project/command config structs, validation errors with precise paths, and a reusable sample config template. Verified Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (15 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.2.2 mix conveyor.init scaffolding
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.init SAMPLE_PROJECT_PATH`.
**Why.** Station 1 of the tracer (§22.4): scaffold the `.conveyor/` tree so a repo
is ready for the factory.
**Scope.** create .conveyor/ (config.toml, policies/{implement,verify}.toml,
prompts/{implementation-prompt@1,reviewer@1}.md, runs/, blobs/), starter
AGENTS.md (via P0.7 generator).
**Acceptance.** init creates the full .conveyor tree + starter AGENTS.md +
artifact dirs.
**Refs.** §6.4, §22.4 (1).
close_reason: Implemented mix conveyor.init to scaffold .conveyor/config.toml, policies, prompts, runs/blobs artifact directories, and starter AGENTS.md without overwriting existing files. Verified with Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (17 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.2.3 mix conveyor.doctor checks (§9.1)
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.doctor` with the full §9.1 check list.
**Why.** Doctor is the operator's first line of defense; it must fail hard when
required prerequisites are missing and only warn for optional future adapters.
**Scope.** checks: versions match matrix; Postgres; Oban; Docker reachable
(rootless preferred); Pi image; provider credential scope (live only); CodeScent
(only if gate-blocking); git; sample repo clean at base; AGENTS.md present +
lint-clean; test commands; policy profiles; artifact dir writable; no prod-looking
secrets mounted; report host sandbox capabilities.
**Acceptance.** Missing Docker/Postgres/test-commands/policies = failure; missing
optional adapter = warning.
**Refs.** §9.1, §12.3 (sandbox caps), §25 P0.2.
close_reason: Implemented mix conveyor.doctor and Conveyor.Doctor checks for versions, Oban, Postgres, Docker, git, AGENTS.md, verify command specs, policy profiles, artifact dirs, optional Pi/provider inputs, and secret posture. Verified missing Docker/Postgres/test commands/policies fail and optional adapter absence warns; Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (22 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer passed.

title: P0.2.4 NextAction findings + stable exit codes (0–7)
description:
**What.** Actionable NextAction findings + stable CLI exit codes across all
commands.
**Why.** Every blocking doctor/audit/gate finding must include at least one
suggested NextAction and the exact Mix command to rerun. Stable exit codes make
the CLI scriptable in CI.
**Scope.** NextAction struct (§6.3 findings.next_actions); exit codes 0–7 (§9);
shared finding rendering.
**Acceptance.** A failing doctor prints a NextAction + rerun command and exits
with the documented code.
**Refs.** §6.3, §9 (exit codes).
close_reason: Implemented shared NextAction and stable CLI exit code mapping, attached rerun actions to blocking doctor findings, and updated mix conveyor.doctor to halt with documented codes. Verified failing doctor output includes NextAction/rerun command and exits 7 for malformed config; Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (23 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer passed.

title: P0.3 — Ash domain & migrations
description:
# P0.3 — Ash domain & migrations

**What.** Define the active Ash/AshPostgres resources from §6.1 (and §8/§12.4
additions: StationEffect, CredentialLease), their migrations, embedded value
schemas (§6.3), database invariants/unique constraints (§6.5), and documented
schema specs for deferred resources (§6.2) WITHOUT creating their tables.

**Why / background.** One coherent Postgres source of truth where policies and
state transitions are enforceable. The schema must establish stable seams for
future decomposition, parallelism, policy, and learning without forcing those
features into the tracer bullet. Immutable fields (digests, base commits, locked
contracts, blob refs) must never update in place — corrections create new
records + ledger events.

**Scope (subtasks).** Project/Toolchain/Cache; Plan/Requirement/HumanDecision/
PlanAudit; Epic/Slice/DiffPolicy/ReviewPolicy; AgentBrief/ContractLock/TestPack/
VerificationSuite/TestPackCalibration; RunSpec + StationPlan; RunAttempt/
AgentSession/StationRun/StationEffect; PatchSet/RiskAssessment/Workspace; Evidence/
ToolInvocation/Review/GateResult; Artifact/RunBundle/ReviewerHealth/GateHealth;
ContextPack/InstructionSource/CodeQualityRun/RunPrompt; Policy/Retention/RunBudget/
Incident/CredentialLease/HumanApproval/ExternalChange/PatchEquivalence/LedgerEvent;
embedded schemas; DB invariants; deferred-resource specs.

**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; active resources CRUD through Ash; deferred
specs document future invariants without creating unused tables; unique
constraints from §6.5 are enforced at the DB layer.

**Refs.** §6, §6.1, §6.1.1, §6.2, §6.3, §6.5, §8 (StationEffect), §12.4
(CredentialLease), §25 P0.3. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Closed for the non-deferred scope: q19.4.1 through q19.4.13 are implemented and verified; the only remaining child q19.4.14 is labeled defer and is excluded per user instruction that existing deferred beads are out of scope for now. Latest full Elixir 1.20 gate during this pass passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 86 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.1 Project + ToolchainProfile + CacheMount
description:
**What.** Define Project, ToolchainProfile, CacheMount Ash resources + migrations.
**Why.** The project root identity, pinned toolchain (image digest, dependency
lock digest, cache policy, SBOM ref), and content-addressed cache mounts.
Reproducibility via digests, performance via SAFE read-only caches (not mutable
ambient state).
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; AshPostgres migrations; basic CRUD actions.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; resources CRUD through Ash.
**Refs.** §6.1, §12.4 (image/cache).
close_reason: Implemented Project, ToolchainProfile, and CacheMount Ash resources with migrations and CRUD tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused resource test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 27 tests.

title: P0.3.10 ContextPack + InstructionSource + CodeQualityRun + RunPrompt
description:
**What.** Define ContextPack, InstructionSource, CodeQualityRun, RunPrompt.
**Why.** ContextPack = scout output (cited relevant files/risks/tests).
InstructionSource = trust-labeled prompt inputs (system/project/plan/brief/
agents_md/repo_file/tool_output → trusted/bounded/untrusted). CodeQualityRun =
quality adapter result + new_high_risk_findings. RunPrompt = the versioned,
immutable assembled prompt.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; InstructionSource trust_level enum enforced.
**Refs.** §6.1, §5 (instruction hierarchy), §13, §14.
close_reason: Implemented ContextPack, InstructionSource, CodeQualityRun, and RunPrompt Ash resources with migrations, relationships, InstructionSource trust/source enum constraints, and CRUD/enum tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused prompt context test passed with 3 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 61 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.11 Policy + Retention + RunBudget + Incident + CredentialLease + HumanApproval + ExternalChange + PatchEquivalence + LedgerEvent
description:
**What.** Define Policy, RetentionPolicy, RunBudget, Incident, CredentialLease,
HumanApproval, ExternalChange, PatchEquivalence, LedgerEvent.
**Why.** The safety/economic/integration/audit spine. Policy (profiles +
allow/deny/env/network/budget/autonomy ceiling). RetentionPolicy (artifact
sensitivity retention). RunBudget (caps + consumed counters). Incident
(violations). CredentialLease (short-lived scoped provider secrets). HumanApproval
+ ExternalChange + PatchEquivalence (manual integration recording). LedgerEvent
(append-only audit + idempotency_key unique).
**Scope.** fields per §6.1/§8/§12.4; unique(LedgerEvent.idempotency_key).
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; LedgerEvent idempotency unique; CredentialLease
status lifecycle present.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.5, §8, §12.4, §21.
close_reason: Implemented Policy, RetentionPolicy, RunBudget, Incident, CredentialLease, HumanApproval, ExternalChange, PatchEquivalence, and LedgerEvent resources with migrations, lifecycle/decision enum constraints, LedgerEvent idempotency uniqueness, and focused CRUD/uniqueness tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused safety audit test passed with 4 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 65 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.12 DB invariants + unique constraints + immutable-field guards
description:
**What.** Enforce the §6.5 database invariants + immutable-field guards.
**Why.** Idempotency and evidence integrity deserve DATABASE backing, not just
Ash validations. Immutable fields (digests, base commits, locked contracts, blob
refs) must never update in place — corrections create new records + ledger events.
**Scope.** all unique constraints from §6.5 as DB constraints; check
constraints/triggers or Ash change guards preventing in-place mutation of
immutable fields; StationEffect uniqueness.
**Acceptance.** Attempting to mutate an immutable digest fails; duplicate
idempotency keys rejected at DB layer.
**Refs.** §6.5.
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (C4): add two explicit acceptance criteria -- (1) at most one active (planned/running) RunAttempt per slice enforced at the DB layer (section 6.5); (2) in-place mutation of an immutable field (digests, base commits, locked contracts, blob refs) is rejected; corrections create new records + ledger events.
close_reason: Implemented DB-backed immutable-field guards with a PostgreSQL trigger function across digest, base commit, blob, bundle, gate freshness, patch equivalence, and ledger identity fields. Verified unique constraints from §6.5 remain DB-backed, one-active RunAttempt is enforced, StationEffect/LedgerEvent idempotency is enforced, and immutable updates fail while mutable fields can still change. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused DB invariants test passed with 3 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 68 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.13 Embedded value schemas (AC / command_specs / findings / risk_rules)
description:
**What.** Implement the §6.3 typed embedded value schemas.
**Why.** Future concepts appear as typed embedded JSON inside active resources
before becoming first-class tables. These shared shapes are validated everywhere.
**Scope.** acceptance_criteria[] (id/text/kind/requirement_refs/required_test_refs/
evidence_status/evidence_refs); command_specs[] / ToolInvocation.command_spec;
findings[] (severity/category/message/next_actions); ReviewPolicy.risk_rules[].
**Acceptance.** Each embedded schema validates; invalid enum values rejected.
**Refs.** §6.3.
close_reason: Implemented shared embedded-schema validations for acceptance_criteria, command_specs/ToolInvocation.command_spec, findings, and ReviewPolicy risk_rules while preserving the existing JSON/map storage shape. Added focused enum rejection tests for each schema family and updated existing resource tests to use valid embedded values. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused embedded schema test passed with 5 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 73 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.14 Deferred-resource schema specs (docs/future-schemas/)
description:
**What.** Document deferred-resource schema specs under docs/future-schemas/
WITHOUT creating tables.
**Why.** Deferred resources should have schema sketches, invariants, and expected
event types so future phases have a clean seam — but no migrations until a phase
uses them (promotion rule §6.2).
**Scope.** WorkspacePool, TaskClaim, MergeQueueItem, BudgetLedger,
AgentReputation, Memory, ExternalTaskRef — schema sketch + invariants + event
types each.
**Acceptance.** Specs exist; no unused tables created.
**Refs.** §6.2.
close_reason: Documented deferred resource schema sketches in docs/future-schemas/README.md for WorkspacePool, TaskClaim, MergeQueueItem, BudgetLedger, AgentReputation, Memory, and ExternalTaskRef; structural verifier passed and no schema/table artifacts were created.

title: P0.3.2 Plan + Requirement + HumanDecision + PlanAudit
description:
**What.** Define Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, PlanAudit resources.
**Why.** Plan quality is a first-class gate. Requirements carry stable keys +
source spans + contract digests for traceability; HumanDecision records explicit
choices; PlanAudit records score/decision/findings/coverage.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; unique(plan_id, stable_key) for Requirement &
HumanDecision (§6.5); normalized_contract + contract_sha256 on Plan.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; unique keys enforced; CRUD works.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.5, §10.
close_reason: Implemented Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, and PlanAudit Ash resources with migrations, CRUD tests, and DB unique indexes for requirement/decision stable keys. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 31 tests.

title: P0.3.3 Epic + Slice + DiffPolicy + ReviewPolicy
description:
**What.** Define Epic, Slice, DiffPolicy, ReviewPolicy resources.
**Why.** The work graph (Epic→Slice) plus the policies that bound a Slice's diff
scope (allowed/protected globs, size caps, dependency/migration/public-API
allowances) and review requirements (risk_rules → required review kinds +
escalation).
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; Slice carries autonomy_level, source_refs,
likely_files, conflict_domains (swarm-readiness §27); unique(epic_id, position).
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; Slice records swarm-readiness fields; unique
position enforced.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.3 (risk_rules), §6.5, §27.
close_reason: Implemented Epic, Slice, DiffPolicy, and ReviewPolicy Ash resources with migrations, CRUD tests, Slice swarm-readiness fields, and unique(epic_id, position). Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 35 tests.

title: P0.3.4 AgentBrief + ContractLock + TestPack + VerificationSuite + TestPackCalibration
description:
**What.** Define AgentBrief, ContractLock, TestPack, VerificationSuite,
TestPackCalibration.
**Why.** The contract spine. AgentBrief is THE contract (ACs, required tests,
verification commands, locked). ContractLock freezes the digests the gate checks
against. TestPack is the locked, read-only acceptance test bundle.
VerificationSuite classifies suites (baseline_regression/acceptance_locked/...) +
expected_on_base/patch. TestPackCalibration records red/green calibration.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; unique(slice_id, version) for AgentBrief & TestPack;
all the *_sha256 lock fields on ContractLock.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; lock digests stored immutably.
