title: Rework Intelligence: retries that learn from their own failures
description:
## Why this epic exists (background)

Rework-on-fail (`AttemptLoop`, M2) is the factory's main autonomy lever: a failed slice retries within a budget instead of halting. But verified code reading (2026-07-02) shows retries fly nearly blind:

- `lib/conveyor/planning/rework_synthesizer.ex:33-41,99-110` builds a rich `prior_findings` map (per-finding message, stage, evidence_refs, path) — and then `lib/conveyor/planning/attempt_loop.ex:177` DISCARDS it. Only failed/green acceptance-criterion IDs reach the next brief (`rework_synthesizer.ex:69-82`).
- `PromptBuilder` has a fully built `prior_findings` seam that renders a "# Prior Trusted Findings" section (`lib/conveyor/planning/prompt_builder.ex:44,176,241-269`) — it is DEAD on the live path; the only caller (`lib/conveyor/stations/implementer.ex:96`) never passes it.
- The agent never sees: the gate stage findings, the failing test output, or its own previous diff. It gets "Failed acceptance criteria: <ids>" and a generic repair instruction.

So the retry budget is spent on attempts that don't know why the last attempt failed. This is the cheapest large autonomy win in the codebase: the seams exist; they are unwired.

## Goal

Each retry attempt receives precise, bounded, trusted failure context (findings, failing-test excerpts, prior-diff summary), and the loop detects non-convergence (same failure twice, empty diff) and parks early instead of burning attempts and tokens.

## ROADMAP alignment

This epic delivers the M6-remainder items "drift/no-progress detection" and "Convergence Sentinel (anti-thrash)" for the within-slice case, plus the feedback-quality half of making rework actually converge. Related: `dr1m.2` (mid-flight check), `a3hf.1.5` (run-health/drift signal in cockpit — the display side).

## Sequencing

1. Wire prior findings into the retry prompt (the dead seams).
2. Add failing-test excerpts + prior-diff summary to rework context (bounded, redacted).
3. Convergence sentinel: failure fingerprinting, same-fingerprint-twice → park; empty-diff → park.
4. Feedback escalation across attempts (verbosity ladder).
5. Tests & e2e with detailed logging.

## Exit

Induced-failure e2e shows: attempt 2's prompt contains the gate findings and failing-test excerpt from attempt 1; a deterministically-stuck slice parks after 2 identical failures with reason convergence_stall (not after exhausting the full budget); rework success rate becomes measurable per run (logged + in run summary).

title: Thread prior findings into the retry prompt (wire the dead seams)
description:
## Problem (verified)

- `attempt_loop.ex:177` calls `ReworkSynthesizer.synthesize/3` and ignores its return value, which contains the full `prior_findings` list (message, stage, evidence_refs, path per finding — built at `rework_synthesizer.ex:33-41,99-110`).
- `PromptBuilder.build!` accepts `prior_findings:` and renders a "# Prior Trusted Findings" section with a trusted instruction source (`prompt_builder.ex:44,176,241-269`) — no live caller passes it (`implementer.ex:96` passes only `context_pack:`).
- `RunSpecForge.forge_retry!` does not thread findings either.

## Change

Thread the synthesizer's findings through the retry path so the next attempt's prompt includes them: AttemptLoop keeps the synthesize result → the retry's RunSpec/implementer invocation carries `prior_findings` → PromptBuilder renders the section that already exists. Persist the findings payload on the retry attempt record (or brief) so the prompt is reproducible from durable state (crash-resume must rebuild the same prompt — the ledger discipline applies).

## Trust boundary note

Findings derive from the gate's own deterministic stages — they are TRUSTED repair input (the existing synthesizer language already says so). Keep them in the trusted section, distinct from untrusted repo excerpts, per ADR-07 instruction-authority rules.

## Acceptance

- Unit: retry prompt (rendered via PromptBuilder) contains the "# Prior Trusted Findings" section with the failed stage findings' messages + paths from the prior attempt.
- Unit: findings payload survives crash-resume (reconstruct retry prompt from durable state, compare digests).
- $0 e2e (fake adapter, induced gate failure): attempt 2 prompt digest differs from attempt 1 and contains the finding text; log line records finding count threaded.
- No regression: first attempts (no prior findings) render unchanged (golden-file diff).
close_reason: Thread synthesizer prior_findings through AttemptLoop->forge_retry! (implement-station durable input)->Implementer->PromptBuilder; retry prompt now renders # Prior Trusted Findings, survives crash-resume via persisted RunSpec, logs threaded count

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: CROSS-SEAM (plan-space review 2026-07-02): design the trusted repair-input section to accept OPERATOR NOTES as a second source alongside gate findings. The terminal-triage epic's rework disposition (terminal-triage-uevc.2) threads a human note into the next attempt; it must render in the same trusted section with an operator-attributed banner, not get invented ad hoc there. Deliverable here: the section renderer takes a list of {source: gate|operator, payload} entries; triage only supplies entries.

title: Rework context: failing-test excerpts + prior-diff summary, bounded and redacted
description:
## What

Beyond gate findings (sibling bead), give the retry attempt the two artifacts a human would demand before re-attempting:

1. **Failing-test excerpt**: the relevant slice of verify/TestExecution output (failed test ids + assertion messages + short tail of stdout/stderr), parsed from the structured test results the VerificationRerunner/ToolchainRunner already produce. NOT the whole log — a bounded excerpt (size cap, e.g. 4-8KB) selected by failure relevance.
2. **Prior-diff summary**: what the failed attempt actually changed — file list + per-file change stats, plus (budget permitting) the hunks touching files named in findings. The agent otherwise re-derives its own previous work from a cold workspace... or worse, repeats it.

## Constraints

- All excerpts pass through `security/redactor.ex` before entering a prompt (secrets discipline).
- Hard token/byte budget per section with deterministic truncation (head+tail with marker) so prompts stay stable and replayable; budgets declared in one place (config), logged when truncation fires.
- Content ordering deterministic (sorted by test id / path) — prompt digests must be stable for cassette replay.
- These live in the trusted findings section (derived from our own gate/toolchain, not repo prose).

## Acceptance

- Unit: excerpt selection picks the failing tests' blocks from a fixture junit/stdout corpus; truncation is deterministic; redactor applied (fixture with a planted secret never reaches the prompt).
- $0 e2e: induced test failure → retry prompt contains the failing assert message + the prior attempt's changed-file list.
- Prompt-size guard test: pathological huge failure output stays within budget.
- Logging: per-retry log line with excerpt sizes, truncation flags.
close_reason: New Conveyor.Recovery.ReworkContext gives a retry the two artifacts a human would demand: (1) failing-test excerpt — failed test ids + assertion messages + short output tail, parsed from the structured verification_result (suites->commands->attempts->tests, status=failed), sorted by id; (2) prior-diff summary — sorted changed-file list (+per-file stats when present). Both pass through security/Redactor BEFORE entering a prompt, are deterministically truncated to a config byte budget (head+tail+marker, UTF-8-safe), and log sizes/truncation. Wired into ReworkSynthesizer: prior_findings gains failing_test_excerpt + prior_diff_summary and the retry AgentBrief.desired_behavior appends them; AttemptLoop threads slice_result.output through prepare_retry!. Budgets declared once in config/config.exs. 9 tests: excerpt selection from fixture corpus, deterministic truncation, planted-secret redaction, prompt-size guard (500KB stays <=6144B, valid UTF-8), diff summary+stats, build; + AttemptLoop e2e (induced failure -> retry brief contains the failing assert message + prior changed-file list).

title: Convergence sentinel: failure fingerprinting, same-failure-twice parks, empty-diff parks
description:
## What

Detect non-convergence INSIDE the attempt loop and stop early, instead of burning the full attempt budget (and tokens) on a stuck slice:

1. **Failure fingerprint**: a stable digest of (failed stage ids, finding codes+paths, failed acceptance-criterion ids, failed test ids). Computed per failed attempt, persisted on the attempt record/ledger.
2. **Same-fingerprint-twice → park** with reason `convergence_stall` (the retry produced the identical failure — more attempts are near-certain waste).
3. **Empty/no-op diff → park immediately** with reason `no_progress` (the agent changed nothing; retrying the same prompt is thrash).
4. (Cheap extra) **Regression direction**: if attempt N fails MORE criteria than N-1, note `regressing` in the park evidence when budget exhausts.

## Why

ROADMAP M6 remainder names exactly this: "drift/no-progress detection; Convergence Sentinel (anti-thrash)". Unattended overnight runs die of thrash: a stuck slice consuming its full budget per slice multiplies into hours + real dollars. Early, evidenced parking is honest abstention — the project's core posture.

## Design notes

- Fingerprint must be insensitive to noise (timestamps, tmp paths, durations) — normalize before hashing. Reuse finding codes rather than raw messages where possible.
- Park reasons are typed strings that will fold into the park-reason taxonomy (a3hf.1.3.1) — coordinate the enum values with that bead rather than inventing conflicting names.
- The sentinel decision + both fingerprints go into the park evidence so the operator (and later the digest/triage surfaces) can see WHY the loop gave up.
- Emit a ledger event per sentinel decision (event-sourced discipline; resume must not double-count).

## Acceptance

- Unit: fingerprint stability (same failure → same digest across runs; timestamp/path noise ignored); difference sensitivity (different failing test → different digest).
- $0 e2e (fake adapter scripted to repeat the same failure): parks after attempt 2 with `convergence_stall`, budget NOT exhausted, both fingerprints in evidence; scripted empty-diff attempt parks with `no_progress` after attempt 1.
- Resume test: sentinel state reconstructs from the ledger.
- Logging: sentinel decisions logged with fingerprints + reason; run summary counts sentinel parks distinctly.
close_reason: Convergence sentinel lands in AttemptLoop. New pure Conveyor.Recovery.FailureFingerprint (stable sha256 over gate status + finding identity keys category/code/path/acceptance_criterion_id/stage/test_id; blind to message/severity/duration/timestamps/tmp) and Conveyor.Recovery.ConvergenceSentinel.decide (empty-diff -> no_progress, same-fingerprint-twice -> convergence_stall, else continue). Wired into the attempt loop BEFORE the retry branch: parks early (status :convergence_parked, report sentinel_park reason) instead of burning budget, emits ledger event attempt.convergence_parked carrying both fingerprints + reason (resume reconstructs from ledger), logs the decision. Existing budget-exhaustion path preserved for NON-convergent repeated failures (test updated to distinct findings/attempt). 15 tests: fingerprint stability/noise-insensitivity/difference (5), sentinel decision (5), e2e convergence_stall parks after attempt 2 (budget not exhausted, both fingerprints in ledger), e2e no_progress parks after attempt 1, + existing loop tests green. NOTE: regression-direction 'regressing' flag (marked cheap-extra, budget-exhaustion path) deferred.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: DEP ADDED (plan-space review 2026-07-02): now blocks on rt6k.1 (findings threading). Rationale: park-after-identical-failure-twice assumes the retry was INFORMED (attempt 2 knew why attempt 1 failed). With today's blind retries, identical failure is common even where a third stochastic attempt might succeed — the sentinel would over-park and reduce autonomy. Sequencing the sentinel after findings-threading makes its premise true. Also: the identical-fingerprint threshold stays configurable (default 2), and fingerprints of attempts that received no feedback (edge cases) should not count toward the stall threshold.

title: Feedback escalation ladder across retry attempts
description:
## What

Vary the rework strategy as attempts accumulate, instead of sending the same shape of prompt N times:

- Attempt 2: findings + failing-test excerpt (the baseline from the sibling beads).
- Attempt 3+: add the prior-diff hunks for finding-adjacent files, an explicit "your previous approach failed twice; reconsider the approach, do not repeat it" instruction, and (optionally, config-gated) a fuller context pack (more scout files).

Keep it deterministic and declared: the ladder is data (per-attempt feedback profile), not scattered conditionals — so cassettes replay and the design-laws tests can assert the ladder shape.

## Explicitly out of scope (for now)

Model escalation (sonnet→opus) and within-slice speculative racing (ADR-25 / dr1m.3) — both are levers for later; racing has its own bead chain. This bead is only about WHAT the retry prompt contains per rung.

## Why P2 (not P1)

The big win is B1/B2 (any feedback at all). The ladder is a refinement on top — valuable for hard slices, but measurable only once baseline feedback exists.

## Acceptance

- Ladder profile is config/data-driven with a documented default; per-attempt prompt content matches the declared rung (golden-file tests per rung).
- $0 e2e: 3-attempt scripted failure shows escalating prompt content; logs record the rung used per attempt.

title: Tests & e2e for rework intelligence: induced-failure suite with detailed logging
description:
## What

The epic's consolidated verification surface (unit pieces live with their beads; this bead owns the cross-cutting e2e suite + observability):

1. **Induced-failure matrix ($0, fake adapter, full SerialDriver)**: (a) fail-then-fixed → accepted on attempt 2 with findings-bearing prompt; (b) identical-failure-twice → convergence_stall park; (c) empty-diff → no_progress park; (d) budget exhaustion with regressing note; (e) crash mid-rework → resume rebuilds the same retry prompt (digest equality).
2. **Rework metrics in the run summary**: attempts per slice, rework success rate, sentinel park counts — asserted in e2e JSON output and visible in `conveyor.run_view`.
3. **Cassette**: one recorded real-agent rework cycle (live_agent-tagged to record; committed cassette replays in CI) proving the feedback actually changes a real model's behavior at least once (qualitative bank, wiring proof).

## Logging requirements

- Per attempt: attempt number, feedback rung, finding count, excerpt bytes, truncations.
- Per sentinel decision: fingerprints + reason.
- Per rework outcome: attempts used vs budget, final disposition.
- E2E asserts on captured logs (CaptureLog) so observability can't silently rot.

## Acceptance

- Matrix green at $0 in default CI; cassette test committed; run-summary metrics documented in the run_report schema notes.

title: Negotiated Scope: stop parking correct work (gate-friction program)
description:
## Why this epic exists (background — all empirical, from dogfood runs 3-4, 2026-07)

The gate currently parks CORRECT work, which destroys both unattended throughput and operator trust (waking up to parked-but-fine slices teaches you to ignore parks — the worst outcome for a trust system):

- Bead `8mnx` (dogfood run 3): agent implemented SLICE-001 correctly, pytest green in-container, but diff_scope emitted out_of_scope_path because the agent edited `cronparse/__init__.py` to export the new error type per the "stable public API" rule — that file wasn't in `likely_files`. Authors must enumerate every touched file exhaustively (brittle), and the agent has no in-loop way to justify a sensible scope expansion.
- Bead `dxgw` (dogfood run 4): 4 files changed tripped max_files_changed (4>3) alongside test-authorship findings. The per-slice locked-test materialization (PR #40) addressed the test-authorship half (Option C); the scope-rigidity half remains.

## The design tension (do not resolve it by weakening the gate)

diff_scope is load-bearing: it is what stops scope-smuggling and protects locked tests. The fix is NOT looser defaults. The fix is a **deterministic negotiation protocol**: the agent may REQUEST a scope amendment with a justification; deterministic policy decides (auto-grant within declared bounds, otherwise park with a precise, actionable reason). Authority stays with policy, never with the agent (CLAUDE.md: untrusted output never overrides policy). ADR-26 (autonomous plan amendment, `dr1m.4`) is exactly this machinery and is already wired into attempt_loop; its classifier bug `dr1m.4.1` blocks activation.

## Components

1. Always-allowed scope classes (deterministic, no negotiation needed): package-export barrels (__init__.py, index.ts, lib.rs, mod.rs), formatter-touched trivia — declared in DiffPolicy profiles, off by default for protected paths.
2. Structured scope-amendment request → deterministic evaluator → grant produces a NEW contract lock version (ADR-20: evolution is always a new lock, never mutation) → rework attempt runs under the amended scope; deny → park with reason scope_denied + the exact denied paths.
3. max_files_changed realism: derive per-slice cap from the contract's own scope size + margin instead of a global constant; lint at authoring time.
4. Tests & e2e reproducing the 8mnx/dxgw shapes.

## Exit

Re-run the 8mnx shape: slice completes autonomously via auto-grant or amendment (no human), with the grant recorded in evidence + a new lock version. Re-run a genuinely-smuggling diff: still parked, reason names the exact out-of-policy paths. False-park rate on the dogfood corpus drops; gate strictness on true violations unchanged (gauntlet still zero false-pass).

title: Always-allowed scope classes in DiffPolicy (package barrels & co.)
description:
## What

A DiffPolicy profile extension: a declared list of path CLASSES that are always in scope for any slice, because touching them is the normal mechanical consequence of in-scope work — starting with package export barrels (`__init__.py`, `index.ts`/`index.js`, `lib.rs`/`mod.rs`), configurable per project profile.

## Why

8mnx is the proof: exporting a new public symbol from `__init__.py` is REQUIRED by the target repo's own conventions, yet structurally unpredictable at authoring time (you don't know which barrel until the code exists). Enumerating them per-slice in likely_files is brittle busywork; a policy-level class is deterministic, auditable, and narrow.

## Constraints

- Class membership is path-pattern based and evaluated deterministically by diff_scope; NO content-based judgment here (that's the amendment path's job).
- Locked test packs and protected paths are NEVER always-allowed — classes must not be able to whitelist tests/** or contract surfaces (add an explicit precedence test: protected beats allowed).
- Off-by-default for anything not in the shipped conservative set; project profile can extend. Document in the diff-policy schema doc.
- The grant still shows up in gate evidence (path X allowed via class Y) — silent allowance breeds mystery diffs.

## Acceptance

- Unit: 8mnx fixture (barrel edit not in likely_files) passes diff_scope with an evidence note; same edit on tests/** still fails (precedence).
- Policy-profile round-trip: class list declared in profile TOML → active in the stage (coordinate with bead a7kf: profiles must actually load — that fix is in the Never-Lie epic).
- MutantGauntlet: a scope-smuggle mutant hiding real logic inside a barrel file is still caught (content isn't judged, but the gauntlet must show the class doesn't become a smuggling hole — e.g. cap allowed-class diff size, or add a size guard finding).
close_reason: DiffScope enforces always-allowed scope classes: shipped package_barrels + DiffPolicy.always_allowed_path_classes field (migration + DB round-trip); class match suppresses out_of_scope_path with an info always_allowed_path evidence note; protected paths never granted (precedence); grants not exempt from size caps (smuggle guard). 8mnx/precedence/gauntlet/round-trip tests green; doc in decomposition-aid.md. --force: nyrl.1's own scope is complete; the dot-child nyrl.1.1 tracks the separate profile-TOML config-surface follow-up.

title: Scope-amendment protocol: agent requests, deterministic policy decides, grant = new lock version
description:
## What

The in-loop negotiation path for scope, built on the already-wired ADR-26 amendment machinery (`dr1m.4`):

1. When diff_scope fails with out_of_scope_path (and only that class of failure), classify amendability: the offending paths, the agent's stated rationale (a structured field the prompt protocol asks for when it knowingly expands scope — plus a fallback inference from the diff itself), and the policy bounds.
2. A deterministic evaluator grants or denies: grants only within declared bounds (max N extra files, never protected paths / locked tests / policy files, path-pattern allowlist per profile). A grant produces a NEW contract lock version with the expanded likely_files (ADR-20: contract evolution is always a new lock+spec, never in-place mutation) and routes back into the rework attempt under the amended contract.
3. Deny → park with reason `scope_denied`, evidence listing exactly which paths and which bound failed.

## Why

This is the difference between "the gate is a wall" and "the gate is a contract negotiator with authority." dxgw/8mnx show correct work being parked; the ADR-26 machinery exists precisely for verification-failure-driven amendment and is already wired into attempt_loop (verified: its 'follow-up: call route/2' comment is stale — routing fires on budget exhaustion today). What's missing is the scope-specific amendment type + the classifier fix (`dr1m.4.1`, hard dependency).

## Authority invariants (test these explicitly)

- The agent can REQUEST, never grant. No prompt content can move a path from protected to allowed (untrusted-input rule).
- Every grant is a new lock version, digest-chained to its predecessor, visible in evidence + run_view (auditable trail of what the contract became overnight and why).
- Amendment count per slice is budgeted (e.g. 1-2), counted against the attempt budget — no infinite negotiate-loops (anti-thrash; coordinate with the convergence sentinel bead).

## Acceptance

- $0 e2e (fake adapter scripted to expand scope sensibly): out_of_scope failure → amendment requested → granted → new lock version → rework attempt passes → run summary shows the amendment trail.
- $0 e2e (scripted to touch tests/**): amendment DENIED, park reason scope_denied names the path + the violated bound.
- Unit: evaluator bound matrix (extra-file cap, protected precedence, pattern allowlist); lock-version chaining digests.
- Design-laws test: no code path mutates an existing ContractLock in place.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: CORRECTION (plan-space review 2026-07-02): the body calls dr1m.4.1 a missing 'classifier fix (hard dependency)' — STALE. dr1m.4.1 (AmendmentRouter mislabels subjects) was CLOSED 2026-06-22; the dep edge is satisfied and this bead is ready NOW. Remaining work is exactly: (a) the scope_expansion amendment type + its deterministic evaluator, (b) the request-artifact mechanism, (c) new-lock-version plumbing. Implementation detail decided in review: if the agent signals via a request file (e.g. .conveyor/amendment_request.json in the workspace), that path must be always-allowed in diff_scope AND stripped from the applied PatchSet before commit — the request is protocol, not product. The post-hoc inference fallback (classify an out_of_scope failure as amendable from the diff itself) should land first since it requires no prompt-protocol change; the explicit request field is the v2 refinement.

title: Per-slice max_files_changed derived from contract scope (kill the global 3)
description:
## What

Replace the flat max_files_changed constant with a per-slice cap derived from the slice's own declared scope: |likely_files| + always-allowed classes headroom + a small margin (values declared in the DiffPolicy profile, not hardcoded). Lint at authoring/plan-prepare time: a slice whose declared scope already exceeds its cap is an authoring error surfaced BEFORE the run (plan-lint), not a 2am park.

## Why

dxgw (dogfood run 4): 4 files changed vs global cap 3 — where 4 was the honest size of the slice (impl + errors + barrel + test materialization). A flat cap punishes correctly-sized slices and does nothing to catch a bloated slice that was authored bloated. Deriving from the contract makes the cap mean something: "you changed more than you declared," which is the actual smell.

## Acceptance

- Unit: cap derivation matrix (small slice, barrel-heavy slice, materialized-locked-tests slice — coordinate with the PR #40 materialization paths so those never count against the agent).
- dxgw fixture shape passes at its honest size; a diff exceeding derived cap still fails with the derived number in the finding message (actionable, not mysterious).
- Plan-lint warning when declared scope exceeds profile bounds (ties into a3hf.2.2.1 plan-lint engine — related link).
close_reason: Per-slice max_files_changed now derived from declared scope, not a flat cap. Added Conveyor.Planning.ScopeCap (config-driven: declared count + always_allowed_headroom + scope_margin; max_declared_files bound). RunSpecAssembler.create_default_diff_policy! uses ScopeCap.max_files_changed(|allowed_path_globs|) so barrel/always-allowed edits (nyrl.1) get headroom and the dxgw honest-4-files shape passes. diff_scope already emits the derived number in the exceed message. PlanWarnings gained a scope_exceeds_bound warning (nyrl.3 tie-in to a3hf.2.2.1). Locked-test materialization stays excluded from the base (already stripped from allowed + committed to base per PR #40). Tests: ScopeCap matrix, dxgw-shape, over-bound warning; updated 2 assembler tests to the derived cap.

title: Tests & e2e: false-park regression corpus from the dogfood findings
description:
## What

A committed regression corpus that encodes the empirical false-park shapes so they can never return, plus the epic's cross-cutting e2e:

1. **8mnx shape**: correct impl + barrel export not in likely_files → completes autonomously (class-allow or amendment), evidence shows the mechanism.
2. **dxgw shape**: honest 4-file slice with materialized locked tests → passes at derived cap.
3. **True-smuggle controls** (must STILL park/fail): tests/** edit, policy-file edit, unrelated-file logic change, oversized-diff-in-allowed-barrel.
4. Full-run $0 e2e over a small plan containing one of each → run summary dispositions exactly as designed; park reasons precise.

## Measurement hook

Track false-park rate as a first-class eval number: the corpus is the denominator; scorecard reports it next to the gate's zero-false-pass number. The pair (false-pass, false-park) is the honest two-sided health of the gate — optimizing one silently degrading the other is the failure mode this guards.

## Logging

Every scope decision (class-allow, amendment grant/deny, cap evaluation) logs path(s), rule id, bound values; e2e asserts the log lines exist.

## Acceptance

- Corpus + e2e green at $0 in default CI; scorecard shows both gate-health numbers; docs/schemas diff-policy page updated.

title: Never-Lie Sweep: every operator-facing surface tells the truth
description:
## Why this epic exists

A trust system that lies in small ways cannot be trusted in big ways. Dogfood runs surfaced a cluster of "the factory says X, reality is Y" defects — individually small, collectively corrosive, and all cheap to fix:

- `22r6`: `mix conveyor.run` exited 0 while ZERO slices were accepted and a Verify station crashed. Exit 0 is the single most load-bearing bit the factory emits (scripts, cron, CI all key on it).
- `uh2g`: AGENTS.md is registered as an instruction source, doctor-checked, and hashed into the contract lock — but its BODY never reaches the agent prompt ("No AGENTS.md excerpt was provided", default at prompt_builder.ex:45-46; the sole build! caller implementer.ex:96 never passes it). The operator reasonably believes their project conventions are in force. They are not.
- `a7kf`: `.conveyor/policies/*.toml` scaffolded by conveyor.init is NEVER loaded on a production run (Profiles.load_dir! is test-only). Config that looks operative and is inert is a lie with a file extension.
- README links to `docs/dogfood/decomposition-aid.md` and `gap-log-template.md` which do not exist.
- The bead board itself overstates open work: DOGFOOD-READINESS identifies false-open P1s (p639 watchdog — implemented; 90v0 skip-and-continue — implemented) and stale in-progress annotations (dr1m.4's 'follow-up' comment is stale).

## The organizing invariant

"Every surface the operator can read — exit codes, prompts (as contracted), config files, docs, the issue board — reflects reality, or is explicitly labeled advisory." Each task closes one class AND adds a regression guard so the class stays dead.

## Membership

The three empirical bugs (22r6, uh2g, a7kf) are adopted as children of this epic (parent-child links) rather than duplicated. New tasks: the config-surface audit (generalizes a7kf), docs truth (missing dogfood docs), and board reconciliation.

## Exit

All children closed; the never-lie invariant tests are in CI; the board matches code reality with evidence cited on every closure.
notes: Board-state note (mmxr.3, 2026-07-02): reconciled 6 divergences — p639 & dxgw confirmed already-closed (timeout watchdog + Option C materialization landed); 90v0 (skip-and-continue) and dr1m.4 (ADR-26 routing; child dr1m.4.1 fixed) closed with file:line evidence; a48z containment epic closed (children k55f/bjp2/lepz done, PRs #39/#40); xs30/vp73/9z4r annotated as exit-evidence-only so br ready stops overstating undone work.

title: Config-surface truth audit: every knob is load-bearing or labeled advisory
description:
## What

a7kf (policies TOML inert) is one instance of a class. Kill the class:

1. Enumerate every operator-editable configuration surface: `.conveyor/config.toml`, `.conveyor/policies/*.toml`, `.conveyor/prompts/*`, env vars read in config/runtime.exs, per-plan/per-slice knobs in the CLI.
2. For each: trace whether a production `mix conveyor.run` actually consumes it. Three allowed outcomes: (a) it is load-bearing (add a test that proves it), (b) it becomes load-bearing (wire it — file a follow-up bead if non-trivial), (c) it is removed or its file/docs gain an explicit ADVISORY banner.
3. Add a lightweight guard for the future: scaffolded-config integration test that flips each shipped knob and asserts an observable behavior change (or the advisory banner exists). New knobs without coverage fail review by convention (note in CONTRIBUTING/AGENTS source).

## Why

Inert config is the most corrosive small lie: the operator changes a policy file before an unattended overnight run and sleeps on a false belief. For a factory whose product IS trust, config honesty is not cosmetic.

## Acceptance

- A committed audit table (docs/ or the bead comments) listing every surface + verdict + evidence file:line.
- Each (a) surface has a consuming test; each (c) surface is gone or bannered.
- The flip-test guard runs in CI at $0.

title: Docs truth: create the missing dogfood docs; kill broken README links
description:
## What

README.md links `docs/dogfood/decomposition-aid.md` and `docs/dogfood/gap-log-template.md`; the docs/dogfood/ directory does not exist (verified 2026-07-02). Getting-started references them too.

1. Write `docs/dogfood/decomposition-aid.md`: the practical guide for breaking a real plan into Conveyor slices — sizing rules (what the gate's caps mean for slice size, per the Negotiated Scope epic), likely_files discipline + always-allowed classes, acceptance-command patterns, locked-test materialization (Option C, PR #40) explained operator-side, common false-park causes and how to author around them. Ground every rule in the empirical findings (8mnx, dxgw) — this doc is where those lessons compound.
2. Write `docs/dogfood/gap-log-template.md`: the structured template for recording per-run findings (run id, slice, symptom, layer, disposition, bead filed?) — the manual precursor of the auto-gap-log (Learning Flywheel epic; keep field names aligned).
3. Add a docs link-check (markdown link lint over README + docs/) to CI or a mix alias so dead intra-repo links fail fast.

## Acceptance

- Both docs exist with the content above; README links resolve; link-check green and wired into CI.
close_reason: Wrote docs/dogfood/{decomposition-aid,gap-log-template,task-graph-authoring}.md (grounded in 8mnx/dxgw); README + getting-started links now resolve; added test/docs_links_test.exs link-check that runs in mix test (CI), failing fast on dead intra-repo links.

title: Board reconciliation: verify-then-close false-open beads; fix stale annotations
description:
## What

The issue board is an operator-facing trust surface too. DOGFOOD-READINESS (Section 4) documents specific divergences; verify each in code, then act — per project policy, verify-then-close with evidence, never bulk-close:

1. `p639` (agent-call watchdog): premise "codex System.cmd has NO timeout" is now false — bounded timeout + brutal_kill + park-on-timeout at codex.ex:188-222, plus M6 slice/run reapers. Verify (incl. the claude_code adapter's timeout via adapter_base), then close with file:line evidence. NOTE: p639 was already annotated closed in a recent commit (826fc99 mentions 'close p639') — confirm its status and reconcile whichever record is stale.
2. `90v0` (skip-and-continue): wiring implemented (serial_driver.ex:207,217, proven live on the gx plan). Decide where its N-run exit-evidence requirement lives (fold into `9z4r` M3 epic — add a note there), then close.
3. `dr1m.4`: the "follow-up: call route/2" comment is stale (routing IS wired, fires on rework-budget exhaustion). Update the bead body; the bead stays open only for the dr1m.4.1 classifier bug.
4. `dxgw`: PR #40 landed per-slice locked-test materialization (Option C — the bead's own recommended resolution). Verify materialization end-to-end on a fixture, then close dxgw citing the PR + test, or narrow it to what remains.
5. `a48z` (Claude Code containment epic): PRs #39/#40 shipped the containment. Verify children/exit criteria; close or narrow.
6. `xs30`/`vp73` (M1/M2 epics): wiring landed per ROADMAP; they stay open for exit-evidence only — retitle/annotate so `br ready` output stops overstating undone work (evidence beads live in the Banked Proof work under 9z4r/bd50/gexs).

## Why

`bv --robot-triage` and `br ready` are the daily entry points; if they surface done work as open P1s, triage is noise and the operator learns to distrust the tracker — same disease as exit-code lies, different organ.

## Acceptance

- Each item resolved with cited evidence in the bead's closing comment; `br dep cycles --json` empty; `br sync --flush-only` run; a short board-state note added to the epic.
close_reason: Reconciled all 6 DOGFOOD-READINESS §4 divergences with cited evidence: p639/dxgw confirmed closed; 90v0/dr1m.4 closed (skip-and-continue + ADR-26 routing verified in serial_driver/attempt_loop); a48z closed (children done); xs30/vp73/9z4r annotated exit-evidence-only. Board no longer surfaces done work as open.

title: M3 exit evidence: N consecutive unattended live runs on a 3-8 slice plan with induced failure
description:
## What

Bank the M3 exit bar itself (ROADMAP §5 M3: "a real 3-8 slice plan completes fully unattended with live agent, surviving an induced slice failure, N consecutive runs"). Wiring is DONE (skip-and-continue proven live on the gx branching plan); this bead is the evidence, which is currently nowhere recorded.

## Protocol (pre-registered, so the evidence is honest)

- Target: a shipped sample plan (gx branching plan preferred — it exercises skip/dependents) + the live contained claude_code adapter.
- N = 3 consecutive runs (provisional per ROADMAP's own convention; record the number chosen and why).
- One run includes an induced failure (a slice whose acceptance deliberately fails once) — the run must park/skip correctly and complete independents; rework may recover it.
- Record per run: run id, dispositions per slice, attempts used, sentinel/park reasons, wall-clock, token spend. Store under eval/ or docs/dogfood/ as committed artifacts; file every gap found as a bead (gap-log discipline).

## Relationship

- Depends on bd50 (first banked run establishes the recording path).
- Subsumes the exit-evidence residue that kept 90v0 open (skip-and-continue wiring bead — see the Never-Lie board-reconciliation task).
- This is deliberately NOT a CI test (live spend); the CI shield is gexs's cassette. This is the run-ledger proof that the autonomy claim is real.

## Acceptance

- 3 consecutive green (or honestly-dispositioned) unattended runs recorded + committed; a short results note appended to ROADMAP or M4-PROGRESS-style doc; gaps filed.

title: Prose-to-Runnable-Plan pipeline: finish M5 (author, decompose, import, approve in one flow)
description:
## Why this epic exists

The single biggest ergonomic wall between "I have a plan" and "the factory is running" (DOGFOOD-READINESS Ring 1):

- `mix conveyor.author` produces ONLY a JSON draft file — it creates no DB rows and cannot hand off to run (verified: conveyor.author.ex:10-12 says so by design; author.ex:49-60 writes one file). The missing piece is the content-aware Decomposer (M5's open front per ROADMAP).
- A 20-slice plan then needs ~40 CLI invocations (per-slice lock + approve) before `mix conveyor.run` accepts it. DOGFOOD-READINESS: "A small approve-plan command would be worth more to dogfooding than any cockpit panel."

## Goal

`prose intent → draft → decomposed slices+deps → plan-lint → import to DB → bulk lock+approve (with one human confirmation) → runnable` as one coherent flow, each step separately invocable, deterministic where possible, agent-powered only where content understanding is required (decomposition, interrogation).

## ROADMAP alignment

This IS the M5 remainder ("in-factory prose→graph auto-decomposition; Interrogator-on-ambiguity"). It is also a precondition of the §4 exit bar ("mix conveyor.author 'intent' → computed plan → unattended run"). Related beads adopted/linked: a3hf.5.3 (Interrogator), a3hf.2.2.x (plan preview/dry-run — the safety gate this flow feeds), a3hf.5.1 (contract-authoring assistant — the conversational refinement layer, later).

## Sequencing

1. Bulk approve (pure ergonomics, no AI, ships alone, immediate dogfood relief).
2. author → import handoff (mechanical: draft JSON → PlanImporter).
3. Content-aware Decomposer v1 (the real M5 core).
4. Interrogator wiring (a3hf.5.3) at authoring time.
5. Tests & e2e.

## Guardrails

Decomposition quality is R4 in ROADMAP's risk list — the load-bearing artifact for the whole dream. The human confirmation step (approve) stays mandatory in this epic; removing it is a later trust decision measured by gate outcomes, not assumed.

## Exit

From a real prose intent: one command chain produces an approved runnable plan; slices carry computed deps (TaskDependency edges), per-slice acceptance; plan-lint passes; `mix conveyor.run` starts it. The 8mnx/dxgw authoring lessons (likely_files discipline, honest caps) are enforced at authoring time, not discovered at 2am.

title: mix conveyor.plan.approve: bulk lock+approve behind a plan-lint gate
description:
## What

One command: `mix conveyor.plan.approve PLAN_ID [--yes]` — runs plan-lint/audits first, prints a compact per-slice summary (key, title, likely_files count, acceptance count, derived caps), asks one confirmation (or --yes), then locks + approves every drafted slice in dependency order. Per-slice lock/approve stay available for surgical use.

## Why

~40 CLI calls for a 20-slice plan is the top dogfood friction (DOGFOOD-READINESS §3 Ring 1 + §6). This is deliberately the FIRST slice of the epic: pure ergonomics, no AI, no schema changes, ships alone.

## Design notes

- Refuse (with the lint findings) if any slice fails plan-lint — bulk-approve must not become bulk-rubber-stamp; the confirmation summary IS the operator's pre-flight read.
- Idempotent: re-running approves only still-drafted slices; already-approved pass through silently.
- JSON output + stable exit codes per CLI conventions (cli/exit_codes.ex); human summary to stderr per the pure-JSON-stdout rule.

## Acceptance

- E2E: sample 7-slice plan drafted → one approve command → conveyor.run accepts it; lint-failing plan is refused with findings on stdout JSON.
- Idempotency + partial-state tests; docs updated (getting-started loses the per-slice loop).
close_reason: Added mix conveyor.plan.approve PLAN_ID [--yes]: bulk lock+approve behind a plan-lint gate. Core logic in Conveyor.Planning.PlanApprove (preview/1 read-only pre-flight + lint gate; approve_all!/1 locks+approves drafted slices in position order, idempotent + partial-state safe). Lint gates the AUTHORED plan (status :draft); once compiled the gate already passed. Lint-failing plan refused with findings as JSON on stdout, exit 2 (never rubber-stamps). 8 tests (6 module + 2 CLI via exit_fun seam). Docs updated (task-graph-authoring.md). Full conveyor.run e2e round-trip belongs to the aaun.4 test/e2e bead.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: OVERLAP COORDINATION (plan-space review 2026-07-02): this bead delivers the CLI half of what a3hf.2.2.3 calls the approve-to-run gate (lint-gated confirmation before spend). a3hf.2.2.3 keeps: conveyor.preview as a read-only dry-run view, the cockpit planned-DAG rendering, and (with a3hf.2.2.2) the cost estimate shown at approval time. When a3hf.2.2.2 lands, surface its estimate in this command's confirmation summary. Do not double-build the approval gate.

title: conveyor.author --import: draft-to-DB handoff via PlanImporter
description:
## What

Close the mechanical gap between author and the DB: `mix conveyor.author "intent" --import` takes the audited draft and, when it is schema-valid + decomposed (initially: because the Decomposer sibling landed, or the operator hand-finished the JSON), calls the existing PlanImporter (conveyor.plan.import.ex:36-46 already writes Project+Plan+Epic+Slices+deps) and prints the created IDs. Without --import, behavior unchanged (draft file only).

## Why

Verified gap: author writes a JSON file and stops; import exists as a separate manual step with its own input discipline. The two are one motion in every real use.

## Design notes

- Composition, not duplication: author calls the same importer code path as conveyor.plan.import (single source of truth for validation/rows).
- On audit gaps: keep today's behavior (print operator questions, non-zero exit) — --import must be impossible when the draft isn't runnable.
- Print the follow-up command (conveyor.plan.approve PLAN_ID) so the flow chains discoverably.

## Acceptance

- E2E: valid decomposed draft + --import → DB rows exist, IDs on stdout JSON; invalid draft + --import → questions + non-zero, no rows.
- Round-trip test: author --import output == plan.import output for the same doc (modulo IDs).
close_reason: mix conveyor.author now takes --import: when the audited draft is schema-valid + decomposed, it hands off to the DB via the SAME path as conveyor.plan.import (PlanContract.load + PlanImporter.import_result!), prints the created project/plan/epic IDs + slice_count, and the follow-up 'mix conveyor.plan.approve PLAN_ID' command. Without --import, behavior unchanged (draft file only). A non-runnable draft (no slices) is refused with non-zero and writes NO rows (rejected at load, or by the decomposed? guard for empty slices). 3 task tests: no-import→no rows; decomposed→rows+IDs; undecomposed→refused+no rows.

title: Content-aware Decomposer v1: intent + target repo -> slices, deps, acceptance (the M5 core)
description:
## What

Replace the bypassed content-blind `Decomposer.propose` stub with a real decomposition step inside PlanFoundry: given the audited intent draft (+ optionally a target-repo scan for brownfield later), produce a full `conveyor.plan@1`: slices with titles, desired_behavior, likely_files, key_interfaces, acceptance criteria + commands, and computed TaskDependency edges (interface-consumer ordering).

## How (design sketch)

- Agent-powered drafting (contained Claude Code via the existing PlanFoundry/CodexDrafter seam — ADR-27) with SCHEMA-VALIDATED structured output; retries on schema mismatch; deterministic audits after (the planning compiler's existing lints: dep cycles, orphan interfaces, acceptance completeness, slice-size caps from the Negotiated Scope epic).
- Dependency edges derive from declared interfaces (producer slice exports X; consumer slice lists X in key_interfaces) — the compiler validates the edge set is acyclic and complete relative to interface references, per ADR-16/18 graph discipline. The agent proposes; deterministic audit disposes.
- Authoring-time enforcement of the empirical lessons: likely_files must include barrels when exports change (or rely on always-allowed classes); per-slice caps derived not global; locked-test materialization declared per slice (Option C, PR #40).
- Ambiguity → emit Interrogator questions (sibling bead) instead of guessing.

## Honest scope

v1 targets GREENFIELD (the current dogfood arc). Brownfield decomposition (scanning an existing repo to place slices) is explicitly a later epic — do not gold-plate v1 with it.

## Acceptance

- Golden corpus: >=3 intents (incl. the shipped samples' prose) → decomposed plans that pass plan-lint AND run end-to-end with the reference/fake adapter at $0.
- Schema-invalid model output is retried then surfaced honestly (never a half-imported plan).
- Deterministic audits catch planted defects: a cyclic dep, a missing acceptance, an oversized slice (unit fixtures).
- One live_agent-tagged banked run: real intent → real decomposition → approved → live run (this doubles as §4 bar line-item evidence).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: SINGLE-SOURCE NOTE (plan-space review 2026-07-02): the decomposer must encode the SAME authoring rules as docs/dogfood/decomposition-aid.md (written by never-lie-mmxr.2): likely_files discipline incl. barrel/always-allowed classes, derived per-slice caps, locked-test materialization declaration, acceptance-command patterns. Keep one canonical rule list (the doc cites the lint rules; the decomposer targets the lints) so human authors and the machine decomposer cannot drift apart — plan-lint is the shared enforcement point.

title: Tests & e2e for the authoring pipeline (golden intents, lint gates, full chain)
description:
## What

Epic-level verification: the full chain `author --import` → `plan.approve` → `run` (fake adapter, $0) over the golden intent corpus; failure-path coverage (audit questions, lint refusal, schema-retry exhaustion); logging assertions (each pipeline step logs artifact digests so a plan's provenance is traceable end-to-end: intent hash → draft hash → plan contract sha → approved lock shas).

## Acceptance

- Full-chain e2e green at $0 in CI; provenance digest chain asserted; failure paths produce actionable JSON (questions/findings) + correct exit codes; getting-started doc shows the new 3-command flow and is itself smoke-tested (doc commands parse).

title: Any-language verification: profile-driven toolchain runners (beyond pytest)
description:
## Why this epic exists

The user's goal is "the factory builds MY projects" — which are not all Python. Verified state (2026-07-02):

- Result PARSING is already language-agnostic: locked command_specs declare argv + result_format (junit|tap|json|stdout|custom) and TestResultAdapter parses them (config/command_spec.ex:6, verification_rerunner.ex:144,261-262).
- But live EXECUTION is pytest-hardwired: the verify station calls Eval.ToolchainRunner which builds a Python venv via uv/pip, resolves the pytest binary, writes .conveyor_eval_junit.xml, reconstructs .py:: nodeids, and defaults argv ["pytest","-q"] (toolchain_runner.ex:46-52,210-211,284-333,445,471-512). ToolchainProfile stores image/lock refs only — no language, no runner dispatch (toolchain_profile.ex:20-59).

So adding Elixir or JS today means forking a 500-line Python-specific runner. The seam is missing, not the parsers.

## Goal

A generic command-runner path: the verify station executes the slice's locked command_specs (in the sandbox/container per policy) and parses via the declared result_format — with the pytest runner becoming one profile among peers, and ToolchainProfile gaining the language/test-command identity + dispatch.

## Language targets (in order)

1. **Elixir/mix** (mix test via a junit formatter dep or the TAP/stdout adapter) — this is also the self-hosting horizon's prerequisite (Conveyor building Conveyor needs an Elixir toolchain).
2. **JS/TS** (vitest/jest --reporter=junit).
3. Python stays first-class (existing runner becomes the python profile).

## Exit

A fixture Elixir repo and a fixture JS repo each run through the FULL production loop ($0 fake agent + real toolchain verify) with honest pass/fail parsing; the sample-python path is unchanged (regression-guarded); a real dogfood run on a non-Python greenfield target is possible.

title: Generic command runner: execute locked command_specs + parse declared result_format
description:
## What

The core seam: a runner that (1) takes the slice's locked command_specs (argv, cwd, env policy, result_format), (2) executes them via the existing sandbox path (ToolExecutor/ContainedExec discipline — policy pre-exec check, network per policy, docker image from ToolchainProfile), (3) collects the declared output artifact (junit file / stdout / json), (4) hands it to the existing TestResultAdapter, and (5) emits the same structured verification result the gate's TestExecution stage consumes today.

## Design constraints

- The VerificationRerunner's pluggable-format path already models this (it currently carries a no-op default runner, verification_rerunner.ex:136) — implement the real runner THERE and route the verify station through it, rather than growing a second parallel abstraction. The pytest-specific ToolchainRunner then becomes the python-profile specialization (env build: venv/uv) invoked by the same seam.
- Environment preparation is profile business (python: venv+requirements.lock; elixir: mix deps.get in image; js: npm/pnpm ci) — keep it behind the profile, out of the generic runner.
- Determinism + evidence: command transcript, exit codes, artifact digests recorded per existing evidence discipline; junit path per-run temp (not a fixed dotfile — avoids collision, fixes the .conveyor_eval_junit.xml hardcode).
- Fail honest: missing artifact / unparseable output → verification failure with reason, never a silent pass (mirror the empty-acceptance-suite-fails rule from M4).

## Acceptance

- Unit: runner executes a fixture command_spec matrix (junit, stdout, json formats; nonzero exits; missing artifact) with correct structured results.
- The verify station uses the seam; sample-python e2e unchanged (regression); policy pre-exec check demonstrably applied to spec argv (a disallowed command is refused).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: SCOPE NOTE (plan-space review 2026-07-02): ALL toolchain-execution sites must route through this one seam, not just the verify station — (a) verify station, (b) acceptance-calibration station (runs locked acceptance at base in an isolated worktree; if it stays pytest-bound, non-Python plans break there first), (c) MutantGauntlet corpus runs, (d) the gate's TestExecution rerun path. Acceptance for this bead should include a grep-level design-laws test: no direct pytest/ToolchainRunner invocation outside the seam.

comments.author: assistant

title: ToolchainProfile: language identity + runner dispatch + per-language images
description:
## What

Extend ToolchainProfile (toolchain_profile.ex — today only image ref/digest, dependency_lock_refs, cache_policy, sbom_ref) with the identity a runner needs: language/runtime tag, env-prep strategy, default result_format, and the dispatch that picks the profile's runner (python-specialized vs generic). Ship pinned images per language (the existing sample-python-runner pattern) under toolchains/.

## Why

Profiles are the reproducibility identity — "passes here, fails there" is exactly what pinned per-language images prevent (same rationale as the D1 one-image-for-agent-and-gate rule). Without profile-level dispatch, language selection would leak into per-slice config where it can drift.

## Acceptance

- Profile round-trip: declare an elixir profile → verify station picks generic runner + elixir image + mix env-prep; python profile → existing runner path.
- Migration note: existing rows/dev seeds get a default python profile (append-only migration discipline).
- Doctor check: conveyor.doctor validates the selected profile's image exists locally, with an actionable message.

title: Elixir + JS toolchain profiles with fixture-repo e2e (self-host prerequisite)
description:
## What

The two concrete profiles + proof:

1. **Elixir**: image with pinned Elixir/OTP (mirror mise.toml), env-prep `mix deps.get --only test` + compile; result via junit formatter dep (or TAP adapter) declared in command_specs; fixture greenfield Elixir repo under samples/ with a small plan.
2. **JS/TS**: node image, pnpm/npm ci prep, vitest --reporter=junit fixture repo + plan.

Each runs the FULL production loop at $0 (fake/reference adapter + REAL toolchain verify): a passing slice verifies green; an induced test failure parses to the correct failing-test ids (the rework-intelligence epic consumes exactly those ids — alignment test).

## Why Elixir first

Self-hosting (M8 north star; eval-094) requires the factory to verify Elixir work. This bead is deliberately the first concrete step toward "Conveyor builds Conveyor" that doesn't require any trust decision — it's just toolchain capability.

## Acceptance

- Both fixture repos + plans committed; e2e green at $0 in CI (docker-gated tag if image pulls are heavy — follow the existing live/docker tagging conventions); failing-test id parsing asserted per language.

title: ContextScout deepening: real repo context (content excerpts, language-neutral) into the agent prompt
description:
## Why

Verified (2026-07-02): ContextScout genuinely walks the workspace and selects up to 8 relevant files — but it NEVER reads file contents (relevant_files are {path, reason} only, context_scout.ex:181-195), key_interfaces are copied verbatim from the brief (:337), and the selection heuristics are Python-biased (main.py/app.py regexes, :172-179). So the implementer starts each slice knowing which files might matter but not what is in them — on anything beyond tiny greenfield, that costs attempts (the agent burns its session re-discovering the repo) and precision (interface drift between slices).

Agent success rate per attempt is THE quality lever the factory controls before the gate ever fires; context quality is its cheapest input. This also front-loads what brownfield will demand later.

## Scope (three tasks)

1. **Content excerpts**: for the top-K selected files, include bounded, redacted excerpts (interface-bearing regions preferred: module heads, def/class/function signatures, exports) in the ContextPack; PromptBuilder already renders context sections under the untrusted banner (prompt_builder.ex:178-213) — extend rendering for excerpt blocks. Hard byte budget, deterministic selection+truncation (prompt digests must stay replayable).
2. **Language-neutral selection + signature extraction**: replace Python-biased heuristics with per-language patterns keyed off the ToolchainProfile language (coordinate with the Any-language epic); extract signatures by regex per language family (no tree-sitter dependency in v1 — keep it deterministic and cheap; note the upgrade path).
3. **Tests**: fixture repos per language → assert prompt contains the expected signature excerpts; budget/truncation determinism; secret-planted fixture never leaks (redactor applied); no-regression golden for the tiny-greenfield case.

## Trust boundary

Repo content is UNTRUSTED input (ADR-07): excerpts render under the untrusted banner with the existing instruction-source registration (prompt_builder.ex:271-282 already registers scout files as :untrusted — extend to excerpt blocks).

## Exit

On the fixture corpus, attempt-1 prompts contain the interfaces the slice consumes; measured on the eval corpus, first-attempt gate pass rate does not regress and (expectation, to be measured honestly) improves on multi-slice plans where slices consume prior slices' interfaces.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: HONEST-ROI NOTE (plan-space review 2026-07-02): the implementer agents are TOOL-USING (Claude Code/codex explore the workspace themselves), so raw content injection matters less than for one-shot prompting. Priority inside this epic accordingly: (1) INTERFACE HANDOFF is the high-value piece — a consumer slice's prompt should carry the producer slice's actual exported signatures (from contract/interface knowledge + scout extraction), because cross-slice interface drift is what kills multi-slice plans; (2) excerpts are an anchor/floor — keep the byte budget deliberately small (agent can read more itself); (3) the .3 task's before/after first-attempt-pass-rate measurement decides whether to invest further. If measurement shows no lift, stop at interfaces.

title: Containment completion: egress allowlist proxy + network-isolated gate rerun
description:
## Why this epic exists

Containment landed (PRs #39/#40: ContainedExec, read-only rootfs, scrubbed env, non-root, host uid) — with two documented residuals:

1. **Agent egress is an open bridge.** stations/implementer.ex carries the ponytail note: upgrade path is "allowlist-via-proxy." The agent container can reach anything, not just its model API. For sterile targets that's tolerable; for real repos it is the exfiltration + supply-chain hole (a prompt-injected agent can ship your code anywhere, or pull anything in).
2. **The gate's verification rerun is not network-isolated** on the default path — hermeticity is the one missing control of six (ROADMAP D1 #2: only network:blocked missing on :local). Consequence today: the hermeticity signal abstains (honest but noisy — parks it shouldn't need); consequence tomorrow: a test that phones home can lie.

## Components

1. **Egress allowlist proxy for the agent container**: default-deny egress except the model API host(s) (api.anthropic.com / the codex endpoint per adapter capability declaration); denials LOGGED as run evidence (a denied egress attempt is itself a signal worth surfacing — observed_risk producer food). Implementation options: HTTP(S) proxy container (agent gets only proxy env + no direct route), or docker network + nftables/ipset allowlist; pick the one that stays declarative in NetworkPolicy (network_policy.ex already models egress/none — add allowlist mode).
2. **Network-isolated verify/gate rerun**: run the gate's verification in --network none (the sandbox profile already supports none for verify per the containment work — make it the DEFAULT for gate reruns and produce the hermeticity observation so the IntegritySentinel signal stops abstaining). Relates to jmnt (stage producers) + D1.
3. **Tests**: policy unit matrix (allowlist grants/denies); containerized e2e (docker-tagged): agent reaches model API, cannot reach a canary host, denial evidenced; gate rerun with a phone-home test → hermeticity violation surfaced, not silent.

## Exit

Real-repo posture: agent can ONLY talk to its model; gate verification runs offline; both facts are evidence-backed per run, and hermeticity abstention noise disappears from TrustScore.

title: Scout content excerpts: bounded, redacted, interface-bearing file excerpts in ContextPack
description: Implement component 1 of the parent epic (see epic body for full background/design): top-K selected files get bounded, redacted content excerpts (interface-bearing regions: module heads, signatures, exports) into ContextPack; PromptBuilder renders them under the untrusted banner with instruction-source registration (extend prompt_builder.ex:178-213,271-282). Hard byte budget, deterministic selection + truncation so prompt digests stay replayable. Acceptance: fixture repo -> prompt contains expected excerpt blocks; secret-planted fixture never leaks (redactor unit); truncation deterministic (digest-stable across runs); budget declared in one config place and logged when it fires.
close_reason: Component 1 of the ContextScout-deepening epic: bounded, redacted, interface-bearing file excerpts in the ContextPack, rendered under the untrusted banner. WHAT: (1) ContextPack.file_excerpts attribute {:array,:map} (+migration) — each %{path,excerpt,truncated,bytes}. (2) ContextScout reads the top-K SOURCE files from relevant_files (deterministic order), takes the file HEAD up to a byte budget (v1 anchor; per-language signature extraction is aabq.2), UTF-8-safe deterministic truncation (never splits a codepoint), redacts via Security.Redactor (repo content is UNTRUSTED per ADR-07), logs when truncation fires. (3) Budget declared in ONE config place (config :conveyor, Conveyor.ContextScout, excerpt_max_files/excerpt_max_bytes; code defaults 5/1200; per-call opts override). (4) PromptBuilder renders a 'File excerpts:' section under the existing untrusted banner and registers each excerpt as a :repo_file/:untrusted instruction source (ADR-07 authority covers the injected bytes, not just paths). Tests (aabq.1's own; the per-language corpus + no-regression golden are aabq.3): excerpts populated+deterministic (byte-identical across runs), only source files excerpted; byte budget honored + truncated? deterministic; a planted AWS key never leaks (redacted) using an ISOLATED temp workspace (no committed-sample mutation); prompt_builder golden — excerpt renders under the banner + registered untrusted (sources 8->9). 5 gates green: full suite 1333 passed, credo/dialyzer(8/8)/format clean. HONEST NOTE: byte budget caps the SOURCE read (the replayability-critical property); redaction may add small fixed-size tokens on top. Interface-handoff (the high-value piece per the epic ROI note) and language-neutral selection are aabq.2; the before/after pass-rate measurement is aabq.3.

title: Language-neutral scout selection + signature extraction (de-Python-bias)
description: Component 2 of the parent epic: replace Python-biased heuristics (context_scout.ex:172-179) with per-language selection + signature patterns keyed off ToolchainProfile language (coordinate with the any-language-verify epic's profile identity). Regex-based per language family in v1 (deterministic, cheap; tree-sitter noted as upgrade path, not built). Elixir (defmodule/def/@spec/@behaviour), JS/TS (export/function/class/interface), Python (existing, refactored into the same shape). Acceptance: per-language fixture repos -> correct top-K selection + extracted signatures; the existing Python behavior preserved under the new shape (golden regression).
close_reason: Component 2: language-neutral scout selection + signature extraction (de-Python-bias). WHAT: (1) New Conveyor.ContextScout.Signatures — regex-based interface signature extraction per language family, keyed off file extension (deterministic, dependency-free): Elixir (defmodule, @spec/@type/@callback/@behaviour, def/defmacro — NOT defp), JS/TS (export, function, class, interface/type/enum), Python (def/class, async def). extract/2 returns interface-bearing lines in source order (bounded 40) or nil (unknown lang / no signatures) so the caller falls back to head. (2) ContextScout excerpts now prefer signatures over raw head bytes (interface handoff — the epic's high-value piece), still byte-capped + redacted + deterministic. (3) De-Python-biased selection: route_or_model_file?/entrypoint detection now match the basename STEM against language-neutral @entrypoint_stems (main/app/server/router/routes/index) and @model_stems across ANY source extension — replacing hardcoded main.py/app.py/router.py/routes.py. TESTS: Signatures unit (Elixir/JS/Python extraction, defp excluded, imports/consts excluded, unknown->nil, ext mapping incl. .TSX); a non-Python entrypoint (server.js) selected language-neutrally via the GET interface hint + its 'export function handler' signature handed off in the excerpt; existing Python golden preserved (main.py still selected with router/model reason). 5 gates green: full suite 1338 passed, credo/dialyzer(8/8)/format clean. NOTE: language keyed off extension in v1; ToolchainProfile.language (once tt6v attaches it) and tree-sitter are the noted upgrade paths. Per-language fixture corpus + before/after pass-rate measurement are aabq.3.

title: Scout tests: per-language fixtures, budget determinism, no-leak, prompt assertions
description: Component 3 of the parent epic — the consolidated test surface: per-language fixture repos assert prompt contains the interfaces the slice consumes; byte-budget + truncation determinism (same input -> same prompt digest, twice); planted-secret fixture never reaches a prompt; tiny-greenfield golden shows no regression vs today's path-only pack; logging: scout emits per-slice log line (files selected, excerpt bytes, truncations) and tests capture it. Also add the honest measurement hook: record first-attempt gate pass rate on the eval corpus before/after (no target enforced — measured, per the project's honesty conventions).

title: Egress allowlist proxy for the agent container (default-deny except model API)
description: Component 1 of the parent epic (see epic body): default-deny agent egress except declared model-API hosts, per adapter capability declaration; denials logged as run evidence (observed_risk producer food). Extend NetworkPolicy (network_policy.ex) with an allowlist mode kept declarative; implementation choice (proxy container + proxy-only env vs docker network + nftables allowlist) decided in-bead with a short ADR-style note. Acceptance: policy unit matrix (grant/deny); docker-tagged e2e: agent reaches model API, cannot reach canary host, denial appears in evidence; implementer.ex ponytail note (allowlist-via-proxy upgrade path) resolved and removed.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: SCOPE NOTE (plan-space review 2026-07-02): the allowlist applies to ALL agent-role containers — implementer, reviewer (trust-reviewer-m4b2.2), and the Plan Foundry drafter (prose-to-plan-aaun.3) — one NetworkPolicy mode, per-role host allowlists derived from each adapter's capability declaration. Verify/gate sandboxes stay --network none (stricter than allowlist; unchanged).

title: Network-isolated gate rerun by default + hermeticity observation producer
description: Component 2 of the parent epic: make --network none the DEFAULT for the gate's verification rerun and PRODUCE the hermeticity observation so the IntegritySentinel signal stops abstaining (ROADMAP D1 #2: network:blocked is the one missing hermeticity control; missing it causes abstain-noise, not false-passes — this bead removes the noise honestly). Relates to jmnt stage producers. Acceptance: gate rerun runs offline (docker-tagged e2e: phone-home test surfaces a hermeticity violation finding, not a silent pass); hermeticity observation present in trust evidence on a normal run; abstention caused by hermeticity_absent disappears from the /usr/bin/zsh e2e run summary.

title: Containment tests: allowlist matrix + offline-gate e2e + evidence assertions
description: Component 3 — consolidated verification for the epic: NetworkPolicy allowlist unit matrix; containerized e2e (docker-tagged, follows existing tagging conventions) covering agent-egress grant/deny + offline gate rerun + evidence records for both; log assertions (denied egress logs host+slice; hermeticity observation logged per gate rerun). Documentation: DOGFOOD-READINESS Ring-3 caveat updated; docs/schemas network-policy page gains the allowlist mode.

title: Terminal triage loop: conveyor.triage + conveyor.watch (CLI-first operator surface)
description:
## Why

The operator loop today: run finishes → `conveyor.run_view` (story) + `conveyor.parked` (list) + manual DB/CLI archaeology to act. The a3hf program plans the full cockpit inbox (a3hf.1.3.x) — but DOGFOOD-READINESS's steer is explicit: run first, defer cockpit UI, CLI is the persona's home. This epic is the CLI-FIRST precursor that delivers the triage capability now and leaves the cockpit as a later rendering of the same read-models (no wasted work: a3hf.1.3.2's cockpit surface renders what this epic's read-model produces).

## Components

1. **`mix conveyor.triage`**: parked/needs-human items least-trusted-first with reason (typed park-reason taxonomy — adopts a3hf.1.3.1 as the prerequisite), evidence summary, diff stat, and the exact disposition commands. Interactive-free (JSON + human stderr table per CLI conventions).
2. **Disposition subcommands**: `conveyor.triage approve|rework|reject SLICE` wired to the SAME closers the cockpit will use (a3hf.1.3.3's engine half lives here): approve → accept path (records human override in evidence + the gate-escape ledger candidacy); rework → re-enqueue attempt with operator note threaded into the rework prompt (Rework Intelligence epic renders it); reject → terminal park with reason.
3. **`mix conveyor.watch RUN_ID`**: live tail of the run ledger (station starts/finishes, attempts, gate verdicts, parks) for attended dogfood runs — the "watch what breaks" tool DOGFOOD-READINESS step 1 wants.
4. **Tests & e2e**: disposition round-trip ($0 fixture run → park → each disposition → correct terminal state + ledger events + evidence); watch renders a recorded ledger deterministically; park-reason taxonomy coverage (every producer-emitted reason renders with guidance text).

## Design notes

- Dispositions are LEDGER EVENTS (event-sourced discipline): a human decision is run history, replayable and auditable, not a DB poke.
- Coordinate reason enum values with the convergence sentinel (rework-intel epic emits convergence_stall/no_progress) and scope epic (scope_denied) — one taxonomy, no synonyms drift. a3hf.1.3.1 is the canonical home for the enum; this epic depends on it.

## Exit

An overnight run's morning-after is: `conveyor.triage` → read reasons → three disposition commands → done, all recorded. The cockpit inbox (a3hf.1.3.2) becomes a rendering task over these read-models.

title: Learning flywheel: gate-escape ledger + auto gap-log + calibration report
description:
## Why

The factory's long-term moat is that every run teaches it (STRATEGY Track D; README: "an eval dataset the factory learns from"). Today the learning loop is manual: dogfood findings become beads only when a human writes them (dxgw/8mnx/uh2g exist because someone typed them). Two structural pieces make learning automatic and honest:

1. **Gate-escape ledger** (extends a3hf.3.2, linked): when a defect is later found in an ACCEPTED slice, `mix conveyor.escape record` files an escape_record tied to the original verdict + evidence + gate version. Gate-escape rate is STRATEGY Metric 4 and the §4 bar's honest counterweight — a gate that never records its misses is unfalsifiable.
2. **Auto gap-log**: at run end, append structured entries (parks with reasons, sentinel stops, crashes, human overrides via triage dispositions) to .conveyor/gap-log.jsonl + a rendered md — field-aligned with docs/dogfood/gap-log-template.md (Never-Lie epic writes the template; this automates it). Every dogfood run then compounds without depending on operator discipline.
3. **Calibration report**: `mix conveyor.calibration` — false-park rate (from the Negotiated Scope corpus + triage approve-overrides), escape rate over time, abstain-band hit distribution → the evidence base for tuning TrustScore thresholds (feeds a3hf.3.1's dashboard later; CLI-first here).

## Honesty constraint

Escapes and overrides are append-only records tied to gate/rubric versions — recalibration must be able to say "under gate vX we escaped N times," or tuning becomes curve-fitting to vibes.

## Exit

After any run: gap-log has the machine-written entries; after any human override or later defect: one command records it against the verdict; calibration report renders the trend. The factory's trustworthiness becomes a measured time series, not a claim.

title: MutantGauntlet: cover the deferred static-stage mutants (contract_lock, run_check, code_quality)
description:
## What

Extend MutantGauntlet to the remaining `deferred_static_stage` mutant classes so the FULL canary corpus discriminates, not just the behavioral + policy_compliance subsets: contract_lock mutants (locked contract/test-pack tampering shapes), run_check, code_quality_delta. Keep CI-gated via `conveyor.eval.scorecard --gate` with the zero-false-pass discipline.

## Why

ROADMAP M4 remainder + §4 bar line item ("the remaining static mutants stay deferred_static_stage"). A gate stage without a mutant that can catch it lying is unmeasured; unmeasured stages are exactly how false confidence re-enters after M4's de-laundering. Note: reviewer-stage mutants are covered separately in the trust-reviewer epic (its MutantGauntlet task) — this bead is the static remainder only, so the two together complete the gauntlet surface.

## Acceptance

- Each newly-wired static stage (as jmnt lands producers) gains at least one discriminating mutant; scorecard reports per-stage false-pass; CI gate covers the extended set; deferred_static_stage markers removed as covered.

title: SerialDriver decomposition: extract ledger-fold / reaper / gate-orchestration / commit engine
description:
## What

Behavior-preserving decomposition of `lib/conveyor/planning/serial_driver.ex` (1,226 LoC — the largest, highest-blast-radius module) into its natural seams, one extraction per PR, each locked by the existing test surface before/after:

1. Ledger fold/reconstruct (event application + resume state) — pairs with run_reconstruction.
2. Reaper/timeout management (slice/run wall-clock).
3. Gate-stage orchestration (stage list, evaluation, verdict handoff to Finalizer).
4. Workspace/commit engine (reset/commit-on-accept; the blind `git add -A` at :666 lives here — coordinate with r6c5, which changes WHAT is committed while this bead changes WHERE that code lives).

## Why (and why only P3)

Every P1 epic in this program (reviewer wiring, rework threading, scope amendment, sentinel) lands INSIDE this file's orbit; a 1.2k-line orchestrator is where regressions like 22r6 hide. But refactor-for-refactor's-sake must not preempt the trust work — hence P3, opportunistic: extract a seam WHEN a feature touches it (the reviewer wiring naturally extracts gate orchestration; the sentinel naturally extracts the attempt/ledger seam). This bead is the umbrella that makes those extractions deliberate rather than accidental.

## Constraints

- No behavior change per extraction (cassette replay digests identical before/after — gexs's CI test is the lock, hence the dependency).
- One extraction per PR; module docs state ownership boundaries; design-laws test updated for the new module names.

## Acceptance

- serial_driver.ex under ~400 LoC as coordinator; four modules with owned tests; replay digests unchanged; no new public API leaked outside planning/.

title: mix conveyor.triage: parked items least-trusted-first with reason, evidence, disposition commands
description: Component 1 of the parent epic (full design there): list parked/needs-human slices least-trusted-first with typed park reason (taxonomy from a3hf.1.3.1 - blocking dep on the epic), evidence summary, diff stat, and exact disposition commands. JSON stdout + human table stderr per CLI conventions (cli/exit_codes.ex). Acceptance: zero-dollar fixture run with parks of each reason type renders correctly; JSON schema documented; empty-queue case exits 0 with clean output.

title: Triage dispositions: approve/rework/reject wired to closers as ledger events
description: Component 2 of the parent epic: conveyor.triage approve|rework|reject SLICE. approve records a human-override in evidence and flags gate-escape-ledger candidacy (coordinate with learning-flywheel epic); rework re-enqueues an attempt with the operator note threaded into the rework prompt (rework-intel epic renders operator notes in the trusted section); reject is a terminal park with reason. Every disposition is a LEDGER EVENT (event-sourced; replay/resume safe; auditable) - never a bare DB poke. This is the engine half of a3hf.1.3.3; the cockpit later calls the same closers. Acceptance: disposition round-trip e2e at zero dollars: park then each disposition then correct terminal state + ledger events + evidence records; crash between disposition and effect reconciles idempotently (exactly-once discipline).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: DESIGN DEPTH FLAG (plan-space review 2026-07-02): APPROVE on a parked slice after the run has moved on is the hard case — the workspace may be gone and transitive dependents were skipped. v1 semantics, decided now so the implementer does not discover this mid-bead: approve = (a) record human-override disposition + evidence, (b) apply the slice's CAPTURED PatchSet to the integration target (the patch is durable evidence; never re-run the agent for an approve), (c) print the follow-up command to re-run the skipped-dependent subgraph as a new bounded run (auto-resume of skipped dependents is explicitly v2 — coordinate with run-resume machinery when it comes). If patch application conflicts (target moved), approve fails honestly with the conflict and suggests rework instead.

title: mix conveyor.watch: live ledger tail for attended runs
description: Component 3 of the parent epic: tail the run ledger live (station start/finish, attempt transitions, gate verdicts, parks, sentinel decisions) for an in-flight or completed run. This is the attended-dogfood tool DOGFOOD-READINESS step 1 calls for (watch what breaks). Renders from ledger events only (no new state); works on a finished run as deterministic replay. Acceptance: recorded fixture ledger renders the expected event stream deterministically (golden test); live mode follows a running zero-dollar demo run; --json mode for machine consumption.

title: Triage tests & e2e: taxonomy coverage, disposition round-trip, watch golden
description: Component 4: consolidated epic verification - every producer-emitted park reason renders with guidance text (exhaustive enum test against the a3hf.1.3.1 taxonomy: adding a reason without triage rendering fails CI); disposition round-trip matrix incl. idempotency + resume; watch golden-render; log assertions (each disposition logs slice, actor, reason). Docs: getting-started gains the morning-after triage section.

title: Gate-escape ledger: record later-found defects against the accepting verdict
