description: Component 1 of the parent epic (full rationale there; extends a3hf.3.2 - related): mix conveyor.escape record with slice/run/note files an append-only escape_record tied to the original verdict, evidence digests, gate stage list and rubric/gate versions. STRATEGY Metric 4 (gate escape rate) becomes measurable. Triage approve-overrides auto-flag candidacy (coordinate with the terminal-triage disposition bead). Acceptance: record + list round-trip; escape rows immutable (append-only enforced); schema documented in docs/schemas; escape rate appears in the calibration report (sibling bead).

title: Auto gap-log: machine-written run findings (parks, sentinel stops, crashes, overrides)
description: Component 2 of the parent epic: at run end (and on triage dispositions), append structured entries to .conveyor/gap-log.jsonl and render md - field-aligned with docs/dogfood/gap-log-template.md (written by the never-lie epic docs task; keep field names identical). Entries: run id, slice, symptom class (park reason / sentinel / crash / override), evidence refs, timestamp. Acceptance: zero-dollar run with induced park + override yields the expected jsonl entries (golden); jsonl is append-only across runs; md render matches template; a doc note explains how entries become beads (the manual judgment step stays human).

title: mix conveyor.calibration: false-park rate, escape rate, abstain distribution over time
description: Component 3 of the parent epic: the measured-trust report - false-park rate (from the negotiated-scope regression corpus + approve-overrides), gate-escape rate (component 1), abstain-band hit distribution, per gate/rubric version, as a time series. CLI-first (JSON + md); feeds a3hf.3.1 dashboard later. Honesty constraint from the epic: every number ties to gate/rubric versions so recalibration is evidence-based, never curve-fitting to vibes. Acceptance: fixture history renders the expected report (golden); version-attribution asserted; documented as the input to TrustScore threshold tuning (tuning itself is a separate, human-reviewed change).

title: Transient-failure classification: infra errors must not burn attempts or park good slices
description:
## Gap (found in plan-space review, 2026-07-02)

An 8-hour unattended run WILL hit transient provider failures (API 5xx/529 overload, rate limits, network blips, container start hiccups). Today every agent-call failure is treated the same: it consumes an attempt from AttemptBudget, and repeated infra noise can exhaust the budget and park a perfectly good slice — a false park caused by weather, not work. That directly attacks the two headline metrics (autonomous completion rate, mean unattended run length).

## Change

1. Classify agent-call outcomes at the adapter boundary (AdapterBase + per-adapter error surfaces): clear INFRA classes (timeout before first token, connection refused/reset, HTTP 429/5xx from the provider, container failed to start) vs WORK outcomes (agent ran and produced output — including bad output).
2. INFRA outcomes: bounded backoff-retry (e.g. up to 2 retries with jittered backoff), do NOT consume attempt budget, recorded as ledger events with the error class.
3. Fail-closed guardrails: only allowlisted, deterministic error classes qualify as infra; DEFAULT for anything ambiguous = burns the attempt (an agent that crashes itself must not earn infinite retries). A per-slice infra-retry cap (separate small budget) prevents an outage from spinning forever; cap exhaustion parks with typed reason infra_error — triage/digest can then distinguish "the provider was down" from "the work is hard" at a glance.
4. EmergencyStop/budget interplay: infra retries still respect run budgets and reapers (a retry storm must not extend the run past its wall-clock reaper).

## Where

AttemptLoop/AttemptBudget (budget semantics), AdapterBase (classification seam), park-reason taxonomy (infra_error — coordinate with a3hf.1.3.1), run ledger (event types).

## Acceptance

- Unit: classification matrix per adapter error shape (timeout/429/5xx/refused/garbled-output/nonzero-exit-with-output) — allowlist exact, ambiguous burns attempt.
- $0 e2e (scripted flaky adapter): 5xx-twice-then-success completes with ZERO attempts consumed by the failures + 2 infra ledger events; persistent-5xx parks with infra_error after the infra cap, attempt budget untouched; run summary counts infra retries separately.
- Resume test: infra-retry state reconstructs from ledger (no double retries after crash).
- Logging: every infra event logs class, adapter, backoff delay, retry index; e2e asserts via CaptureLog.
close_reason: Infra-vs-work classification + adapter-seam bounded retry shipped — the mechanism so transient provider failures never burn an attempt. Conveyor.AgentRunner.FailureClass.classify/1: allowlist-exact infra classes (timeout/rate_limited/provider_unavailable/connection_failed/container_start_failed), fail-closed (ambiguous/agent-produced-output => :work => burns). AdapterBase.run_with_timeout now retries infra outcomes in-place (bounded cap 2, exponential backoff, injectable sleep, structured Logger warnings with class/adapter/retry index) — absorbed at the seam so the AttemptLoop never sees them as attempts; small backoff keeps total wall-clock under the reaper. 14 tests: classification matrix + scripted flaky-adapter (5xx-twice-then-success, persistent, work-never-retried) + logging. Remaining observability/integration (persisted ledger events with error class, typed infra_error park label after cap, resume reconstruction, full-pipeline $0 e2e) split to rt6k.6.1.

title: Doctor truth: prereq checks match the active adapter (claude_code) and containment stack
description:
## What (verify-then-fix)

DOGFOOD-READINESS (pre-PR#39) documented `mix conveyor.doctor` as enforcing git + docker + a logged-in `codex` CLI. The default backend is now contained Claude Code (PRs #39/#40). Verify doctor covers the ACTUAL first-run path for the current default and fix what it misses:

1. Active-adapter prereqs: claude CLI present + authenticated (subscription login), with an actionable failure message; codex checks only when codex is the selected adapter.
2. Containment prereqs: `conveyor-agent:latest` image present (config/dev.exs:64 requires prebuilt), docker usable rootless/regular, workspace mount viability.
3. Policy/config load: `.conveyor/policies/*.toml` parse cleanly (ties to a7kf once loading is live — doctor should catch a bad TOML before a night run does).
4. Doctor's checks must be adapter-parameterized (doctor --adapter X or read from config), not hardcoded to one vendor.

## Why (never-lie framing)

Doctor is the operator's pre-flight trust surface. A doctor that validates the WRONG backend passes green while the actual run fails at slice 1 — the exact "surface says X, reality is Y" class this epic kills.

## Acceptance

- Doctor matrix test: each missing prereq (no claude login, no image, bad TOML) yields a distinct, actionable finding; all-green host passes.
- Getting-started doc's doctor section matches actual checks (doc + code updated together).
- Logging: doctor emits one line per check with pass/fail + remedy hint.

title: ROADMAP execution-update: record the 2026-07-02 improvement program + milestone mapping
description:
## What

ROADMAP.md is the declared single source of truth for direction; it currently ends at the 2026-06-25 execution update. Add a short execution-update block recording the 2026-07-02 improvement program and its milestone mapping so board and doc agree:

- trust-reviewer epic -> M4 remainder (reviewer_aggregation carved out of jmnt; gauntlet-static-mutants task covers the deferred static mutants).
- negotiated-scope epic -> ADR-26 activation for scope (dr1m.4 family) + empirical dogfood fixes (8mnx/dxgw).
- rework-intel epic -> M6 remainder (convergence sentinel / no-progress detection) + retry-feedback quality + transient-failure classification.
- prose-to-plan epic -> M5 remainder (content-aware Decomposer; author->import; bulk approve).
- containment-complete epic -> D1 #2/#5 (hermetic gate rerun; egress allowlist).
- never-lie epic -> M0-spirit honesty sweep (exit codes, AGENTS.md threading, config truth, board reconciliation).
- banked proof -> bd50/gexs re-scoped + 9z4r.2 (M1/M3 exit evidence).
- Still unfiled from M6 remainder (deliberately): GC sweeper (ADR-10), morning-digest wiring (a3hf.1.1), EmergencyStop/BudgetReservations enforcement (a3hf.2.1.x, CLI-first steer applied).

One paragraph + the epic slugs is enough; per prettier proseWrap:always.

## Acceptance

ROADMAP top block updated; no other doc claims disturbed; markdown formatted (prettier).
close_reason: ROADMAP.md updated: added a 2026-07-02 execution-update blockquote at the top (most-recent-first, above the 2026-06-25 block), recording the improvement program + epic->milestone mapping exactly as specified: trust-reviewer->M4 remainder (reviewer_aggregation carved out of jmnt; gauntlet-static-mutants for deferred static mutants); negotiated-scope->ADR-26 scope activation (dr1m.4 family) + dogfood fixes (8mnx/dxgw); rework-intel->M6 remainder (convergence sentinel/no-progress, retry-feedback quality, transient-failure classification); prose-to-plan->M5 remainder (content-aware Decomposer, author->import, bulk approve); containment-complete->D1 #2/#5 (hermetic gate rerun, egress allowlist); never-lie->M0-spirit honesty sweep; banked proof->bd50/gexs re-scoped + 9z4r.2; plus the deliberately-unfiled M6 items (GC sweeper ADR-10, morning-digest a3hf.1.1, EmergencyStop/BudgetReservations a3hf.2.1.x). No other doc claims disturbed; prettier proseWrap:always compliant (npx prettier --write reports unchanged); mix format green. Markdown-only change — compile/test/credo/dialyzer unaffected from the last green run (1338 passed).

title: Test flake: VerificationRerunnerTest test-id leakage under non-random seed (base-1 vs 1)
description: Under MIX_ENV=test mix test --seed 0, test/conveyor/verification_rerunner_test.exs:54 fails: expected test identity 'base-1' but got '1' — a global/shared test-id counter leaks across async tests. Passes in isolation. Pre-existing (independent of serial_driver run_status fix in 22r6). Fix: make the rerunner test's identity source deterministic/isolated (async: false or unique-per-test ids).
close_reason: Root cause was nondeterministic suite order (Ash.read! with no ORDER BY), not id leakage; VerificationRerunner now sorts suites by canonical suite_kind then key. --seed 0 full suite green.

title: Wire always-allowed scope classes from project policy TOML into DiffPolicy
notes: Split from nyrl.1. The DiffScope STAGE now enforces always-allowed classes (shipped package_barrels set + DiffPolicy.always_allowed_path_classes field, precedence + evidence + size-guard, all tested). REMAINING: a config surface so a project can DECLARE extra classes in policy TOML and have them populate DiffPolicy.always_allowed_path_classes at RunSpec assembly time (run_spec_assembler.create_default_diff_policy! currently derives DiffPolicy only from slice.likely_files; profiles loaded by a7kf are command profiles, not scope config). Decide where scope classes live in TOML (a [diff_scope]/[scope] section or a dedicated file) and thread the loaded value through to create_default_diff_policy!. Acceptance: class list declared in project TOML -> active in the stage (end-to-end), with a round-trip test.
close_reason: Config surface for always-allowed scope classes now wires end-to-end. A project declares extra classes as [[project.always_allowed_path_classes]] (name + globs) in .conveyor/config.toml; Conveyor.Config parses+validates them into ProjectConfig.always_allowed_path_classes (optional, default []). RunSpecAssembler.create_default_diff_policy! loads the project config (graceful fallback to [] when absent/invalid; opts override seam) and populates DiffPolicy.always_allowed_path_classes, so the DiffScope gate honors the declared classes (unioned with the shipped set). 6 tests: Config parses classes / defaults empty / rejects missing globs; assembler round-trip (config.toml class -> DiffPolicy field -> DiffScope grants a matching docs/ path outside allowed_globs, no blocking finding). Completes nyrl.1.

title: Infra-retry observability: ledger events, typed infra_error park, resume, full e2e
notes: Remainder of rt6k.6 (transient-failure classification). Mechanism landed in rt6k.6: FailureClass classifier + AdapterBase seam bounded infra-retry (absorbed so attempts aren't burned) + logging. REMAINING: persist infra retries as run-ledger events carrying the error class (for digest/triage); when the infra-retry cap is exhausted, surface a typed infra_error park reason to AttemptLoop (distinct from rework-exhausted) so triage sees 'provider was down' vs 'work is hard'; resume test (infra-retry state reconstructs from ledger, no double retries after crash); full-pipeline $0 e2e with a scripted flaky adapter through SerialDriver.
close_reason: Completes rt6k.6's infra-retry mechanism with full observability + typed parking. (1) AdapterBase.run_with_timeout now returns {stdout, exit_code, infra_meta}; on infra-retry-cap exhaustion infra_meta = %{class, retries}, threaded through claude_code/codex RawRunResult.metadata['infra_error'] -> implementer station output -> slice_result.output. (2) AttemptLoop surfaces a typed :infra_error park (report infra_error_class) DISTINCT from :attempt_budget_exhausted, before consuming a retry — provider-down != work-hard; emits an attempt.infra_error ledger event carrying class+retries (digest/triage source). (3) SerialDriver maps it to a 'infra_error' gate_result and, being a committed slice outcome, it reconstructs verbatim on resume with NO double retry (Ledger dedups on idempotency_key). (4) Full-pipeline /usr/bin/zsh e2e through SerialDriver with a scripted infra output. Refactored loop/6 into handle_rework/retry_rework to stay under cyclomatic cap. Tests: adapter_base 3-tuple (exhausted infra carries class+retries; work=nil), AttemptLoop infra_error park + ledger, SerialDriver full-pipeline infra park, resume reuse (no double retry). Closes the rt6k rework-intelligence cluster.
